findings.title: PUT /api/patients/:id lets a Receptionist archive any patient via isActive, bypassing the Admin-only delete gate and the R8 dependency guard
findings.mechanism: `updatePatient` deletes only `UHID`, `patientId` and `_incrementVisit` from the body, then applies `{ $set: updateData }` verbatim via `findByIdAndUpdate`. `isActive` is a normal schema field (patientModel.js:166) and is absent from both `HARD_CLINICAL_FIELDS` and `ALLERGY_FIELDS` in the controller (patientController.js:207-219), so `classifyClinicalWrites` resolves `requiredAction = "patient.write-demographics"` — a permission held by Receptionist. Archival is therefore reachable through PUT, entirely sidestepping `deletePatient` (patientService.js:417-572), which is the only path that enforces the Admin-only `patient.delete` gate, the 409 dependency guard (active admission / non-terminal bill / open advance) and the `USER_TERMINATED` BillingAudit emission.
findings.failureScenario: Patient UH42 has an Active IPD admission and an 80,000 rupee GENERATED bill. `DELETE /api/patients/<id>` correctly 409s with PATIENT_HAS_DEPENDENCIES, and a Receptionist could not call it anyway (Admin-only). The same Receptionist instead sends `PUT /api/patients/<id>` with body `{"isActive": false}` → 200 OK. The patient disappears from `getAllPatients` and `searchPatients` (both hard-filter `{isActive: true}`), while the Admission stays `Active` on the IPD census and the bill stays open and unreconcilable from the front desk. No BillingAudit row is written, so the archival is invisible to the audit chain. Sending `{"isActive": true}` silently un-archives any previously cascade-deleted patient.
findings.fixSketch: Add `isActive` to the delete-list in `updatePatient` alongside UHID/patientId (lifecycle state must only transition through `deletePatient`). Better: replace the deny-list with an explicit allow-list of updatable demographic fields, and route any `isActive` transition through `deletePatient`/a dedicated restore endpoint so the dependency guard and audit emission always run.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four links of the claimed mechanism verified against actual code; no refutation found. 1) patientService.js:362-370 — updatePatient strips ONLY UHID, patientId, _incrementVisit, then `const updateOp = { $set: updateData }` is applied verbatim via `Patient.findByIdAndUpdate(id, updateOp, { new: true, runValidators: true })` (line 383). Deny-list, no allow-list. 2) patientModel.js:166 — `isActive: { type: Boolean, default: true }` is a normal schema field, so $set writes it and runValidators does not reject `false`. 3) patientController.js:207-219 — HARD_CLINICAL_FIELDS = {bloodGroup, dateOfBirth, age, gender}; ALLERGY_FIELDS = {knownAllergies, allergyList}. `isActive` is in neither, so classifyClinicalWrites (246-263) returns {hard:false, allergy:false} → requiredAction = "patient.write-demographics" (line 287). permissions.js:98 grants that to ["Admin","Receptionist"]. Route guard patientRoutes.js:41 is the same baseline action. Controller passes req.body unmodified to the service (line 298). 4) No sanitizer/allow-list exists: grep for mongoSanitize|sanitize|allowlist|whitelist|pickFields|isActive across app.js, server.js, routes/index.js and all middleware/*.js returned ZERO matches. Nothing strips isActive in transit. Bypassed protections are real: deletePatient (patientService.js:417-572) is the only path enforcing (a) the Admin-only gate — permissions.js:106 `"patient.delete": ["Admin"]`, route patientRoutes.js:42; (b) the 409 dependency guard at 430-455 probing Admi…

findings.title: ABHA number/address and abhaKycVerified are settable by a Receptionist via PUT /api/patients/:id, bypassing the Admin-only abdm.write gate
findings.mechanism: The ABDM identity fields `abhaNumber`, `abhaAddress`, `abhaId`, `abhaLinked`, `abhaKycVerified`, `abhaLinkedAt` are plain schema fields (patientModel.js:54-59). The sanctioned linking path is `POST /api/abdm/link-abha` → `abdmLinkService.linkPatientAbha`, gated at `requireAction("abdm.write")` (routes/Abdm/abdmAdminRoutes.js:21), which permissions.js:567 restricts to Admin. But `updatePatient`'s `$set: updateData` accepts those same fields, and none of them appear in `HARD_CLINICAL_FIELDS`/`ALLERGY_FIELDS`, so they classify as `patient.write-demographics` — Receptionist-writable. The Admin-only gate on ABHA linking is thus decorative.
findings.failureScenario: A Receptionist sends `PUT /api/patients/<id>` with `{"abhaNumber":"11-1111-1111-1111","abhaAddress":"victim@sbx","abhaId":"11-1111-1111-1111","abhaLinked":true,"abhaKycVerified":true}` → 200 OK. The patient now carries a forged, KYC-'verified' ABHA that no ABDM eKYC ever backed. `abhaId` is what the FHIR exporter stamps as the https://abdm.gov.in/abha identifier (patientModel.js:56), so this record will be published to the ABDM gateway under someone else's health ID, and `abdmController.getStatus` (abdmController.js:164) counts it in `abhaLinked: true`. No AbdmTransaction journal row is written because `linkPatientAbha` was never invoked.
findings.fixSketch: Strip all `abha*` fields in `updatePatient` (they must only be written by `abdmLinkService.linkPatientAbha`), or add them to a gated field set requiring `abdm.write`. Same strip is needed in `createPatient`, which currently passes `patientData` straight into `new Patient(...)` (patientService.js:87) with only UHID/patientId/_id removed.
findings.verifyEvidence: Attempted refutation at four layers; all four confirmed the finding. 1) MODEL (patientModel.js:54-59): abhaNumber/abhaAddress/abhaId/abhaLinked/abhaKycVerified/abhaLinkedAt are plain schema fields — no `immutable`, no findOneAndUpdate hook. Grep for pre(|immutable|findOneAndUpdate in the model returns only a pre("save") at :248, which findByIdAndUpdate bypasses by design. No schema-level defense exists. 2) SERVICE (patientService.js:362-383): updatePatient deletes ONLY UHID, patientId and _incrementVisit, then builds `const updateOp = { $set: updateData }` (:370) and calls Patient.findByIdAndUpdate(id, updateOp, {new:true, runValidators:true}) (:383). Every abha* key in the body reaches the $set. Confirmed verbatim. 3) CONTROLLER (patientController.js:265-298): passes req.body straight to patientService.updatePatient (:298) with no whitelist/strip. The classifier sets are HARD_CLINICAL_FIELDS = {bloodGroup, dateOfBirth, age, gender} (:207-212) and ALLERGY_FIELDS = {knownAllergies, allergyList} (:213-219). abha* is in NEITHER, so classifyClinicalWrites returns {hard:false, allergy:false} and the else-branch at :287 assigns requiredAction = "patient.write-demographics". roleCan check at :289 then passes for Receptionist. 4) PERMISSIONS: permissions.js:98 "patient.write-demographics": ["Admin","Receptionist"] — Receptionist qualifies. permissions.js:567 "abdm.write": ["Admin"] — and abdmAdminRoutes.js:21 `router.post("/link-abha", requireAction("abdm.write"), ctrl.linkAbha)` is…

findings.title: R8 #49 regression: totalServicesVisits is permanently 0 — the pre-set was removed for a downstream incrementer that never runs for Services
findings.mechanism: `counterSetDownstream` (patientService.js:74-79) includes `registrationType === "Services"`, and the R8-FIX(#49) comment justifies this with 'IPD/Daycare/Services → admissionService.createAdmission (now increments)'. That premise is false for Services: `REG_TO_ADM_TYPE = { IPD: "Planned", Emergency: "Emergency", Daycare: "Day Care" }` (ReceptionConsole.jsx:57) has no Services key, `NEEDS_BED` (line 55) excludes Services, and the admission block is gated by `if (visitType === "IPD" || visitType === "Daycare" || visitType === "Emergency")` (line 891). A Services registration therefore never creates an Admission, so `admissionService.createAdmission`'s `at === "Services"` branch (admissionService.js:342) is unreachable. `createPatient` also has no Services dispatch branch — only OPD (line 121) and Emergency (line 153). The fallback path is dead too: `updatePatient`'s `_incrementVisit` branch (patientService.js:367-381) has zero callers anywhere in Frontend/src.
findings.failureScenario: Receptionist registers a lab/imaging walk-in with registrationType="Services". `visitCounterField` returns "totalServicesVisits", but `counterSetDownstream` is true so the `= 1` pre-set at line 80-82 is skipped, and nothing downstream ever increments it. The patient is saved with totalServicesVisits = 0 and it stays 0 forever, across every subsequent Services visit. The reception lookup and `getPatientHistory` (which selects totalServicesVisits, line 614) report 'Services 0' for a patient with a real billed lab visit — the exact 'registered but shows 0' symptom the auto-dispatch block at lines 96-120 was written to eliminate. Same class of gap hits OPD-with-doctor whenever `OPDService.createOPDVisit` throws, since that call is wrapped in a swallowing try/catch (lines 143-152) that still returns 201.
findings.fixSketch: Remove `"Services"` from `counterSetDownstream` so the `= 1` pre-set applies at registration (Services has no admission to carry the increment). For the OPD path, move the pre-set into the catch, or increment via `updateVisitCount` only after `createOPDVisit` resolves, so a swallowed failure does not leave the counter at 0. Consider deleting the dead `_incrementVisit` branch.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM FULLY CONFIRMED — every cited line matches, and all refutation paths are closed. 1) patientService.js:74-79 — `counterSetDownstream` does include `patientData.registrationType === "Services"`, so the `= 1` pre-set at :80-82 is skipped. The R8-FIX(#49) comment at :69-73 states the premise verbatim: "IPD/Daycare/Services -> admissionService.createAdmission (now increments)". 2) That premise is FALSE for Services. ReceptionConsole.jsx:57 `const REG_TO_ADM_TYPE = { IPD: "Planned", Emergency: "Emergency", Daycare: "Day Care" }` has no Services key; :55 `NEEDS_BED = (t) => ["IPD","Emergency","Daycare"].includes(t)` excludes it; :891 the admission POST is gated `else if (visitType === "IPD" || visitType === "Daycare" || visitType === "Emergency")` and builds `admissionType: REG_TO_ADM_TYPE[visitType]` (:899) — unreachable for Services, and would be `undefined` even if reached. 3) admissionService.js:336-347 — the `(at === "Services") ? "Services"` branch (:342) exists but is DEAD: a repo-wide grep for `admissionType: "Services"` / `registrationType: "Services"` returns zero admission producers. The only match for "Services" in models is the permissive enum at admissionModel.js:188. Nothing ever constructs an Admission with admissionType "Services". 4) createPatient has no Services dispatch — only `registrationType === "OPD" && doctor` (:121) and `=== "Emergency"` (:153, now a no-op comment block after R8 #48). 5) Fallback/alternate incrementers are all dead or never invok…

findings.title: abhaAddress/abhaNumber have no uniqueness or conflict check — ABDM demographic discovery can return the wrong patient's care contexts
findings.line: 69-75 (link, no conflict check); 114 (findOne by abhaAddress); models/Patient/patientModel.js:54-55
findings.mechanism: `patientModel.js:54-55` declares `abhaNumber` and `abhaAddress` with `index: true` but NOT `unique: true` (contrast `patientId`/`UHID` at lines 7-8, which are unique+sparse). `linkPatientAbha` (abdmLinkService.js:69-75) assigns `patient.abhaAddress = abhaAddress || patient.abhaAddress` and saves, without ever checking whether that ABHA is already bound to a different UHID. `discoverForDemographics` then resolves a gateway request with `Patient.findOne({ abhaAddress: abhaAddr })` (line 114) — an unordered single-document match. With duplicates present, which patient answers the discovery is arbitrary and can flip between queries. Combined with the ABHA mass-assignment above, a non-Admin can create the duplicate.
findings.failureScenario: ABHA `ram@sbx` is linked to UH10 (correct). Later, a data-entry slip or the PUT path from finding #2 sets `abhaAddress: "ram@sbx"` on UH77 as well. Nothing rejects it. An ABDM CM discovery request for `ram@sbx` hits line 114; `findOne` returns UH77. `buildCareContextsForPatient("UH77")` runs and the gateway is handed UH77's admissions/visits as Ram's care contexts. Ram consents to the link and fetches health data belonging to a different patient — a cross-patient PHI disclosure through the national exchange, and an NHA M2/M3 certification blocker.
findings.fixSketch: Add sparse unique indexes on `abhaNumber` and `abhaAddress` (they default to `""`, so a partial index on `{ $ne: "" }` is required to avoid collapsing all unlinked patients). In `linkPatientAbha`, pre-check for an existing holder with a different `_id` and throw 409. Make `discoverForDemographics` fail closed (`matched:false`) when the lookup returns more than one candidate rather than picking arbitrarily.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four links of the claimed mechanism verified against actual code; nothing refuted. 1. NO UNIQUENESS (confirmed verbatim). patientModel.js:54-55: `abhaNumber: { type: String, default: "", trim: true, index: true }` and `abhaAddress: { type: String, default: "", trim: true, index: true }` — index only, NOT unique. Contrast lines 7-8: `patientId: { type: String, unique: true, sparse: true }`, `UHID: { type: String, unique: true, sparse: true }`. A grep across all of Backend/ found no schema.index() call or migration adding a unique/partial index on either ABHA field. The auditor's line cite is exact. 2. NO CONFLICT CHECK ON LINK (confirmed verbatim). abdmLinkService.js:69-75 assigns `patient.abhaNumber = abhaNumber || patient.abhaNumber; patient.abhaAddress = abhaAddress || patient.abhaAddress; patient.abhaId = abhaNumber || patient.abhaId; patient.abhaLinked = ...` then `await patient.save()` (line 75). No query for an existing holder of that ABHA with a different _id; no 409 path. 3. UNORDERED RESOLUTION (confirmed). abdmLinkService.js:114: `if (abhaAddr) patient = await Patient.findOne({ abhaAddress: abhaAddr }).lean();` — no sort, no count/multi-match guard. Line 124 only handles the ZERO-match case (`if (!patient) return { matched: false, ... }`); a 2-of-N match silently picks one. Line 126 feeds the winner straight into `buildCareContextsForPatient(patient.UHID)`, and lines 127-133 return that patient's care contexts to the gateway as the discovery answer. 4. MASS-ASS…

findings.title: createPatient performs no demographic duplicate detection — the same human silently gets multiple UHIDs and a split clinical record
findings.mechanism: `createPatient` validates department/doctor/TPA, strips identity fields and saves. There is no lookup for an existing patient on any demographic tuple (name + contactNumber + DOB/gender). `contactNumber` carries a plain non-unique index (patientModel.js:200) and `fullName` none, so nothing at the schema layer catches it either. The pre-save hook (patientModel.js:248-286) unconditionally mints a fresh UHID from the `uhid:global` counter for every insert. The only uniqueness in the system is on the machine-generated `patientId`/`UHID` themselves, which are new by construction — so the E11000 handler in the controller (patientController.js:37-49) can never fire for a demographic duplicate.
findings.failureScenario: Ram Kumar, 9876543210, DOB 1980-04-02, is registered on Monday → UH42. On Thursday he returns; the receptionist does not find him (misspelt search, or the `looksLikeUHID` shortcut at patientService.js:255 diverts the query) and re-registers identical demographics → UH43, created without warning. His allergyList, prior OPD visits and previous-dues now live under UH42, while the new encounter bills under UH43. The `getPreviousPendingDues` probe (billingService.js:427-432) queries by UHID, so the reception dues banner shows clean for UH43 despite unpaid balances on UH42 — billing rule 1 (surface prior pending dues) is defeated. The drug-allergy gate reading `patient.allergies` on UH43 returns [] even though UH42 records an anaphylaxis entry.
findings.fixSketch: Before `new Patient(...)`, probe for candidates on `{ contactNumber, isActive: true }` plus a normalised-name/DOB match. Return 409 with the candidate list and require an explicit `confirmDuplicate: true` (or a `mergedFrom` link) to proceed, so the receptionist consciously chooses 'same person → load existing' vs 'genuinely different'. A compound index on `{ contactNumber: 1, fullName: 1 }` makes the probe cheap.
findings.verifyEvidence: Attempted refutation on four fronts (service, controller, schema, downstream impact); all failed — code matches the finding. 1) MECHANISM CONFIRMED. patientService.js:26-88 createPatient: deletes UHID/patientId/_id (L30-32), validates department (L36-39), doctor (L40-52), TPA (L54-62), sets visit counter (L68-82), sets lastVisitDate (L85), then `const patient = new Patient(patientData); await patient.save();` (L87-88). There is NO lookup against any existing patient on name/contactNumber/DOB/gender. Grep for duplicate|dedup|existingPatient|mergedFrom|confirmDuplicate across Backend/**/*atient* returned only unrelated comments (same-day OPD-visit dedup, bill DUPLICATE watermark, counter race comments) — no demographic dedup, and no patient-merge route exists (grep of Backend/routes for merge → nothing). 2) SCHEMA CLAIM CONFIRMED. patientModel.js:200 is `PatientSchema.index({ contactNumber: 1 })` — plain, non-unique. Comment at L198-199 states only patientId and UHID get unique indexes. fullName has no unique index (only a text index, L212-225). Pre-save hook L248-286 mints a fresh UHID from `uhid:global` via nextSeqPatient whenever `!this.UHID` (L271-282); since createPatient:30 deletes any client UHID, this ALWAYS fires on insert. 3) SHARPEST SUB-CLAIM CONFIRMED — the E11000 handler at patientController.js:37-49 can never fire for a demographic duplicate. The code's own comment (L38-40) concedes it: "Patient only has unique indexes on patientId, UHID — never on contactNumber…

findings.title: getPreviousPendingDues caps at the 200 newest bills before filtering for balance, silently hiding older unpaid dues from the registration banner
findings.mechanism: The query fetches `PatientBill.find({ UHID, billStatus: { $in: ["DRAFT","GENERATED","PARTIAL"] } }).sort({ createdAt: -1 }).limit(200).lean()` and only afterwards filters in JS for `toNum(b.balanceAmount) > 0.5`. The `.limit(200)` is applied to the candidate set, not to the pending set, and the `createdAt: -1` sort means the oldest bills are the ones dropped. Because the balance filter runs post-limit, `hasPending`, `count` and `totalDue` are all computed over a truncated window.
findings.failureScenario: A chronic patient accumulates 200+ non-terminal bills over several years (each OPD visit leaves a DRAFT/GENERATED row). A 12,000 rupee unpaid bill from 2024 is the 240th newest and falls outside the limit. The receptionist loads the patient in ReceptionConsole; the previous-dues probe (ReceptionConsole.jsx:625-644) receives `hasPending: false` and the banner stays hidden. `totalDue` under-reports by 12,000 and the patient is admitted for a new visit with an undisclosed prior balance — the opposite of billing rule 1's intent.
findings.fixSketch: Push the balance filter into the Mongo query (`balanceAmount: { $gt: 0.5 }`) so the limit applies to genuinely-pending rows, and compute `count`/`totalDue` with an unlimited `$group` aggregation while capping only the returned `bills[]` array for display.
findings.verifyEvidence: Mechanism verified verbatim at Backend/services/Billing/billingService.js:429-438. The query is `PatientBill.find({ UHID, billStatus: { $in: ["DRAFT","GENERATED","PARTIAL"] } }).sort({ createdAt: -1 }).limit(200).lean()` (429-432), and the `toNum(b.balanceAmount) > 0.5` test plus the excludeVisitId/excludeAdmissionId scoping run in JS AFTER the limit (433-438). `hasPending`, `count` and `totalDue` (440-442) are therefore all computed over the truncated window, and because the sort is `createdAt: -1` the rows dropped are the OLDEST — precisely the ones most likely to hold stale unpaid dues. The finding's description of the code is exact. I attempted three refutations, all of which failed: 1. "balanceAmount is a virtual, so plain .lean() is the real bug / the filter can't work at all" — REFUTED. PatientBillModel.js:314 declares `balanceAmount: { type: Dec, default: () => toDec(0) }`, a stored Decimal128 field. Plain `.lean()` is correct here; the sibling getBillsByUHID's `.lean({virtuals:true})` (417) is needed for other reasons. No larger bug hiding underneath. 2. "The candidate set is already ~= the pending set, so the limit only truncates genuine dues and 200 is unreachable" — REFUTED, and this actually makes the finding slightly WORSE than filed. recordPayment sets `billStatus = balance === 0 ? "PAID" : "PARTIAL"` (billingService.js:1766-1768) ONLY when the prior status was not DRAFT — a fully-paid DRAFT bill deliberately STAYS `DRAFT` with balanceAmount 0. Those zero-bala…

findings.title: POST /api/opd passes req.body unfiltered into the OPD model — Receptionist can fabricate clinical fields, bypass the D9 allergy gate, and lock the doctor out of the assessment
findings.mechanism: R7hr-226 defined OPD_CLINICAL_FIELDS and strips them from the body for non-Admin callers on PUT /:visitNumber (OPDController.js:167-170). createOPDVisit (line 50) does `opdService.createOPDVisit(req.body)` with no strip at all, and OPDService.createOPDVisit (services/Patient/OPDService.js:222) does `new OPD(opdData)` — every schema-declared field is settable. The OPD schema declares provisionalDiagnosis (OPDModels.js:231), prescribedMedications (:248), assessedBy (:339), doctorSignatureImage (:351) and doctorSignedAt (:352). Two extra consequences: (a) the D9 fail-closed drug-allergy gate `_assertRxSafe` runs only in saveOPDAssessment (OPDService.js:826) and addPrescription (:978) — never on create — so prescribedMedications injected at create reach disk ungated and are dispensed by the pharmacy today-rx panel (OPDService.getTodayPrescriptionsByUHID selects prescribedMedications, :340); (b) saveOPDAssessment computes `alreadySigned` purely from doctorSignatureImage/doctorSignedAt presence (OPDService.js:685-698). POST /api/opd is gated on `reception.register` = Admin + Receptionist (routes/Patient/OPDRoutes.js:56).
findings.failureScenario: A Receptionist POSTs /api/opd with `{patientId, doctorId, chiefComplaint:'fever', provisionalDiagnosis:'Sepsis', assessedBy:'Dr. Sharma', doctorSignatureImage:'data:image/png;base64,AAA', prescribedMedications:[{medicineName:'Amoxicillin'}]}` for a patient whose Patient.allergyList contains 'Amoxicillin'. The visit is created 201 with a fabricated signed assessment; the allergy gate never runs, so the pharmacy's UHID today-rx lookup shows Amoxicillin for dispensing. When Dr. Sharma later opens the visit and POSTs /assessment, saveOPDAssessment sees alreadySigned=true and throws 409 OPD_ASSESSMENT_SIGNED — the real clinician is blocked from documenting the visit unless they supply an 'amendment reason' against a note they never wrote.
findings.fixSketch: Apply the same OPD_CLINICAL_FIELDS strip (plus doctorSignatureImage/doctorSignedAt/assessedBy/assessedAt/status) to createOPDVisit for non-Admin callers, or better: whitelist the reception-owned create payload explicitly in the controller and call _assertRxSafe if prescribedMedications survives.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited line verified against actual code; all line numbers exact. 1) MASS-ASSIGNMENT GAP IS REAL. OPDController.js:50 = `const visit = await opdService.createOPDVisit(req.body);` — no strip, no whitelist. Contrast OPDController.js:166-170 in updateOPDVisit which DOES build `payload = {...req.body}` and deletes every OPD_CLINICAL_FIELDS key (declared 11-16) when `req.user?.role !== "Admin"`. The asymmetry is exactly as described. OPDService.createOPDVisit (103-308) contains ONLY dedupe (113-122), patient lookup (125), active-IPD block (142-161), doctor-availability check (176-209), and denormalization (211-220) — no field filtering — then OPDService.js:222 `const opd = new OPD(opdData); await opd.save()`. Mongoose strict mode is no defence: all injected fields are schema-declared — OPDModels.js:231 `provisionalDiagnosis`, :248 `prescribedMedications[]`, :339 `assessedBy`, :340 `assessedAt`, :351 `doctorSignatureImage`, :352 `doctorSignedAt`. 2) RBAC REACH CONFIRMED. OPDRoutes.js:56 `router.post("/", requireAction("reception.register"), opdController.createOPDVisit)`; permissions.js:71 `"reception.register": ["Admin","Receptionist"]`. Router mounts only attachDoctorProfile (OPDRoutes.js:11) — no sanitizing middleware between route and controller. 3) D9 ALLERGY-GATE BYPASS CONFIRMED. `_assertRxSafe` is defined at OPDService.js:613 and invoked at only two sites: :826 (saveOPDAssessment, guarded by `if (Array.isArray(assessmentData.prescribedMedications))` at :820) and :978…

findings.title: Appointment check-in bills a flat ₹500 ServiceMaster OPD-CON instead of the doctor's opdFirst/opdFollowup fee — the entire R7dp/R7dq consult-fee passthrough is bypassed on the appointment path
findings.mechanism: checkIn hands OPDService.createOPDVisit a consultationFee taken solely from the request body; it never calls the first-visit-status logic (controllers/Doctor/doctorController.js:368-413 getFirstVisitStatus) nor reads Doctor.consultationFee.opdFirst/opdFollowup (models/Doctor/doctorModel.js:109-110). The receptionist UI that drives this endpoint posts an EMPTY body — Frontend/src/pages/reception/Appointments.jsx:84 `axios.post(.../appointments/${apt._id}/check-in, {})` — so consultationFee is always 0. In autoBillingService.onOPDRegistered the R8-CRIT guard treats 0 as unset (`overrideAmount = visitFee > 0 ? visitFee : undefined`, autoBillingService.js:2054-2059), so addItemToBill falls back to ServiceMaster OPD-CON.defaultPrice, seeded as a flat 500 (seeders/serviceMasterSeeder.js:1697). The Reception Console walk-in path DOES do this correctly (ReceptionConsole.jsx:492-509), so the two OPD entry doors price the same consult differently.
findings.failureScenario: Dr. Sandeep's fee sheet is opdFirst ₹800 / opdFollowup ₹200. Patient books an appointment by phone and the receptionist clicks Check-in. The OPD visit is created with consultationFee=0, and the bill's OPD-CON line reads ₹500 — the hospital loses ₹300 on every first-visit appointment, and a follow-up patient is overcharged ₹300 versus the receipt fee the doctor's sheet promises. The same patient walking in without an appointment is billed correctly, so the bill amount depends on which door they came through.
findings.fixSketch: In checkIn, resolve the fee server-side before calling createOPDVisit: reuse getFirstVisitStatus's logic (prior OPD for this patientId+doctorId → opdFollowup else opdFirst, falling back to consultationFee.opd) and pass it as consultationFee; keep req.body.consultationFee only as an explicit Admin/Receptionist override.
findings.verifyEvidence: All five cited locations verified verbatim; the mechanism holds end-to-end. 1) appointmentController.js:201 is exactly `consultationFee: req.body.consultationFee || 0`, passed into OPDService.createOPDVisit (lines 193-203). checkIn performs NO fee resolution: the only Doctor read (lines 184-188) fetches `personalInfo.fullName` for display denormalization, never `consultationFee`. getFirstVisitStatus is never called. 2) Frontend/src/pages/reception/Appointments.jsx:84 confirmed: `await axios.post(`${API_ENDPOINTS.BASE}/appointments/${apt._id}/check-in`, {})` — empty body, so req.body.consultationFee is always undefined -> 0. 3) STRONGEST REFUTATION ATTEMPT FAILED: I checked whether OPDService.createOPDVisit resolves the fee server-side as a safety net. Grep for `consultationFee|opdFirst|opdFollowup` across Backend/services/Patient/OPDService.js returns ZERO matches. The service only enriches opdData with patientVisitSeq/UHID/paymentType normalization/patient denormalization (lines 211-220) then `new OPD(opdData)` (line 222). The fee is pure passthrough — no server-side fallback exists anywhere on this path. 4) autoBillingService.js (actual path Backend/services/Billing/autoBillingService.js) lines 2054-2059 confirmed verbatim: `const visitFee = Number(opdVisit.consultationFee); const overrideAmount = Number.isFinite(visitFee) && visitFee > 0 ? visitFee : undefined;` passed as `unitPriceOverride` at line 2069. The R8-CRIT comment itself documents the mechanism: "Falls back to…

findings.title: Slot-uniqueness partial index uses $nin, which MongoDB rejects in partialFilterExpression — the index never builds, so the concurrent double-booking race the fix claims to close is still open and the E11000 handler is dead code
findings.mechanism: `AppointmentSchema.index({doctorId,appointmentDate,slotTime},{unique:true, partialFilterExpression:{status:{$nin:['Cancelled','NoShow']}}})`. MongoDB's partialFilterExpression accepts only equality, $exists:true, $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte, $type, top-level $and/$or, and $in — $nin is not supported, so createIndex fails ('Expression not supported in partial index'). Mongoose's autoIndex build failure surfaces only as an unhandled 'index' event, so boot succeeds silently and the collection has no unique constraint. Every other partial index in this codebase uses the supported $in form for exactly this field pattern (models/Billing/BillingTrigger.js:234, :208), which corroborates the operator restriction. Consequence: the pre-check findOne in appointmentController.book (:37-44) is the ONLY defence, and the E11000 catch added at :60-62 ('Two simultaneous bookings ... race past it and the partial-unique index throws E11000') can never fire.
findings.failureScenario: Two receptionists (or one double-clicking) POST /api/appointments for doctorId=D, date=2026-07-20, slotTime='10:15' within the same event-loop window. Both findOne conflict checks return null, both Appointment.create() succeed with no E11000, and two patients are told they own the 10:15 slot. getSlots (:120-121) then collapses them into a single bookedMap entry, so the front desk sees only one of the two names and the second patient's booking is invisible until they show up.
findings.fixSketch: Replace $nin with the supported $in form: `partialFilterExpression: { status: { $in: ['Booked','Confirmed','CheckedIn','Completed'] } }`, then run syncIndexes (config/db.js:94-98 already does this for other models) and assert the index exists at boot rather than swallowing the build error.
findings.verifyEvidence: Verified empirically against the project's live MongoDB, not just by reading. (1) Replaying the exact spec from appointmentModel.js:59-65 on a scratch collection fails: `NIN INDEX FAILED: 67 Error in specification {unique:true, partialFilterExpression:{status:{$nin:["Cancelled","NoShow"]}}} :: caused by :: Expression not supported in partial index: $not status $in ["Cancelled","NoShow"]`. The $in form the fixSketch proposes creates fine on the identical key. (2) db.appointments.indexes() on the live collection shows `{key:{doctorId:1,appointmentDate:1,slotTime:1}, name:"doctorId_1_appointmentDate_1_slotTime_1", background:true}` — NO `unique`, NO `partialFilterExpression`. The pre-R8 non-unique index is what is actually on disk; the "fixed" unique partial index never built. (3) Failure is silent: nothing awaits Model.init(), and config/db.js:93-98 calls syncIndexes() only for Building/Floor/Ward/Room/Beds — Appointment is not included. (4) Corroboration: $nin inside a partialFilterExpression occurs exactly once in the whole backend (this line); every other partial index uses the supported form — BillingTrigger.js:212 and :234 (`status:{$in:[...]}`), KitchenIndentModel.js:181, RoomCategoryChargesModel.js:135 (equality). (5) Consequence confirmed in code: appointmentController.book pre-checks with findOne (:37-44) then Appointment.create() (:48) — an un-serialized read-then-write with no DB backstop; the `e.code===11000` catch at :60-62, whose comment explicitly claims "the pa…

findings.title: Appointment check-in resolves a phone-only booking to an existing Patient by phone number alone, ignoring the booked name — shared family phones bind the visit, chart and bill to the wrong person
findings.mechanism: When apt.patientId is null (phone booking), checkIn does `Patient.findOne({ contactNumber: apt.patientPhone, isActive: true })` and, on a hit, assigns `apt.patientId = stub._id; apt.UHID = stub.UHID` with no comparison against apt.patientName, no age/gender check, and no disambiguation when multiple patients share the number (findOne takes an arbitrary first match). That patientId then flows straight into OPDService.createOPDVisit (:193-203), which denormalises the matched patient's name/UHID onto the visit (OPDService.js:213-220), increments THAT patient's totalOPDVisits (:226-229), creates the bridging Admission under their UHID (:252-270), and fires onOPDRegistered which bills the consult to their UHID (autoBillingService.js:2064). Sharing one mobile number across a family is the norm in the target market.
findings.failureScenario: Mother Sita (UHID H-1001, contactNumber 9876543210) is an existing patient. Her son Ravi's appointment is booked over the phone as {patientName:'Ravi', patientPhone:'9876543210'} with no patientId. At check-in the lookup returns Sita; Ravi's OPD visit is created under Sita's UHID with patientName='Sita'. Ravi's chief complaint, vitals, diagnosis and prescription are written into Sita's chart, the OPD-CON charge lands on Sita's bill, and Sita's visit counter increments. Ravi has no record at all — and Sita's medico-legal record now contains another person's PHI.
findings.fixSketch: Only auto-bind when the phone match is unambiguous AND the name matches (normalised compare against fullName), otherwise return 409 with the candidate list and require the receptionist to pick the patient or confirm a new registration.
findings.verifyEvidence: All legs verified in actual code; refutation attempts failed. (1) CITED CODE MATCHES VERBATIM — Backend/controllers/Appointment/appointmentController.js:154-156: `let stub = apt.patientPhone ? await Patient.findOne({ contactNumber: apt.patientPhone, isActive: true }) : null;` then :171-172 `apt.patientId = stub._id; apt.UHID = stub.UHID;`. There is NO comparison against apt.patientName, no age/gender check, and no multi-match disambiguation — findOne returns an arbitrary first match. (2) PHONE-ONLY BOOKING IS A SUPPORTED, INTENDED PATH — appointmentController.js:31-32 validates only patientName/patientPhone/doctorId/appointmentDate/slotTime; patientId is optional. Frontend Appointments.jsx:277 sends `patientId: patientId || undefined` with the comment "The backend tolerates missing patientId / UHID and creates a stub Patient at check-in time." lookupPatient() (Appointments.jsx:247) is an optional manual step, so a phone booking for a family member routinely arrives with patientId unset. (3) KEY REFUTATION ATTEMPTED AND FAILED — if contactNumber were unique, family sharing would be impossible and this would collapse. It is not: patientModel.js:39 `contactNumber: { type: String, required: true }` (no unique), and patientModel.js:200 `PatientSchema.index({ contactNumber: 1 })` is a NON-unique index. Multiple active Patients can legitimately share one number. (4) WRONG patientId PROPAGATES EXACTLY AS CLAIMED — OPDService.js:213-220 overwrites opdData.UHID/patientName/age/gender…

findings.title: OPD daily token counter keys on the UTC date, so tokens issued between 00:00 and 05:29 IST continue the previous day's sequence
findings.mechanism: `const dateKey = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` builds the token counter key from the UTC calendar date, then `nextSequence('opd-token:'+dateKey+':'+docKey)`. India is UTC+5:30, so 00:00-05:29 IST on day N+1 still maps to UTC day N. This is precisely the bug class autoBillingService called out and fixed with an Intl/HOSPITAL_TZ date key (autoBillingService.js:169-183 `getDateKey`); the OPD token hook was never migrated. The neighbouring visitNumber counter (:417-419) is year-keyed so it is unaffected.
findings.failureScenario: A 24×7 hospital registers OPD walk-ins overnight. Dr. A saw 86 patients on 19-Jul (IST). At 02:00 IST on 20-Jul the first patient of the new day registers; toISOString() still yields '2026-07-19', so nextSequence returns 87 and the patient's printed token reads 87 instead of 1. The queue board and serve-next ordering for 20-Jul start mid-sequence, and the counter only resets at 05:30 IST — mid-clinic on the night/early shift.
findings.fixSketch: Reuse the same HOSPITAL_TZ Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-CA') date-key helper autoBillingService already exports/uses, rather than toISOString().slice(0,10).
findings.verifyEvidence: Code at Backend/models/Patient/OPDModels.js:421-425 matches the finding verbatim: `const dateKey = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` then `nextSequence('opd-token:'+dateKey+':'+docKey)`. toISOString() is UTC-by-definition irrespective of host TZ, so the counter key rolls at 05:30 IST, not IST midnight. Backend/utils/counter.js:95-100 confirms nextSequence uses the key verbatim (`findOneAndUpdate({_id:key},{$inc:{seq:1}},{upsert:true,new:true})`) — no internal TZ normalization rescues it. Ironically counter.js already holds IST-anchored helpers (_IST_TZ :12, _IST_YEAR_FMT :13, _currentIstYear :16, fyStartYear :29) and a year-rollover audit (:58) whose /(?<!\d)(20\d{2})(?!\d)/ regex matches the YYYY-MM-DD key's year segment and passes it silently — so the existing guard neither helps nor catches this. The comparison claim checks out: Backend/services/Billing/autoBillingService.js:176-183 defines exactly `HOSPITAL_TZ = process.env.HOSPITAL_TZ || "Asia/Kolkata"` + `Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-CA",{timeZone:HOSPITAL_TZ,...})` + `getDateKey = (d=new Date()) => DATE_KEY_FMT.format(d)`; the same pattern is duplicated in utils/queryGuards.js:64-67 (istStartOfToday), utils/cronScheduler.js:92, jobs/preExpiryEmailCron.js:82. The OPD token hook is a genuine unmigrated straggler. The neighbouring visitNumber counter claim is also correct — OPDModels.js:417-419 is year-keyed (`opd-visit:${yy}`) so unaffected (it uses server-local getFullYear() rather than the IST helper in the same module…

findings.title: MLC finalize/close separation-of-duties is dead code on every non-replica-set deployment (createdById never persisted)
findings.line: 169-209 (non-tx branch 194-208 vs tx branch 176-191); consumed at Backend/controllers/MLC/mlcController.js:171 and :255
findings.mechanism: createMLC has two write paths. The transaction path (mlcService.js:176-191) sets `createdById: actor.id || actor._id || null`. The fallback non-transaction path (mlcService.js:194-208) builds an almost-identical object but silently OMITS `createdById` — it only sets `createdBy` (a display string) and `createdByRole`. The path is chosen at line 171 by `useTx = !!session && (session.client?.s?.options?.replicaSet || session.client?.options?.replicaSet)`, so any standalone/single-node Mongo (the per-hospital Docker deploy model) — and any Atlas SRV connection where `replicaSet` is not an explicit option — takes the fallback path and stores `createdById: null` (schema default, MLCReportModel.js:194). Both SoD gates are written defensively as `if (existing.createdById && String(existing.createdById) === actorId)` (mlcController.js:171, :255), so a null `createdById` makes the condition unreachable and the check no-ops rather than failing closed.
findings.failureScenario: On a single-node Mongo hospital deploy, Dr. Sharma (role=Doctor, designation=Consultant, registrationNumber set) POSTs /api/mlc for a stab-wound patient. The row is written with createdById=null. Dr. Sharma then POSTs /api/mlc/RK0001/finalize herself: the SAME_ACTOR guard at mlcController.js:171 evaluates `null && ...` → falsy → skipped; designation and MCI-reg checks pass since she is a Consultant. The MLC is Finalized with coSignedBy = the creator herself, and coSignedAt/finalizedById all point at one person. The chain-of-custody second-attestation that MLCReportModel.js:197-205 documents as mandatory never occurs — and the record looks properly co-signed in court. Identical bypass for /close.
findings.fixSketch: Add `createdById: actor.id || actor._id || null` to the non-tx MLC.create object at mlcService.js:194-208 (dedupe both paths into one buildDoc() so they cannot drift again). Separately, make the gates fail closed: reject finalize/close when `existing.createdById` is missing (`if (!existing.createdById || String(existing.createdById) === actorId) return 409`), and backfill createdById on existing rows from createdBy/audit where resolvable.
findings.verifyEvidence: All technical claims CONFIRMED against the actual code; only the severity label is overstated. 1) OMISSION IS REAL. mlcService.js:194-208 (non-tx branch) sets createdBy (line 206) + createdByRole (line 207) but omits createdById. The tx branch sets it at line 189 (`createdById: actor.id || actor._id || null`). A repo-wide Grep for `createdById` across Backend returns exactly ONE write site — mlcService.js:189. No pre-save/post hook, no backfill anywhere. MLCReportModel.js:194 declares `createdById: {type: ObjectId, ref:"User", default: null}`, so the non-tx path persists null. 2) ACTOR PLUMBING IS CORRECT, isolating the bug to the omission. mlcController.js:27-33 `actorFrom` forwards `id: req.user?._id || req.user?.id || null`. The two write paths have simply drifted. 3) NON-TX PATH IS THE SHIPPED PROD DEFAULT (the load-bearing claim — it holds). mlcService.js:171-172 gates on `session.client?.s?.options?.replicaSet || session.client?.options?.replicaSet`. Evidence it is absent by default: docker-compose.yml:18 `image: mongo:7` with no `--replSet` (standalone); docker-compose.yml:44 `MONGO_URI: mongodb://${MONGO_USER}:${MONGO_PASSWORD}@mongo:27017/${HOSPITAL_SLUG}?authSource=admin` (no replicaSet param); Backend/config/db.js:24 passes only serverSelectionTimeoutMS/heartbeatFrequencyMS, never replicaSet; Backend/.env.production.example:35 ships a standalone URI with line 34 noting replica-set is opt-in. => useTx falsy on the default per-hospital Docker deploy. 4) GATES FAIL O…

findings.title: PUT /api/mlc/:idOrMlr sets status directly — bypasses SoD, Consultant/HOD gate, and the Closed-is-terminal guard, enabling deletion of a finalized medico-legal record
findings.mechanism: Two independent controls are both bypassed by the generic PUT. (1) The finalize/close controls (SAME_ACTOR, Consultant/HOD designation, MCI reg number) live ONLY in mlcController.finalize/close (mlcController.js:159-290). mlcService.updateMLC strips identifier fields (mlrNumber/doctorId/patientId at :292-297) but happily accepts `status` — it even auto-stamps `finalizedAt` (:298) and `closedAt` (:299) — and its only guard is the MCI-reg check (:306-328), which is wrapped so a lookup miss is swallowed and applies nothing when the actor is Admin. (2) MLCReportModel.js:236 attaches the state machine via `attachStatusGuard`, but statusTransitionGuard.js:308 registers it as a `schema.pre("save")` hook only — and updateMLC:330 uses `MLC.findOneAndUpdate(...)`, which does not run document save middleware. So the registry's `MLCReport: { Closed: [] }` terminal rule (statusTransitionGuard.js:68-72) is never consulted on this path. deleteMLC's chain-of-custody rule (:343) trusts the mutable `status` field it just allowed to be rewritten.
findings.failureScenario: A Doctor with mlc.write on their own case (loadAndAuthorize passes) wants a finalized MLR for a police case to disappear. Step 1: PUT /api/mlc/RK0001 {"status":"Draft"} → findOneAndUpdate writes Closed→Draft with no save hook, no 409 from the registry, and no SoD/designation check. Step 2: DELETE /api/mlc/RK0001 → deleteMLC reads status==='Draft' (:343), the 409 never fires, and findOneAndDelete (:350) hard-deletes the record. The finalized medico-legal report is gone from the DB. Cheaper variant of the same hole: a Consultant PUTs {"status":"Finalized"} on their own Draft — finalizedAt is stamped at :298 with no second actor, no designation check, and no coSignedBy, defeating the whole R8 finalize control.
findings.fixSketch: In mlcService.updateMLC, delete `status`, `finalizedAt/By/ById`, `closedAt/By/ById/closedReason`, and `coSigned*` from the patch and force all lifecycle changes through the /finalize and /close endpoints (return 400 with a pointer if status is present). Additionally call `assertTransition('MLCReport', existing.status, patch.status, ...)` from any status-mutating path, and/or attach the guard to `pre('findOneAndUpdate')` so findOneAndUpdate is covered like the guard's own header comment (statusTransitionGuard.js:6-8) claims.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four refutation attempts failed; the mechanism is exactly as described. (1) NO findOneAndUpdate coverage. statusTransitionGuard.js:301-323 attachStatusGuard registers ONLY post("init") (:304) and schema.pre("save") (:308). Zero matches for pre("findOneAndUpdate") anywhere in the file — despite its own header comment (:6-8) claiming it covers "any controller path that mutates status via findOneAndUpdate". MLCReportModel.js:236 attaches only this guard and defines no other pre hooks. mlcService.updateMLC:330 uses MLC.findOneAndUpdate(filter, {$set: patch}, {new:true, runValidators:true}) = query middleware, so the save hook never runs and the registry rule MLCReport: { Draft:["Finalized","Closed"], Finalized:["Closed"], Closed:[] } (:68-72) is never consulted. runValidators does NOT help: "Draft" is a valid enum member (MLCReportModel.js:179-184). (2) NO alternate guard call. Grep for assertTransition across Backend: only InvestigationOrderModel.js:348 and admissionModel.js:460 import it directly for their query paths. MLC does not. The codebase itself corroborates — mrdController.js:203 comment: "Admission / MLCReport have no such guard." (3) NO body sanitization. mlcController.updateMLC:120 passes raw req.body to the service. loadAndAuthorize:102-114 checks ownership ONLY (doctorId match), never status. mlcService.updateMLC:292-297 strips mlrNumber/mlrPrefix/mlrSeq/doctorId/patientId/UHID but accepts status, and auto-stamps finalizedAt (:298) and closedAt (:299). (4) MCI…

findings.title: createMLC spreads req.body unfiltered — status, coSignedBy, finalizedById, createdById and legalHold are all mass-assignable at creation
findings.mechanism: mlcController.createMLC passes `{ ...req.body, doctorId }` straight into mlcService.createMLC, which spreads `...payload` as the first key of the MLC.create object. Only patientId/UHID/doctorId/mlrNumber/mlrPrefix/mlrSeq/patientName/age/gender/contactNumber/doctorName/createdBy/createdByRole are overridden afterwards. Every other schema field is attacker-controlled — including the entire lifecycle block (status, finalizedAt, finalizedBy, finalizedById, closedAt, closedById, closedReason at MLCReportModel.js:179-191), the chain-of-custody block (coSignedBy, coSignedByName, coSignedAt at :203-205), createdById (:194), and the MRD retention block (legalHold, legalHoldBy at :210-214). The schema-level state guard cannot help: statusTransitionGuard.js:309 returns early on `this.isNew`, so a document born Finalized is never checked. No route-level body validation exists (mlcRoutes.js:23).
findings.failureScenario: A Doctor POSTs /api/mlc with body {doctorId, patientId, mlcType:'Assault', status:'Finalized', finalizedBy:'Dr. Verma', finalizedById:'<Dr Verma _id>', coSignedBy:'<Dr Verma _id>', coSignedByName:'Dr. Verma', coSignedAt:'2026-07-17T10:00:00Z', createdById:'<some third user _id>'}. The record is created already Finalized, attributed to and apparently co-signed by Dr. Verma who never saw the patient — the finalize endpoint's SoD, Consultant/HOD and MCI-reg checks are all skipped because finalize was never called. Setting createdById to a third party additionally poisons the SoD comparison at mlcController.js:171 so the author can later 'finalize' their own record legitimately. Setting legalHold:true lets any doctor pin arbitrary records out of the MRD purge queue.
findings.fixSketch: Replace the `...payload` spread with an explicit allow-list of clinician-authorable fields (source, externalDetails, mlcType, incident*, allegedHistory, broughtBy*, police fields, generalCondition, consciousness, smellOfAlcohol, vitals, injuries, examinationFindings, investigationsAdvised, provisionalDiagnosis, opinion, disposition, emergencyId, admissionId, opdVisitId) and hard-set status:'Draft' plus null lifecycle/co-sign/legalHold fields at creation.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited mechanism verified in the real code; refutation attempts failed. 1) mlcService.js:176-191 (transaction path) and :194-208 (non-transaction path) both spread `...payload` as the FIRST key of the MLC.create object, then override only: patientId, UHID, patientName, age, gender, contactNumber, doctorId, doctorName, mlrNumber, mlrPrefix, mlrSeq, createdBy, createdByRole (+ createdById on the tx path ONLY, line 189). No other field is filtered. 2) mlcController.js:46-49 — `mlcService.createMLC({ ...req.body, doctorId }, actorFrom(req))`. req.body is passed through verbatim; no pick/allow-list anywhere between HTTP and Mongoose. 3) mlcRoutes.js:23 — `router.post("/", requireAction("mlc.write"), ctrl.createMLC)`. No body validator/schema. Only auth+RBAC. 4) MLCReportModel.js confirms all the fields the finding names are real, writable schema paths: status :179-184 (enum ["Draft","Finalized","Closed"]), finalizedAt/finalizedBy/finalizedById :185-187, closedAt/closedBy/closedById/closedReason :188-191, createdById :194, coSignedBy/coSignedByName/coSignedAt :203-205 (comment at :197-202 explicitly states these are "Enforced by mlcController.finalize and mlcController.close" — i.e. nothing enforces them at create), legalHold/legalHoldReason/legalHoldBy/legalHoldByName/legalHoldAt :210-214. 5) utils/statusTransitionGuard.js:309 — `schema.pre("save", function (next) { if (this.isNew) return next(); ... })`. Attached to MLCReportSchema at MLCReportModel.js:236. Confirms a docum…

findings.title: updateTriageCategory writes triageCategory with no enum validation — an off-enum value silently sinks the patient to the bottom of the ER queue
findings.mechanism: `Emergency.findOneAndUpdate({ emergencyNumber }, { triageCategory, triageTime: new Date() }, { new: true })` omits `runValidators: true`, so the model's `enum: ['Critical','Emergency','Urgent','Semi-urgent','Non-urgent']` (emergencyModel.js:36-40) is never applied — Mongoose only runs validators on update when explicitly asked. The controller (emergencyController.js:520-526) does not validate `req.body.triageCategory` either. Any string reaches the DB. getActiveEmergencies' `$switch` (emergencyService.js:663-672) matches only exact-cased canonical values plus colour aliases and falls through to `default: 9` (:671, commented 'unknown / unset categories sort last'), then sorts `_triagePriority: 1` (:676). Two further consequences on the same call: the update is not null-checked, and `triageCategory: undefined` is dropped by Mongoose rather than rejected.
findings.failureScenario: An ER nurse re-triages a deteriorating patient via PUT /api/emergency/ER-2026-000123/triage with {"triageCategory":"critical"} (lower-case — from a hand-rolled client, an integration, or a copy-paste). The write succeeds, the API returns 200 'Triage category updated successfully', and the NABH register faithfully records `category=critical` (nabhRegisterEmitter.js:286-288). But GET /api/emergency/active now scores that patient `_triagePriority = 9` — BELOW every Non-urgent (5) patient — so the resuscitation-priority case renders dead-last on the ER board behind every sprained ankle. The same call with a body missing triageCategory returns 200 with only triageTime bumped (silent no-op), and against a wrong emergencyNumber returns 200 with data:null (emergencyController.js:523 never null-checks) — the nurse believes the re-triage landed.
findings.fixSketch: Validate against the model enum in updateTriageCategory before writing (reject with 400 on miss), pass `{ new: true, runValidators: true }`, throw 404 when findOneAndUpdate returns null, and reject a missing/empty triageCategory. Consider mapping the $switch `default` to 1 (fail-safe-high) rather than 9 so an unknown category can never hide a sick patient.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE MECHANISM CONFIRMED, one sub-claim REFUTED, severity overstated. CONFIRMED at cited lines: 1. emergencyService.js:721-725 — `Emergency.findOneAndUpdate({ emergencyNumber }, { triageCategory, triageTime: new Date() }, { new: true })`. Verbatim as cited: no `runValidators`, no null check. 2. emergencyModel.js:36-40 — `triageCategory: { type: String, enum: ["Critical","Emergency","Urgent","Semi-urgent","Non-urgent"], required: true }`. Mongoose does not apply enum/required on findOneAndUpdate without `runValidators: true`. Grep found NO global `mongoose.set(...)` / `mongoose.plugin(...)` enabling it; conversely ~30 other call sites DO pass `{ new: true, runValidators: true }` (dietitianController.js:121, mlcController.js:218, dischargeSummaryController.js:374 with comment "runValidators added per audit C-07 — without it, the status flip..."). So the omission here is a genuine gap against the codebase's own established pattern, not a convention. 3. emergencyController.js:520-526 — destructures `req.body.triageCategory`, passes through unvalidated; never null-checks `visit`; returns 200 "Triage category updated successfully" with `data: visit`. 4. emergencyService.js:664-671,676 — `$switch` matches only exact-cased canonicals + colour aliases (Red/Orange/Yellow/Green/Blue/Deceased), `default: 9` (comment: "unknown / unset categories sort last"), then `$sort: { _triagePriority: 1, arrivalDate: 1 }`. An off-enum value therefore scores 9, below Non-urgent (5). Fail-dangerous de…

findings.title: GET /api/emergency spreads req.query straight into Emergency.find() — Mongo query-operator injection (ReDoS / filter manipulation)
findings.mechanism: Express' extended query parser (index.js:123 `express.urlencoded({ extended: true })`) turns `?foo[$regex]=x` into a nested object, and every non-page/limit key is forwarded verbatim into `Emergency.find(filters)` with no key/operator allow-list. There is no global sanitizer — index.js registers no express-mongo-sanitize (the only 'sanitize' hit in index.js is a comment at line 11), and the route (emergencyRoutes.js:21) applies only `requireAction('er.read')`. Doctor scoping is safe here (`Object.assign(filters, scope)` at emergencyController.js:116 overwrites any attacker `$or` and the rest is ANDed), so this is not a scope-escape — the exposure is operator injection into the query itself.
findings.failureScenario: A Receptionist (er.read is granted to Admin/Doctor/Nurse/Receptionist per permissions.js:406) calls GET /api/emergency?presentingComplaints[$regex]=(a%2B)%2B%24 — the unescaped nested-quantifier regex is evaluated by Mongo against every ER document's complaint text, pinning the mongod CPU and stalling the ER board for all users (catastrophic backtracking). Note mlcService.listMLC:260 already escapes exactly this class of input, so the ER list is the inconsistent surface. Cheaper variants: `?isMLC[$ne]=true` or `?status[$exists]=true` to reshape the register listing outside the intended filter set.
findings.fixSketch: Pick the ER list filters off req.query explicitly (status, triageCategory, disposition, isMLC, UHID, date range) and coerce each to a scalar; reject any value that is an object/array. Escape any free-text search with the same `replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')` idiom used in mlcService.js:260, or add express-mongo-sanitize globally in index.js.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM REFUTED. The finding attributes nested-object query parsing to index.js:123 `express.urlencoded({extended:true})`, but urlencoded governs BODY parsing, not req.query. req.query is governed by the `query parser` app setting. Backend/package.json:20 pins `"express": "^5.1.0"`, and Express 5 changed the default query parser from `extended` to `simple`. Verified at runtime: `app.get('query parser')` === "simple"; no `app.set("query parser", ...)` override exists anywhere (grep clean). ALL THREE POCs ARE INERT. Driving a real Express 5 app with index.js's exact middleware plus the controller's exact destructure (emergencyController.js:110 `const { page=1, limit=10, ...filters } = req.query`):   ?presentingComplaints[$regex]=(a%2B)%2B%24 -> {"presentingComplaints[$regex]":"(a+)+$"}   ?isMLC[$ne]=true                           -> {"isMLC[$ne]":"true"}   ?status[$exists]=true                      -> {"status[$exists]":"true"} These are literal flat STRING keys, not nested operator objects. `presentingComplaints[$regex]` is a non-existent field path -> Emergency.find (emergencyService.js:104) matches zero docs and returns an empty page. No regex is ever compiled, so the claimed catastrophic-backtracking ReDoS ("pinning mongod CPU, stalling the ER board") is impossible. The $ne/$exists "filter reshaping" variants likewise reshape nothing. The mlcService.js:260 "inconsistent surface" argument also fails: that escapes a free-text regex the code itself constructs, whereas this…

findings.title: ER→IPD bridge accepts a caller-supplied admissionId with no existence or patient-ownership check
findings.line: 352-353 (`if (dispositionData.admissionId) { visit.admission = dispositionData.admissionId; }`) inside updateDisposition's Admitted branch
findings.mechanism: In the Admitted branch, when the caller supplies `admissionId` the service assigns it directly to `visit.admission` with no lookup — it never verifies the Admission exists, that its `patientId`/`UHID` matches `visit.patientId`/`visit.UHID`, or that its status is Active. Every other field in this branch is derived from the visit or from a real Bed CAS claim (:376-381), so this is the one unchecked cross-document link. Because the id is truthy, the entire stub-creation + bed-claim + billing-rebind block (:354-485) is skipped, and the value flows onward into the NABH register as `admissionLinkId` (emergencyController.js:413 → nabhRegisterEmitter.js:330), which then locks the row (:333-334).
findings.failureScenario: An Admin/Doctor (ipd.discharge per emergencyRoutes.js:75) dispositions ER-2026-000123 (patient A, an MLC assault case) with {disposition:'Admitted', admittedToBed:'B-12', admittedToWard:'ICU', admissionId:'<patient B's active admission _id>'} — a realistic copy-paste from an adjacent tab. The ER visit for patient A is now permanently linked to patient B's admission; the Emergency Register row is written with admissionLinkId pointing at B and immediately locked (nabhRegisterEmitter.js:333), so the NABH AAC.1 register asserts A was admitted under B's IPD episode. Patient A gets no bed claim, no admission stub and no billing bootstrap, and the disposition is sticky-terminal (emergencyService.js:321-329) so it cannot be corrected without an Admin re-open. A garbage/nonexistent ObjectId produces the same silent dangling ref.
findings.fixSketch: Before assigning, load the Admission and assert it exists, is not Discharged/Cancelled, and that String(adm.patientId) === String(visit.patientId) (or adm.UHID === visit.UHID); throw a 400 otherwise. Same check belongs on the emergencyController.updateDisposition entry so the register never sees an unvalidated admissionLinkId.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited link verified in actual code; refutation attempts failed. 1) emergencyService.js:352-353 is exactly as claimed: `if (dispositionData.admissionId) { visit.admission = dispositionData.admissionId; } else if (!visit.admission) {`. No Admission lookup, no patientId/UHID ownership assertion, no status check. The `else if` confirms the structural claim: a truthy caller-supplied admissionId SKIPS the whole block at :354-485 — the IPD counter/stub Admission.create (:382-422), the Bed CAS claim `Bed.findOneAndUpdate({bedNumber: bed, status:"Available"}, ...)` (:377-381), the PatientBill/BillingTrigger rebind (:443-459), and `autoBilling.onAdmissionCreated(stub)` (:470-476). So the patient gets no bed occupancy, no admission stub, no billing bootstrap. 2) Untrusted input reaches it: emergencyController.js:389-400 `updateDisposition` passes `req.body` through verbatim (only injects `body.actor`). The R8-FIX(#21) field-scrub at emergencyController.js:194-204 strips disposition/deathDetails/damaDetails/referralDetails only on the GENERIC update route, not this endpoint, and never covered `admissionId`. 3) Persistence confirmed: emergencyModel.js:238-241 `admission: { type: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: "Admission" }` — no validator/hook; emergencyService.js:636 `await visit.save()` commits it. 4) Register consequence confirmed: emergencyController.js:413 `admissionLinkId: visit.admission || visit.admissionId` → nabhRegisterEmitter.js is at services/Compliance/ (finding…

findings.title: updateAdmission mass-assignment lets Doctor/Receptionist clear mustCosign, legalHold and the initial-assessment gate
findings.mechanism: updateAdmission builds `safe = {...data}` and deletes only status, admissionNumber, patientId, bedId, attendingDoctorId, treatmentTeam and dischargeWorkflow, then `$set`s everything else with runValidators. `mustCosign` (admissionModel.js:255), `legalHold`/`legalHoldReason`/`legalHoldBy`/`legalHoldAt` (admissionModel.js:172-176) and `initialAssessment.*` (admissionModel.js:398-401) are all real schema paths and are NOT stripped. PUT /api/admissions/:id is gated only on `ipd.assign-bed` = [Admin, Receptionist, Doctor] (permissions.js:118) with no care-team/ownership check in the controller. This lets that route write three fields whose dedicated endpoints are gated far more narrowly: legalHold is supposed to be write-only via POST /api/mrd/legal-hold on `compliance.legal-hold.write` = [Admin, MRD] (permissions.js:617), and initialAssessment via `mar.write` (admissionRoutes.js:114-115).
findings.failureScenario: A Junior Resident (role=Doctor) is the attending on ADM IPD-26-07, so createAdmission auto-set mustCosign=true (admissionService.js:234-254). They send `PUT /api/admissions/<id> {"mustCosign": false}` — accepted, because ipd.assign-bed includes Doctor and mustCosign is not stripped. They then POST /api/discharge-summary/<id>/finalize. dischargeSummaryController.js:585 evaluates `isJR && admission.mustCosign === true` → false, so the REQUIRE_SENIOR_COSIGN 409 never fires and the JR self-finalizes the discharge without senior co-sign. Same route: a Receptionist sends `{"legalHold": false}` on an admission MRD placed under litigation hold, and retentionEnforcer.js:158 (`filter[legalHoldField] = {$ne: true}`) puts the record back on the purge-candidate queue — bypassing the Admin/MRD-only hold RBAC. Or `{"initialAssessment": {"doctorCompleted": true, "nurseCompleted": true}}` clears the NABH COP.2 gate without any mar.write permission.
findings.fixSketch: Replace the denylist with an allowlist of patchable fields in updateAdmission (reasonForAdmission, provisionalDiagnosis, specialInstructions, expectedDischargeDate, expectedStayDays, estimatedCost, department/departmentId, contactNumber, email…). At minimum add `delete safe.mustCosign; delete safe.legalHold; delete safe.legalHoldReason; delete safe.legalHoldBy; delete safe.legalHoldByName; delete safe.legalHoldAt; delete safe.initialAssessment; delete safe.UHID; delete safe.hasBed;`.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link verified in actual code; attempted refutation failed. 1. admissionService.js:1557-1579 — denylist confirmed verbatim: `const safe = {...data}` then deletes ONLY status, admissionNumber, patientId, bedId, attendingDoctorId, treatmentTeam, dischargeWorkflow, then `Admission.findByIdAndUpdate(id, {$set: safe}, {new:true, runValidators:true})`. mustCosign / legalHold* / initialAssessment are NOT stripped. 2. admissionController.js:258-262 — `updateAdmission = handle(async (req,res) => AdmissionService.updateAdmission(req.params.id, req.body))`. Raw req.body, no allowlist, no ownership/care-team check. 3. admissionRoutes.js:97 — `router.put("/:id", idGuard, authenticate, requireAction("ipd.assign-bed"), ctrl.updateAdmission)`. idGuard = validateObjectIdParam("id") (routes line 25) — param validation only, NOT a body sanitizer. No mass-assignment middleware on the path. 4. permissions.js:118 "ipd.assign-bed": [Admin, Receptionist, Doctor]; :134 "mar.write": [Admin, Nurse]; :617 "compliance.legal-hold.write": [Admin, MRD]. Privilege asymmetry exactly as claimed. 5. Schema paths are all TOP-LEVEL on admissionModel.js, so strict mode does not drop them and $set writes them: legalHold/legalHoldReason/legalHoldBy/legalHoldByName/legalHoldAt at 172-176; mustCosign at 255 ("set at admission time... discharge summary finalize reads it"); initialAssessment{doctorCompleted,nurseCompleted,...} at 398-404. All plain Booleans → runValidators raises nothing on {"mustCosign": false}.…

findings.title: cancelAdmission / deleteAdmission DoctorOrder cascade queries visitId with an ObjectId that is never stored there
findings.mechanism: Both paths query `DoctorOrder.find/exists({ visitId: String(admission._id) })`. But DoctorOrder.visitId is a plain String (DoctorOrderModel.js:192) and for IPD orders it holds the admissionNUMBER, not the admission ObjectId: the server-side normaliser sets `body.visitId = adm.admissionNumber` (doctorOrderRoutes.js:463) and the frontend sends `visitId: visitId || ipdNo` / `visitId: ipdNo` (DoctorOrdersPanel.jsx:1543,1669; DoctorAssessmentPage.jsx:367,400) — i.e. the 'IPD-26-01' string. The hex ObjectId string never equals the admission number, so both queries match zero documents. Notably the R8-FIX(#22) OT-booking gate in the SAME file (admissionService.js:381-391) got this right — it scopes on `admissionId` / `ipdNo` / `admissionNumber` — so the correct linkage fields exist and are populated; only the cancel/delete cascade still uses the stale visitId key.
findings.failureScenario: Admission IPD-26-01 has 6 Active DoctorOrders (each stored with visitId='IPD-26-01', admissionId=<oid>). Admin calls POST /api/admissions/<id>/cancel. cancelAdmission line 803 runs `DoctorOrder.find({visitId: '68f3c1...a9', status: {$in:[Pending,Acknowledged,Active,InProgress]}})` → 0 hits, cascadeSummary.ordersCancelled stays 0, admission flips to Cancelled and all 6 orders remain Active in the nursing queue — the exact phantom-alert failure the R7bd cascade was written to eliminate. Same key in deleteAdmission line 1792: the 'active doctor order' blocker never fires, so DELETE /api/admissions/<id> without force=true soft-deletes an admission that still has live orders instead of returning the intended 409 ADMISSION_HAS_DEPENDENCIES.
findings.fixSketch: Reuse the admScope pattern from the OT gate: build `const scope = [{admissionId: admission._id}]; if (admission.admissionNumber) scope.push({ipdNo: admission.admissionNumber}, {admissionNumber: admission.admissionNumber}, {visitId: admission.admissionNumber});` and query `{$or: scope, status: {...}}` in both cancelAdmission and deleteAdmission.
findings.verifyEvidence: All claims verified against actual code; attempts to refute failed. 1. CITED CODE IS AS CLAIMED. admissionService.js:803-806 `DoctorOrder.find({ visitId: String(admission._id), status: {$in:["Pending","Acknowledged","Active","InProgress"]} })`; admissionService.js:1792-1795 `DoctorOrder.exists({ visitId: String(admission._id), status: {$in:[...]} })`. Both key on the stringified admission ObjectId. 2. FIELD TYPE CONFIRMED. DoctorOrderModel.js:192 `visitId: String` (plain string, no ref/cast). Separate correct linkage fields exist: :208 `admissionId` (ObjectId, ref Admission, indexed), :209 `ipdNo` (String, indexed), :210 `admissionNumber` (String, indexed). 3. WHAT visitId ACTUALLY HOLDS = admissionNumber, never the ObjectId. Four independent confirmations: (a) server normaliser doctorOrderRoutes.js:463 `if (!body.visitId) body.visitId = adm.admissionNumber || null;` and :862 same on update path; (b) frontend senders DoctorOrdersPanel.jsx:1543,1669 `visitId: visitId || ipdNo` and DoctorAssessmentPage.jsx:367,400 `visitId: ipdNo`; (c) the SOLE panel caller DoctorNotesPage.jsx:1606 passes `visitId={patient?.ipdNo || patient?.admissionNumber || patient?.visitId}` — no ObjectId can reach the prop; (d) decisively, the codebase's own backfill script backfillClinicalLinkage.js:13 comments "try matching by visitId === admissionNumber" and :53 implements `Admission.findOne({ admissionNumber: visitId })`. 4. KEY SPACES PROVABLY DISJOINT. AdmissionModel.js:378 documents admissionNumber…

findings.title: The wired bed-transfer workflow (completeHandover) never re-stamps bed tariff or package tier — patient keeps paying the old ward rate
findings.mechanism: There are two bed-move implementations. admissionService.transferBed (admissionService.js:943-1159) does the move AND the two billing follow-ups: the R7bd-A-9 future-day bed-item re-stamp (1091-1123) and the R7bd-A-17 package tierUsed/unitPrice sync (1131-1156). completeHandover — the endpoint the shipped UI actually calls (Frontend/src/config/api.js:106 BED_TRANSFERS, App.jsx:630 /bed-transfers) — duplicates only the bed/admission mutation (lines 210-281) and has neither billing step. It writes admission.bedId/bedNumber/wardId/roomId/floorId and stops.
findings.failureScenario: Patient in a General Ward bed (₹2,000/day, package tierUsed='generalWard') deteriorates and is moved to ICU via the UI: POST /api/bed-transfers then PUT /api/bed-transfers/:id/handover. The admission now points at the ICU bed, but no bed-day item is re-stamped and admission.package.tierUsed stays 'generalWard' with the general-ward unitPrice. Every subsequent runDailyBedChargeAccrual tick reads the stale package tier and keeps billing ₹2,000/day for an ICU bed for the rest of the stay. The identical transfer done via POST /api/admissions/:id/transfer (admissionService.transferBed) re-tariffs correctly — so the bill depends on which endpoint the operator happened to use.
findings.fixSketch: Make completeHandover delegate the bed/admission mutation and the billing follow-ups to admissionService.transferBed(transfer.admissionId, transfer.toBedId, transfer.reason) after the SoD check, or extract the re-stamp + package-tier-sync blocks into a shared `_postTransferRetariff(admission)` helper and call it from both paths.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE MECHANISM CONFIRMED, BLAST RADIUS OVERSTATED. Confirmed at the cited lines: 1. bedTransferController.js:210-304 — completeHandover mutates bed + admission only (bedId/bedNumber/wardName/roomId/wardId/floorId at 237-242), releases old bed (275-281), flips transfer to Complete (287-297). No bed-item re-stamp, no package tier sync. Exactly as filed. 2. The re-stamp (1091-1123) and package tierUsed/unitPrice sync (1125-1156) exist ONLY in transferBed. NOTE: real path is Backend/services/Patient/admissionService.js, not the cited Backend/services/admissionService.js (which does not exist) — line numbers match exactly, so this is a path typo, not a wrong citation. 3. Wiring claim verified: NursePatientPanel.jsx:2043 PUTs /bed-transfers/:id/handover; DoctorPatientPanel.jsx:2024 POSTs /bed-transfers; BedVisualLayout.jsx:3867 POSTs /bed-transfers. Frontend/src/Services/admissionService.js:222 transferBed (POST /admissions/:id/transfer) is DEFINED BUT HAS ZERO CALLERS — grep across Frontend/src returns only the definition. So the shipped UI does exclusively use the un-retariffed path. Confirmed. 4. Package billing impact real: autoBillingService.js:2826-2834 — `const pkg = admission.package; ... const pkgRate = toNum(pkg.unitPrice || 0) * prorateMultiplier;` reads the stale unitPrice off the admission doc on every tick; 2849 stamps `(tier=${pkg.tierUsed})` into orderDetails. A PER_DAY package with tierUsed='generalWard' survives an ICU move via completeHandover and keeps billing…

findings.title: transferBed zeroes today's Room/Nursing bill items but the daily dedup blocks re-accrual, permanently losing the charge
findings.mechanism: The re-stamp loop selects bill items where `BED_PREFIXES.test(code) || cat === 'Room' || cat === 'Nursing'` and `chargeDate >= today`, sets `item.quantity = 0`, and relies on the comment's promise that 'the runDailyBedChargeAccrual cron / next flushDailyChargesForAdmission run picks up the new tier'. It does not. createTrigger's daily dedup is keyed on `(admissionId, serviceCode, dateKey)` against BillingTrigger, not against the bill item (autoBillingService.js:773-805): the trigger for today's serviceCode already exists in status completed/billed, so the re-attempt returns `{skipped: true, reason: 'Daily dedup — already charged today'}` and no replacement item is ever created. Zeroing the item does not delete or reopen the trigger.
findings.failureScenario: Patient is moved bed-to-bed within the same General Ward at 14:00. Today's bed-day item (serviceCode BED-GEN) and nursing item (IPD-NUR-DAILY) both have chargeDate = today, so both get quantity=0. The new bed is the same room category, so the next accrual computes the same serviceCode BED-GEN for the same dateKey → dedup hit → skipped. Today's bed AND nursing charges are billed at zero and never re-fire. The nursing item leaks on EVERY transfer regardless of category, since IPD-NUR-* does not vary with the bed.
findings.fixSketch: Do not zero the item blind. Either (a) void the matching BillingTrigger for (admissionId, serviceCode, dateKey) alongside zeroing the item so the re-accrual is allowed to fire, or (b) recompute the new tier price inline and re-price item.unitPrice in place instead of setting quantity=0, or (c) restrict the re-stamp to `chargeDate > today` and let only genuinely future days re-accrue.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE DEFECT CONFIRMED — today's bed-day charge is zeroed on transfer and never re-accrues. Two sub-claims in the finding are wrong; the net severity stays medium. VERIFIED CHAIN: 1. admissionService.js:1091-1123 — re-stamp block is verbatim as cited. `BED_PREFIXES = /^(BED-|IPD-RM-|IPD-ICU-|IPD-NUR-)/` (:1094), `if (!itemDate || itemDate < today) continue;` (:1107), `item.quantity = 0;` (:1111), `await bill.save();` (:1116). It never touches BillingTrigger. 2. The zeroing DOES destroy money (finding assumed this without proof; it holds). PatientBillModel.js:555-569 recalcTotals recomputes per item: `const qty = toNum(item.quantity); const gAmt = unit * qty; ... item.grossAmount = toDec(gAmt); item.netAmount = toDec(nAmt);` and the pre-save hook calls `this.recalcTotals()` (:785). So qty=0 → grossAmount/netAmount = 0 on save. 3. Dedup blocker is real. autoBillingService.js (actual path Backend/services/**Billing**/autoBillingService.js — finding cited the wrong path) :767-805: `dedupQuery = { admissionId, serviceCode, dateKey, status: { $in: ["completed","billed","pending","pending-review"] } }` → `return { skipped: true, reason: "Daily dedup — already charged today", existing }`. The trigger is flipped to `status:"billed"` at :940-948 right after auto-billing, so it sits squarely in the dedup status set. Zeroing the item does not void or reopen it. CORRECTION 1 — dedup is NOT the primary blocker; the loss is BROADER than claimed. flushDailyChargesForAdmission emits `serviceC…

findings.title: Dirty-bed (CleaningPending) guard is enforced on admit but absent from every bed-transfer path
findings.mechanism: R7hr-241 added the IPC.6 turnover guard to the admit path — createAdmission reserves the bed with `{_id, status:'Available', 'housekeeping.state': {$nin: ['CleaningPending','CleaningInProgress']}}` (admissionService.js:154) — and bedService.getAvailableBeds applies the same $nin so dirty beds are hidden from the picker (bedService.js:420-423). None of the transfer paths carry that predicate: admissionService.transferBed matches on `{_id: newBedId, status: 'Available'}` only (lines 962, 1017), bedTransferController.createTransfer matches `{_id: toBedId, status: 'Available'}` (lines 60, 74-75), and completeHandover matches `{_id: transfer.toBedId, status: {$in: ['Reserved','Available']}}` (line 211). A just-discharged bed is left at status 'Available' + housekeeping.state 'CleaningPending' (admissionService.js:486-501, admissionController.js:1429-1445), so it is Available-but-dirty and every transfer path will take it.
findings.failureScenario: Bed G-12 is vacated by an MRSA-isolation patient at 10:00; discharge sets status='Available', housekeeping.state='CleaningPending' and spawns an urgent terminal-clean CleaningTask. At 10:05, before housekeeping arrives, a nurse transfers another patient into G-12 via POST /api/bed-transfers (or POST /api/admissions/:id/transfer). The status:'Available' predicate passes, the bed flips to Reserved/Occupied while housekeeping.state is still 'CleaningPending', and a patient is placed in an uncleaned isolation bed. The open terminal-clean task stays in the queue pointing at a now-occupied bed. The same move through the admit endpoint would have been correctly refused.
findings.fixSketch: Add `'housekeeping.state': {$nin: ['CleaningPending','CleaningInProgress']}` to the bed predicate in admissionService.transferBed (both tx and no-tx branches), bedTransferController.createTransfer's reserve CAS, and completeHandover's occupy CAS — mirroring admissionService.js:154. Return a distinct 409 code (e.g. BED_NOT_CLEANED) so the UI can explain the refusal.
findings.verifyEvidence: All cited code verified verbatim; attempted refutation failed on every axis. 1) GUARD PRESENT ON ADMIT: admissionService.js:154 — `{ _id: data.bedId, status: "Available", "housekeeping.state": { $nin: ["CleaningPending","CleaningInProgress"] } }` (comment cites R7hr-241 / IPC.6). bedService.js:420-423 applies the same $nin in getAvailableBeds (R7hr-197). 2) GUARD ABSENT ON ALL TRANSFER PATHS (exact text read): - admissionService.js:962 (tx branch): `{ _id: newBedId, status: "Available" }` - admissionService.js:1017 (no-tx branch): `{ _id: newBedId, status: "Available" }` - bedTransferController.js:60 pre-check: `Bed.findOne({ _id: toBedId, status: "Available" })` - bedTransferController.js:74-75 reserve CAS: `{ _id: toBedId, status: "Available" }` -> `$set:{status:"Reserved"}` - bedTransferController.js:210-211 handover occupy CAS: `{ _id: transfer.toBedId, status: { $in: ["Reserved","Available"] } }` -> `$set:{status:"Occupied"}` None carry the housekeeping predicate. 3) DIRTY-AVAILABLE STATE IS REAL: admissionService.js:486-501 sets `status:"Available"` AND `"housekeeping.state":"CleaningPending"` in one $set on discharge; admissionController.js:1429-1445 (gate-pass discharge, R7bd-A-11) does the same, and admissionController.js:1458-1464 auto-creates the CleaningTask. So a just-vacated bed is genuinely Available-but-dirty. 4) NO COMPENSATING GUARD: repo-wide grep for `housekeeping` shows the only $nin guards are admissionService.js:154 and bedService.js:422. utils/statusT…

findings.title: legalHold on an Admission is honoured only by the retention sweep — cancel and soft-delete ignore it entirely
findings.mechanism: Admission.legalHold (admissionModel.js:172) is written by the Admin/MRD-only POST /api/mrd/legal-hold (mrdController.js:168-215) and read in exactly one place: retentionEnforcer.js:158 excludes held records from the purge-candidate count.
