Neither deleteAdmission nor cancelAdmission reads it. deleteAdmission's only pre-flight is the !force blocker probe for open bills/orders/MARs (lines 1783-1811); with force=true it runs the full cancel cascade and flips status='Deleted' with no legal-hold consideration. cancelAdmission's only guard is the payments check (lines 751-766).
findings.failureScenario: MRD places admission IPD-26-04 under legal hold for an open MLC/litigation (legalHold=true, legalHoldReason='Court order XYZ'). An Admin later calls DELETE /api/admissions/<id>?force=true. deleteAdmission never inspects legalHold, so it cascades: releases the bed, cancels the DoctorOrders, flips DRAFT/GENERATED bills to CANCELLED (lines 848-880), voids pending BillingTriggers, then sets status='Deleted'. The record under court-ordered preservation is now excluded from every list view and its financial artefacts are voided — the custody guarantee the legal-hold flag exists to provide is silently defeated by a role (ipd.delete = [Admin]) that does not hold compliance.legal-hold.write.
findings.fixSketch: At the top of deleteAdmission and cancelAdmission, after loading the admission, add: `if (admission.legalHold) { const err = new Error(`Admission is under legal hold (${admission.legalHoldReason || 'no reason recorded'}) — clear the hold via POST /api/mrd/legal-hold before cancelling/deleting.`); err.status = 423; err.code = 'ADMISSION_UNDER_LEGAL_HOLD'; throw err; }`. Consider the same guard on the reactivate path's DischargeSummary un-finalize (admissionController.js:1735).
findings.verifyEvidence: CODE CLAIM CONFIRMED: Admission.legalHold (admissionModel.js:172-176) is written only by mrdController.js:168-238 (setLegalHold) and read in exactly one place backend-wide — retentionEnforcer.js:158 (`if (t.legalHoldField) filter[t.legalHoldField] = { $ne: true }`). deleteAdmission (admissionService.js:1774-1837) pre-flights only the !force blocker probe (1783-1811); cancelAdmission (726-941) guards only status (729-733) and payments (751-766). Neither reads legalHold. That much is accurate. IMPACT REFUTED ON THREE COUNTS: (1) legalHold is NOT a destruction interlock — nothing is ever purged. retentionEnforcer.js:19-21 states "This service implements step 2 — it scans + summarises but DOES NOT mutate documents", and 15-17: "An admin reviews the summary + decides what to archive — no auto-purge (NABH explicitly disallows automatic destruction of clinical records without legal-team sign-off)." The flag's ONLY implemented semantic is excluding a row from an advisory candidate COUNT in a CRON_RECONCILED BillingAudit summary. No code path destroys an Admission. The finding's premise of a "custody guarantee" the flag provides does not exist in code. (2) The hold survives the delete fully intact. deleteAdmission is a SOFT delete: lines 1830-1835 set status="Deleted" + deletedAt/deletedById/deletedByName/deleteReason; comment 1772-1773 — "The row is never physically removed — it remains for audit reconstruction." legalHold=true is never cleared, and the retention filter (155-158) ke…

findings.dimension: Billing core (bills, advances, payments, refunds, credit notes)
findings.title: PaymentSchema has no receivedById/receivedByRole/voidedById — Mongoose strict mode silently drops them, killing the refund segregation-of-duties gate and per-cashier shift reconciliation
findings.mechanism: PaymentSchema declares only receiptNumber, amount, paymentMode, transactionId, paidAt, receivedBy, remarks, voidedAt/voidedBy/voidedByRole/voidReason and the tds* fields. It does NOT declare `receivedById`, `receivedByRole`, or `voidedById`. Every writer pushes them anyway (recordPayment 1747, bulkCollect 1164, voidPayment 1887-1890, recordRefund 2103-2106) and Mongoose's default strict mode discards unknown subdocument paths on save — so `payments[].receivedById` is ALWAYS absent on disk. Two controls read it back: (a) recordRefund's SoD probe at billingService.js:1988-1996 selects `payments.receivedById` and refuses SAME_ACTOR only if it matches refundedById — with the field always undefined, `p.receivedById && ...` is always false, so the check never fires; (b) cashierSessionController.js:121-124/160-163 filters bills by `"payments.receivedById": session.cashierId`, which matches zero documents. Backend/scripts/backfillReceivedById.js:21 states its own precondition — "Run only after R7bb-FIX-B-1 has shipped (i.e. PaymentSchema has the field)" — which never landed; the script itself would also no-op because its `p.receivedById = candidate._id` assignment is dropped at save too. Note voidedByRole IS declared while voidedById is not, showing the field set was added only partially.
findings.failureScenario: Cashier A (role Accountant, has billing.refund) records a ₹40,000 CASH payment on bill BILL-26-14 — the receivedById she is authenticated as is dropped at save. Cashier A then POSTs /api/billing/BILL_ID/refund {amount: 40000, reason: "patient overpaid", mode: "CASH"} with no approverOverride. The SoD probe reads payments[].receivedById as undefined for every row, `sameActor` is false, and the refund proceeds — the same person collects and returns ₹40,000 cash with no second signature, which is exactly the control D3-CRIT-1 claims to enforce. Separately, when she closes her shift, `PatientBill.countDocuments({"payments.receivedById": cashierId, ...})` returns 0 and the close-window `find()` returns no bills, so cashCollected computes to 0 (plus advance deposits only) and the drawer variance report shows the entire shift's ₹40,000 of bill collections as unaccounted/invisible.
findings.fixSketch: Add to PaymentSchema: `receivedById: { type: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: "User", default: null }`, `receivedByRole: { type: String, trim: true, default: null }`, `voidedById: { type: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: "User", default: null }` (mirroring PatientAdvanceModel.js:71-72). Add an index on `payments.receivedById` for the shift queries. Then run scripts/backfillReceivedById.js --dry-run. Until backfilled, the refund SoD probe should fail-closed (refuse when receivedById is missing on positive rows) rather than fail-open.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every claim verified; all refutation attempts failed. 1) FIELD ABSENCE CONFIRMED. Grep for `receivedById|receivedByRole|voidedById` across Backend/models/PatientBillModel/PatientBillModel.js returns ZERO hits. PaymentSchema (lines 170-211) declares only: receiptNumber, amount, paymentMode, transactionId, paidAt, receivedBy, remarks, voidedAt(198), voidedBy(199), voidedByRole(200), voidReason(201), tdsAmount/tdsCertificateNo/tdsSection. The auditor's observation that voidedByRole IS declared while voidedById is NOT is accurate, confirming a partially-applied field set. 2) STRICT MODE APPLIES (no rescue). No `mongoose.set('strict',...)` anywhere in Backend. The only `strict:false` occurrences are unrelated scripts: scripts/e2eFullSystemTest.js:631 and scripts/dedupeInitialAssessments.js:175. PatientBillModel.js:369 = `payments: [PaymentSchema]`, so the pushed-but-undeclared paths at billingService.js:1747-1748, 1887-1890, 2103 are silently discarded on save. 3) REFUND SoD GATE IS DEAD AND FAIL-OPEN. billingService.js:1988-1996 selects `payments.receivedById` and evaluates `return p.receivedById && String(p.receivedById) === String(refundedById);` — left operand always undefined -> sameActor always false -> the 409 SAME_ACTOR at 1997-2002 never throws. I searched for a backstop and found NONE: billingController.js:1699-1723 (refundPayment) only Admin-gates `approverOverride` (1710-1711) and forwards refundedById (1717); the service probe is the sole SoD check. 4) DECISIVE CORRO…

findings.dimension: Billing core (bills, advances, payments, refunds, credit notes)
findings.title: PENDING_APPROVAL and REJECTED credit notes still reverse GST in both the GST register and the GSTR-1 CDNR export — the maker-checker gate is cosmetic
findings.mechanism: CreditNote.js:58-71 introduces `status: [APPROVED|PENDING_APPROVAL|REJECTED]` and documents the control as "the CN won't appear in the GST register until a different Accountant/Admin approves"; billingService.recordRefund:2289-2320 sets status=PENDING_APPROVAL whenever `amt > 10000 || taxShare > 0`. But both consumers query only on date: `_bucketCreditNotes` does `CreditNote.find({ creditNoteDate: { $gte: periodStart, $lt: periodEnd } })` with no status predicate, and the register's cnAgg does `$match: { creditNoteDate: { $gte: from, $lte: to } }` likewise. So an unapproved — or explicitly REJECTED — credit note is aggregated into `reversals` and subtracted from netTaxableValue/netCgst/netSgst/netIgst, and is emitted as a CDNR row in the filed GSTR-1.
findings.failureScenario: Receptionist refunds ₹50,000 (taxable, taxShare ₹2,500) on a GENERATED bill. recordRefund creates CN-2026-000042 with status PENDING_APPROVAL — no Accountant ever approves it, or an Accountant explicitly REJECTS it as unjustified. At month end the accountant pulls GET /api/billing/gst-register and runs the GSTR-1 export: the CDNR section contains CN-2026-000042 and output tax is reduced by ₹2,500 that was never sanctioned. The hospital files a return under-reporting ₹2,500 of GST, and a REJECTED note — which by design should have zero effect — still moved the liability.
findings.fixSketch: Add `status: "APPROVED"` to the match in gstr1Exporter._bucketCreditNotes (line 495) and to the cnAgg $match in billingController.js:3318. Surface PENDING_APPROVAL notes separately in the register response (e.g. `pendingReversals`) so the accountant can see what is blocked from filing rather than having it vanish.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link in the cited chain is present in the actual code. 1. CreditNote.js:66-71 defines status enum [APPROVED|PENDING_APPROVAL|REJECTED] default APPROVED, and the comment at :64-65 states the intended control verbatim: "the CN won't appear in the GST register until a different Accountant/Admin approves via /credit-notes/:id/approve". 2. The gate fires as claimed — Backend/services/Billing/billingService.js:2289 `const NEEDS_APPROVAL = amt > 10000 || taxShare > 0;` and :2317 `status: NEEDS_APPROVAL ? "PENDING_APPROVAL" : "APPROVED"`. (Minor path correction: the file is services/Billing/billingService.js, not services/billingService.js as cited.) 3. gstr1Exporter.js:495-497 confirmed verbatim: `CreditNote.find({ creditNoteDate: { $gte: periodStart, $lt: periodEnd } }).lean()` — no status predicate. The only post-filter (lines 507-538, the R8-FIX(#2) cancel-CN dedup) keys off reasonCode "07" + bill CANCELLED + period-lock, never status. Unapproved CNs flow into the CDNR row builder at :540-549. 4. billingController.js:3317-3328 confirmed verbatim: `$match: { creditNoteDate: { $gte: from, $lte: to } }` — no status predicate. Sums become `reversals` (:3343) and are subtracted at :3349-3353 into netTaxableValue/netCgst/netSgst/netIgst/netTaxAmount. 5. Grep over all CreditNote.find|aggregate|countDocuments call sites shows NO consumer filters on status anywhere; the only status read is the approve handler's own guard at billingController.js:1743. TWO CORRECTIONS (neither change…

findings.dimension: Billing core (bills, advances, payments, refunds, credit notes)
findings.title: updateItemQuantity reads a field that does not exist (item.tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit), so any quantity edit on a TPA bill wipes the patient's co-pay to zero and bills the insurer above the approved cap
findings.mechanism: `const tpaLimit = item.tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit ? toNum(item.tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit) * quantity : lineTotal;` — `tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit` is declared nowhere in BillItemSchema (PatientBillModel.js:21-159) and appears nowhere else in the backend (the real field is `ServicePricing.tpaApprovedLimit`, consumed only at addServiceToBill:556-559). It is therefore always undefined, so tpaLimit collapses to lineTotal and line 842-843 sets `item.tpaPayableAmount = lineTotal`, `item.patientPayableAmount = 0`. The pre-save recalcTotals does not repair this: with tpaPercent 0 it takes the caller-supplied absolute branch, `tpaShare = Math.min(toNum(item.tpaPayableAmount), lineTotal)` = lineTotal (PatientBillModel.js:604), so ptShare = 0 and the original approved-limit cap set at add time is permanently destroyed.
findings.failureScenario: TPA patient, service priced ₹1,000 with ServicePricing.tpaApprovedLimit = ₹800. addServiceToBill correctly writes tpaPayableAmount ₹800 / patientPayableAmount ₹200. The receptionist then corrects the quantity 1→2 via PUT /api/billing/:billId/items/:itemId (billing.override, DRAFT bill). tpaLimit falls back to lineTotal ₹2,000 → item.tpaPayableAmount = ₹2,000, patient share ₹0. The bill now shows patientPayableAmount ₹0 (no co-pay collected at the counter, ₹400 of real revenue lost) and claims ₹2,000 from the TPA against an ₹800/unit sanction — the insurer short-pays at settlement and the gap becomes a write-off.
findings.fixSketch: Either persist the per-unit cap on BillItemSchema (e.g. `tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit: { type: Dec, default: null }`, written by addServiceToBill from pricing.tpaApprovedLimit) and keep this code, or re-resolve the cap here via `ServicePricing.getPriceFor(item.serviceId, bill.paymentType, bill.tpa)`. Do not silently fall back to lineTotal — that is the fail-open direction. Simplest correct interim: scale the existing cap, `tpaPayable = min(toNum(item.tpaPayableAmount) / oldQty * quantity, lineTotal)`.
findings.verifyEvidence: Attempted refutation on four fronts; all failed. (1) FIELD ABSENT: backend-wide grep for `tpaApprovedLimitPerUnit` returns exactly 2 hits — billingService.js:839 and :840, i.e. only its own read site. BillItemSchema at Backend/models/PatientBillModel/PatientBillModel.js:41-55 declares only `tpaPercent` (Number, default 0), `tpaPayableAmount` (Dec), `patientPayableAmount` (Dec) — no per-unit cap. Bill-level `tpaApprovedAmount` (line 424) is a separate non-per-item field. The read is therefore always undefined and line 839-841 collapses `tpaLimit` to `lineTotal`; line 842-843 then set `item.tpaPayableAmount = lineTotal`, `item.patientPayableAmount = 0`. `Math.min(tpaLimit, lineTotal)` at 842 is a no-op since both operands are lineTotal. (2) recalcTotals DOES NOT REPAIR — it cements: addServiceToBill (billingService.js:575-600) never pushes `tpaPercent`, so it defaults to 0, and the pre-save hook takes the absolute branch exactly as claimed at PatientBillModel.js:597-606: `if (toNum(item.tpaPercent) > 0) {...} else { tpaShare = Math.min(toNum(item.tpaPayableAmount), lineTotal); }` then `ptShare = Math.max(0, lineTotal - tpaShare)` = 0. Cited line 604 is exact. Schema comment at lines 50-52 confirms tpaPayableAmount is a "caller-supplied absolute amount" the hook trusts — and the caller just corrupted it. (3) CAP IS REAL AT ADD TIME: billingService.js:554-560 computes `const tpaCap = toNum(pricing?.tpaApprovedLimit); tpaPayableAmount = tpaCap > 0 ? Math.min(tpaCap * quantity, li…

findings.dimension: Billing core (bills, advances, payments, refunds, credit notes)
findings.title: recalcTotals skips the whole totals block when billItems is empty, leaving stale gross/net/patientPayable — an emptied DRAFT bill keeps a phantom balance that is chased as a due and can take payment
findings.mechanism: The per-item aggregation and every bill-level total (grossAmount, totalDiscount, taxAmount, netAmount, tpaPayableAmount, patientPayableAmount, roundOffAmount, pendingOrdersAmount) are computed only inside `if (billItems.length > 0)`. When the array becomes empty they are never reset to 0 — the values from the previous save persist. The balance block below runs unconditionally and derives `balanceAmount = max(0, toNum(this.patientPayableAmount) - patientPaid)` from that stale figure. billingService.removeItemFromBill:782-785 filters the array with no floor and saves, so emptying a DRAFT bill is a one-click reachable state.
findings.failureScenario: Receptionist creates a DRAFT bill, adds a ₹5,000 service (save → patientPayableAmount ₹5,000, balanceAmount ₹5,000), then realises it was the wrong patient and deletes the line via DELETE /api/billing/:billId/items/:itemId. recalcTotals skips its block; the bill saves with zero items but netAmount ₹5,000 and balanceAmount ₹5,000. At the patient's next visit getPreviousPendingDues (billingService.js:427-454, which includes DRAFT with balanceAmount > 0.5) warns the biller of ₹5,000 of "previous pending dues" that do not exist, and because DRAFT bills accept payments (recordPayment R7hr-188 path), the overpay gate at billingService.js:1717 computes balanceNow ₹5,000 off the stale field and will happily accept ₹5,000 against a bill with no line items.
findings.fixSketch: Move the aggregation out of the length guard, or add an else-branch that zeroes gross/discount/tax/net/tpaPayable/patientPayable/roundOff/pendingOrders before the balance calculation. E.g. `const items = this.billItems || []; let gross=0,...; items.forEach(...)` and always write the totals.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every element of the finding is confirmed by the actual code, and I reproduced the defect live. 1. THE GUARD IS REAL AND TOTAL — PatientBillModel.js:541 `if (this.billItems && this.billItems.length > 0) {` opens a block that closes at :678. Every bill-level total is written INSIDE it: pendingOrdersAmount (:636), grossAmount (:645), totalDiscount (:646), taxAmount (:647), tpaPayableAmount (:648), roundOffAmount (:662), patientPayableAmount (:663), netAmount (:664), cgst/sgst/igst (:670-676). There is no else-branch. On an empty array all of these retain their previously-saved values. 2. THE BALANCE BLOCK IS UNCONDITIONAL AND CONSUMES THE STALE FIELD — :684-697 sits after the closing brace. :696 `this.balanceAmount = toDec(Math.max(0, toNum(this.patientPayableAmount) - patientPaid));` derives balance from the stale patientPayableAmount. The only zeroing branch (:693) requires billStatus REFUNDED/CANCELLED — a DRAFT does not qualify. 3. NO OTHER RESET PATH EXISTS — I read the full pre-save hook (:750-791): it contains only the writeOffAmount append-only guard, then `this.recalcTotals()` at :785. pre("validate") (:738-748) only mints a billNumber. Nothing else resets totals, so the model-level guard is the last word. 4. THE CALL SITES ARE EXACTLY AS CITED — removeItemFromBill (services/Billing/billingService.js:782-785) does `bill.billItems = bill.billItems.filter(...)` then `await bill.save()` with no floor on the resulting length; its status freeze (:774) blocks GENERATING/GEN…

findings.dimension: Billing core (bills, advances, payments, refunds, credit notes)
findings.title: Package-superseded line items fall into the pendingOrdersAmount bucket, so an ANH-package bill advertises charges that will never land
findings.mechanism: The branch is `if (isItemBillable(item) && !item.excludedByPackage) { ...aggregate... } else if (item.orderStatus !== "Cancelled") { pendingNet += lineTotal; }`. An item with orderStatus "Completed" (or undefined) that carries excludedByPackage=true fails the first condition, then passes the second (its status is not "Cancelled"), so its full lineTotal is added to pendingNet and persisted as pendingOrdersAmount. The intent documented at 629-632 is that the pending bucket captures only Ordered + InProgress; package exclusion is a permanent supersession, not a "coming soon" charge.
findings.failureScenario: IPD patient has ₹18,000 of per-line bed/nursing/doctor-visit charges already on the bill. An ANH package is attached mid-stay and those lines are flagged excludedByPackage. On the next save, recalcTotals correctly keeps them out of grossAmount/patientPayableAmount but writes pendingOrdersAmount = ₹18,000. The bill/ledger UI shows "Pending Orders ₹18,000" on top of the package total, so the cashier and the patient are told ₹18,000 of charges are still coming when they have in fact been absorbed by the package — a predictable billing dispute at discharge.
findings.fixSketch: Restrict the pending bucket to genuinely pending orders: `else if (!item.excludedByPackage && (item.orderStatus === "Ordered" || item.orderStatus === "InProgress")) { pendingNet += lineTotal; }`.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED, IMPACT REFUTED. Mechanism (real): PatientBillModel.js:623-633 is verbatim as filed. isItemBillable (line 165-167) = `!item.orderStatus || item.orderStatus === "Completed"`. An item with excludedByPackage=true and orderStatus Completed/undefined fails branch 1 (`isItemBillable(item) && !item.excludedByPackage`), passes branch 2 (`item.orderStatus !== "Cancelled"`), so lineTotal is added to pendingNet and persisted at line 636 (`this.pendingOrdersAmount = toDec(pendingNet)`). This is reachable in production: autoBillingService.js:2366-2382 sets excludedByPackage=true on Room/Nursing/Consultation/DOC-/BED- lines on ANH attach — auto-charges that carry no orderStatus, exactly the ones that fall through — then :2398-2414 re-saves each affected bill, firing recalcTotals. The written value contradicts the schema contract at :332-337 ("line items still in Ordered / InProgress state") and the inline comment at :630-631. Fix sketch is correct. Impact (not supported): pendingOrdersAmount has ZERO readers repo-wide. Case-insensitive grep over all of D:\Spherehealth returns only the two definition/write sites (PatientBillModel.js:338, :636) — no controller, service, report, print template, or frontend component reads it. The failure scenario's premise ("bill/ledger UI shows 'Pending Orders Rs.18,000'... cashier and patient are told") is false: the only UI with that label, ServicesOrdersPanel.jsx:438-446, computes its own figure client-side at :260-265 (`activeOrders…

findings.title: GSTR-1 treats tax-INCLUSIVE bill-level netAmount as the pre-tax taxable base — overstates B2C turnover and inflates every invoiceValue by the tax
findings.mechanism: `_bucketPatientBills` reads `netAmount = toNum(b.netAmount)` (bill-level) at line 227 and at line 241 comments `netAmount is post-discount + pre-tax in this model`. That is false: `PatientBillModel.js:664` computes `this.netAmount = toDec(gross - disc + tax - extra + _roundOff)` — the bill-level field is tax-INCLUSIVE (and matches the stated known design). The same codebase gets this right elsewhere: the cancel path at `billingController.js:1923` derives its CN taxable base as `Math.max(0, toNum(bill.netAmount) - toNum(bill.taxAmount))`, explicitly subtracting tax back out. Two consequences: (a) the b2c row's `taxableValue` (line 334 `cur.taxableValue += taxable`) is inflated by the full tax amount while its cgst/sgst/igst come from the correct bill-level fields (lines 335-337), so the row self-contradicts; (b) `invoiceValue` at line 286 adds `taxAmount` to an already-tax-inclusive `netAmount`, double-counting tax. Note the b2b/b2cl `items[]` array (line 292) is built from `ratesByItem`, which sums item-level `it.netAmount` — tax-EXCLUSIVE and correct — so the defect is isolated to the bill-level reads and the two representations disagree within a single emitted invoice.
findings.failureScenario: Intra-state bill: gross 10,000, no discount, 5% GST -> taxAmount=500, netAmount=10,500 (tax-inclusive). GSTR-1 b2c emits taxableValue=10,500 with cgst=250/sgst=250 — the portal's taxable x rate cross-check expects 10,000 x 5% = 500 and rejects/flags the row; declared turnover is overstated by the entire tax. Separately, invoiceValue is emitted as 11,000 instead of 10,500. The invoiceValue inflation also mis-drives the B2CL classification at line 317: an inter-state no-GSTIN invoice with a true value of 248,000 (taxable 236,190 + 11,810 IGST at 5%) computes invoiceValue = 248,000 + 11,810 = 259,810 > 250,000 and is wrongly listed individually under b2cl instead of aggregated into b2c.
findings.fixSketch: Derive the pre-tax base explicitly: `const taxable = netAmount - taxAmount;` and `invoiceValue: Number(netAmount.toFixed(2))` (netAmount already includes tax). Better: build the b2c row from the same `ratesByItem` per-rate item-level sums already computed at lines 249-256 so the bill-level and line-level representations cannot drift. Correct the misleading comment at line 241.
findings.verifyEvidence: All cited code verified verbatim; could not refute any link in the chain. MECHANISM CONFIRMED: - gstr1Exporter.js:225 `const netAmount = toNum(b.netAmount);` (bill-level), :241 `const taxable = netAmount; // netAmount is post-discount + pre-tax in this model`. - PatientBillModel.js:664 `this.netAmount = toDec(gross - disc + tax - extra + _roundOff);` — `tax` is ADDED, so bill-level netAmount is tax-INCLUSIVE. The line-241 comment is factually false, and this also contradicts the explicitly documented KNOWN DESIGN ("bill-level netAmount is TAX-INCLUSIVE"), so this is the exporter violating a stated invariant, not the auditor misreading intent. - Corroboration the codebase knows better: controllers/Billing/billingController.js:1923 `taxableValue: Math.max(0, toNum(bill.netAmount) - toNum(bill.taxAmount))` — the cancel/CDNR path explicitly subtracts tax back out. (Finding cited this as controllers/billingController.js; real path is controllers/Billing/billingController.js — line 1923 exact. Immaterial path typo.) - Item-level base is genuinely tax-exclusive: PatientBillModel.js:558-569 `const tAmt = item.isTaxable ? (nAmt * toNum(item.taxPercent)) / 100 : 0; item.netAmount = toDec(nAmt);` — so `ratesByItem` (gstr1Exporter.js:252-253, summing `it.netAmount`) is correct, and the b2b/b2cl `items[]` at :292 disagrees with the same invoice's bill-level `invoiceValue` at :286. The two representations really do drift within one emitted invoice. CONSEQUENCE (a) CONFIRMED — self-contrad…

findings.title: Both GST exporters ignore CreditNote.status — PENDING_APPROVAL and REJECTED credit notes still reverse output tax, defeating the maker-checker control entirely
findings.mechanism: `CreditNote.js:66-71` defines `status: { enum: ['APPROVED','PENDING_APPROVAL','REJECTED'], default: 'APPROVED' }` as an R7bb-FIX-E-2 / D3-CRIT-2 maker-checker, whose own schema comment (lines 62-65) states: 'the CN won't appear in the GST register until a different Accountant/Admin approves via /credit-notes/:id/approve'. Neither exporter applies that filter. `_bucketCreditNotes` queries only on `creditNoteDate`; the GSTR-3B `cnAgg` `$match` likewise filters only on `creditNoteDate` and then subtracts the full sum at lines 91-94. Critically, `billingService.js:2289` sets `const NEEDS_APPROVAL = amt > 10000 || taxShare > 0` — so EVERY tax-bearing credit note (i.e. exactly the population that reaches the CDNR section and moves output tax) is created in PENDING_APPROVAL at line 2317. The control therefore gates precisely the notes the exporters read unfiltered, and a note explicitly REJECTED by a checker still reduces the hospital's declared liability.
findings.failureScenario: A cashier raises a 50,000 refund on an intra-state 18% bill; taxShare=7,627, so the CN is created PENDING_APPROVAL (billingService.js:2317). The Accountant reviews it and REJECTS it as fraudulent (status='REJECTED'). The refund CN nonetheless appears in GSTR-1 cdnr and is subtracted in GSTR-3B section_3_1, reducing declared CGST+SGST by 7,627 for a reversal that was never authorised. A colluding or careless cashier can suppress output tax at will by raising refund CNs that are never approved — the sign-off gate has no effect on what is filed.
findings.fixSketch: Add `status: 'APPROVED'` to the `_bucketCreditNotes` query (gstr1Exporter.js:495) and to the GSTR-3B `$match` (gstr3bExporter.js:65). Consider surfacing the count of PENDING_APPROVAL CNs in the period on the return `summary` so the accountant sees unapproved reversals blocking a clean filing rather than them silently vanishing.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED at every cited location. 1) gstr1Exporter.js:495-497 — `const cns = await CreditNote.find({ creditNoteDate: { $gte: periodStart, $lt: periodEnd } }).lean();` — no `status` predicate. The R8-FIX(#2) block that follows (lines 499-538) filters only on `reasonCode === "07"` + GstMonthlySnapshot period-lock; it never inspects `status`. 2) gstr3bExporter.js:64-75 — `{ $match: { creditNoteDate: { $gte: periodStart, $lt: periodEnd } } }` then `$group` summing taxableValue/cgstAmount/sgstAmount/igstAmount; lines 91-94 subtract the full sum: `netCgst = Number((cgstOut - cnCgst).toFixed(2))` etc., feeding section_3_1 (line 104-109). No status predicate. 3) CreditNote.js:66-71 — `status: { type: String, enum: ["APPROVED","PENDING_APPROVAL","REJECTED"], default: "APPROVED", index: true }`. Lines 58-65 comment verbatim: "the CN won't appear in the GST register until a different Accountant/Admin approves via /credit-notes/:id/approve" — a promise the exporters do not keep. 4) billingService.js:2289 (actual path Backend/services/Billing/billingService.js, NOT services/billingService.js as cited — minor citation error) — `const NEEDS_APPROVAL = amt > 10000 || taxShare > 0;` → line 2317 `status: NEEDS_APPROVAL ? "PENDING_APPROVAL" : "APPROVED"`. The `taxShare > 0` disjunct is the load-bearing claim and it holds: EVERY tax-bearing CN — exactly the population that populates CDNR and moves output tax (cgstShare/sgstShare/igstShare set at 2282-2284) — is created PENDING_APPROV…

findings.title: R8-FIX(#2) cancel-CN double-reversal guard was applied to GSTR-1 only — GSTR-3B still double-reverses cancelled invoices, so the two returns disagree for the same month
findings.mechanism: A CANCELLED bill is already reversed by EXCLUSION from outward supply: `gstService.js:62` matches `billStatus: { $nin: ['DRAFT','CANCELLED'] }`, so the cancelled invoice never enters `grossTotals`. The cancel path at `billingController.js:1915-1931` additionally writes a full-value reversal CreditNote with `reasonCode: '07'` and status defaulting to APPROVED. gstr1Exporter.js:499-538 recognises this and skips such cancel-CNs when the original bill's GST period is still OPEN (R8-FIX(#2)), because the exclusion is the single complete reversal. gstr3bExporter.js has NO equivalent logic — its `cnAgg` sums every CreditNote in the period unconditionally and lines 91-94 subtract it from an outward total that already excluded the cancelled bill. This is a regression surface left by R8-FIX(#2): the fix hardened one exporter and left its sibling, so GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B now report structurally different output tax for the same period, which is itself a portal reconciliation failure.
findings.failureScenario: An intra-state bill of 11,800 (taxable 10,000 + 900 CGST + 900 SGST) is generated and then cancelled in the same open month. gstService excludes it from grossTotals (correct, -10,000/-900/-900). The cancel CN (taxableValue 10,000, cgst 900, sgst 900) is then subtracted AGAIN at gstr3bExporter.js:91-94. GSTR-3B section_3_1 declares taxable -10,000 and CGST/SGST -900 each relative to truth; GSTR-1 for the same month (which drops the cancel-CN) declares the correct figures. Output tax is under-reported by 1,800, the two returns mismatch, and GSTR-1-vs-3B liability comparison on the portal raises a notice.
findings.fixSketch: Extract the R8-FIX(#2) skip logic from gstr1Exporter.js:499-538 into a shared helper (e.g. `resolveReversibleCreditNotes(periodStart, periodEnd)`) and have both exporters consume it, so the cancel-CN/period-lock rule can never drift between the two returns again. Applying the CN-status filter from the finding above in the same helper keeps both concerns in one place.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited premise verified against actual code; refutation attempts all failed. 1. EXCLUSION IS REAL — gstService.js:60-63 (hospitalPipeline $match) is verbatim as cited:    `billGeneratedAt: { $gte: periodStart, $lt: periodEnd }, billStatus: { $nin: ["DRAFT", "CANCELLED"] }`    A CANCELLED bill therefore never contributes to grossTotals. billingController.js:1865 confirms cancelBill sets `b.billStatus = "CANCELLED"` (followed by `await b.save()` at :1888), so the bill genuinely lands in that $nin bucket. 2. THE CANCEL-CN IS REAL AND FULL-VALUE — Backend/controllers/Billing/billingController.js:1915-1931 (note: the finding cites `Backend/billingController.js`; the true path is `Backend/controllers/Billing/billingController.js` — path detail only, mechanism unaffected) creates a CreditNote with `reasonCode: "07"` (:1921), `taxableValue: Math.max(0, toNum(bill.netAmount) - toNum(bill.taxAmount))` (:1923), and full `cgstAmount/sgstAmount/igstAmount` (:1925-1927). The inline comment at :1909-1910 states "status stays the schema default (APPROVED — subtracts from the register immediately)", and CreditNote.js:66-71 confirms `status ... default: "APPROVED"`. So the CN is live in the register the moment the bill is cancelled. 3. GSTR-1 HAS THE GUARD — gstr1Exporter.js:499-538 is exactly the R8-FIX(#2) block described: it collects `reasonCode === "07"` CNs (:508-510), confirms the original bill is `billStatus: "CANCELLED"` (:514-516), resolves the bill's IST period (:526-529), chec…

findings.title: Hospital-bill B2C rows are still keyed on a single dominantRate — R8 fixed this for pharmacy only, so mixed-rate hospital bills are declared entirely at one GST slab
findings.mechanism: R8-CRIT explicitly fixed this class of bug in `_bucketPharmacySales`: lines 456-482 now emit one B2C(S) row PER GST rate by iterating `ratesByItem`, with the comment noting that keying the whole sale on a single dominantRate 'understat[ed] (or, when the largest line was exempt, zero[ed]) output tax'. The identical defect remains in `_bucketPatientBills`: line 323 keys the whole bill on `${placeOfSupply}-${dominantRate}` and dumps the bill-level taxable and bill-level cgst/sgst/igst into that one row. `dominantRate` is additionally selected by the single LARGEST LINE (line 254 `if (v > dominantTaxable)`) rather than by the largest rate GROUP — the per-rate totals in `ratesByItem` are computed at line 253 but never used for the hospital b2c bucket. The tax amounts stay correct (they come from bill-level fields) but the rate attribution does not, so the portal's per-row taxable x rate = tax cross-check fails.
findings.failureScenario: A bill has one room-rent line of 6,000 at 5% GST and three consumable lines of 2,000 each (6,000 total) at 18%. dominantRate is picked from the single largest LINE (6,000 room rent) = 5%. The entire bill's taxable is filed under the 5% b2c row while carrying the real blended tax (300 + 1,080 = 1,380). The portal computes 12,000 x 5% = 600 against a declared 1,380 and flags the row; the 18% slab shows zero turnover for that bill. Aggregated across a month this misstates the rate-wise breakup the department reconciles against HSN Line 12.
findings.fixSketch: Mirror the pharmacy fix (lines 456-482) in `_bucketPatientBills`: iterate `Object.entries(ratesByItem)` and emit one b2c row per rate, computing each row's cgst/sgst/igst from that rate's own taxable rather than from bill-level totals. This composes naturally with the netAmount fix above, since ratesByItem is already the correct tax-exclusive per-rate base.
findings.verifyEvidence: Mechanism verified exactly as described, at every cited line. 1) gstr1Exporter.js:254 — `if (v > dominantTaxable) { dominantTaxable = v; dominantRate = r; }` sits INSIDE the per-item loop (249-281), so `dominantRate` tracks the largest single LINE, not the largest rate GROUP. `ratesByItem[r] = (ratesByItem[r] || 0) + v` (line 253) accumulates correct per-rate totals but is never consulted for this choice. 2) gstr1Exporter.js:323 — `const k = `${placeOfSupply}-${dominantRate}`;` keys the entire bill on that one rate. Lines 326 (`rate: dominantRate`) and 334-337 (`cur.taxableValue += taxable; cur.cgstAmount += cgst; ...`) dump bill-LEVEL totals (read at 225-229 from b.netAmount/b.cgstAmount/b.sgstAmount/b.igstAmount) into the single row. `ratesByItem` feeds only `invoice.items` (292-306), which is dead for the b2c path — b2c rows carry no `items` array. 3) Asymmetry with the R8 fix is real and self-documented: `_bucketPharmacySales` 454-482 carries the R8-CRIT comment ("keying the whole sale on a single dominantRate ... understating (or, when the largest line was exempt, zeroing) output tax") and iterates `Object.entries(ratesByItem)` emitting one row per rate with `gstAtRate = (rTaxable * rr) / 100`, plus a `counted` flag so invoiceCount increments once. `_bucketPatientBills` received no equivalent change. 4) No downstream correction. The merge at 815-828 keys on `${r.placeOfSupply}-${r.rate}` and sums blindly; 829-838 only rounds. So the mis-attributed hospital row is merged…

findings.title: R7bm-F7 state-code normalisation was added to gstr1Exporter only — gstService, GSTR-3B and recalcTotals still do raw string compares, so a non-canonical HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE flips intra/inter-state between the two returns
findings.mechanism: gstr1Exporter.js:108-136 introduced `normalizeGstStateCode` + `isIntraStateSupply` precisely because, per its own comment (lines 49-52), raw string compares meant '29' vs '29 ' vs '29-KA' vs 'Karnataka' 'all looked different and would mis-classify the supply'. It then normalises HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE at line 136 and both sides of every comparison. But the sibling consumers were never migrated: gstService.js:102 takes the raw env value and line 140 does a bare `$ne` against the raw stored placeOfSupply; PatientBillModel.js:583 and :668 do `String(this.placeOfSupply).trim() !== _hosp`; gstr3bExporter.js:35 reads the raw env too. So the exact mis-classification R7bm-F7 was written to prevent still occurs on every non-gstr1 path — and because GSTR-3B is built from gstService's grossTotals, the two returns can now split a supply differently from identical data. gstr1's normaliser is what makes the divergence possible rather than merely uniform.
findings.failureScenario: Operator sets HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE='29-KA' (a form gstr1's own normaliser documents as valid at line 103). Bills store placeOfSupply='29'. GSTR-1 normalises both to '29' -> intra-state -> emits CGST+SGST. gstService compares '29' !== '29-KA' -> `_isInterState`=true -> GSTR-3B section_3_1 reports the same supplies as IGST, and PatientBillModel.recalcTotals:583 likewise stamps every item igstAmount=tax with cgst=sgst=0. The month is filed with GSTR-1 showing CGST/SGST and GSTR-3B showing IGST for identical turnover — a guaranteed portal mismatch, and the wrong tax head is credited to the state.
findings.fixSketch: Export `normalizeGstStateCode` (already exported at gstr1Exporter.js:917) into a shared `utils/gstState.js` and consume it in gstService.js:102, gstr3bExporter.js:35 and PatientBillModel.js:580/667 — normalising the env value once at module load and each stored placeOfSupply at compare time. Alternatively fail-fast at boot if HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE does not normalise to itself, so a non-canonical config cannot reach either exporter.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four cited locations verified as raw string compares; the gstr1-only normalisation is confirmed to be load-bearing. CONFIRMING EVIDENCE: 1. gstr1Exporter.js:136 — `const HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE = normalizeGstStateCode(HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE_RAW) || "29"` normalises the env value at module load. isIntraStateSupply (:145-147) normalises BOTH sides. 2. gstr1Exporter.js:296-304 — the normalised `intraState` DIRECTLY drives emitted heads: `cgstAmount: intraState ? (taxableValue*rate)/200 : 0`, `igstAmount: !intraState ? (taxableValue*rate)/100 : 0`. It recomputes from rate rather than reading stored amounts, so the normalised decision genuinely governs GSTR-1 output (this was the key refutation attempt — it failed). 3. gstService.js:102 raw `(process.env.HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE || "").trim()`; :140 bare `{ $ne: ["$_pos", hospitalStateCode] }` against raw stored placeOfSupply. 4. gstService.js:70-80 — hospital pipeline reads stored billItems.cgstAmount/sgstAmount/igstAmount verbatim, so recalcTotals' raw-compare decision propagates into grossTotals → GSTR-3B §3.1 (gstr3bExporter.js:56-61). The 3B/GSTR-1 split mechanism is real. 5. gstr3bExporter.js:35 — `process.env.HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE || "29"`, raw. 6. PatientBillModel.js:583 and :668 — `String(this.placeOfSupply).trim() !== _hosp`, raw. The `this.constructor?.HOSPITAL_STATE_CODE` fallback at :580 is DEAD — grep across Backend finds no definition of that static anywhere, only reads. 7. No guard prevents the trigger: no env schema / boot…

findings.title: CORPORATE bills never get a payer/patient split — recalcTotals only splits when paymentType === "TPA", so the whole corporate claim workflow is broken and the patient is billed the employer's share
findings.mechanism: recalcTotals() computes the payer split inside `if (this.paymentType === "TPA")` only. For paymentType === "CORPORATE" it takes the else branch (tpaShare = 0; ptShare = lineTotal) for every line, then unconditionally overwrites the bill-level fields: `this.tpaPayableAmount = toDec(tpaPay)` (= 0) and `this.patientPayableAmount = toDec(_ptRounded)` (= full net). Meanwhile every TPA endpoint explicitly admits CORPORATE: tpaPreAuthSubmit (line 1541), tpaApprove (1621) and tpaSettle (2294) all gate on `["TPA","CORPORATE"]`. So tpaPreAuthSubmit's `bill.tpaPayableAmount = Number(req.body.requestedAmount)` (line 1552) is wiped back to 0 by the pre-save hook on the very same save(), and tpaApprove's fallback `Number(req.body.approvedAmount) || b.tpaPayableAmount || 0` (line 1652) resolves to 0.
findings.failureScenario: Corporate patient (employer pays 100%), IPD bill ₹50,000, paymentType=CORPORATE. Desk POSTs /billing/:id/tpa-preauth-submit {requestedAmount: 50000} → 200, but the persisted bill reads tpaPayableAmount = 0 and patientPayableAmount = ₹50,000 (balanceAmount ₹50,000 shown as due FROM THE PATIENT at the cashier and in previous-dues/aging). Coordinator then POSTs /tpa-approve with no approvedAmount → tpaApprovedAmount = 0. Employer wires ₹50,000 → POST /tpa-settle {settledAmount:50000} → `approved` = 0 (line 2301), overpay = 50000 > 10 → hard 400 "Settled amount ₹50000 exceeds approved ₹0". Corporate settlement is unreachable, and the claim form (claimFormService.js:136) prints "Insurer/TPA Payable Rs. 0.00 / Patient Share Rs. 50,000.00".
findings.fixSketch: Change the guard at PatientBillModel.js:597 to `if (this.paymentType === "TPA" || this.paymentType === "CORPORATE")` (both are third-party-payer splits driven by item.tpaPercent / item.tpaPayableAmount), and add an E2E case that runs preauth→approve→settle on a CORPORATE bill.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link of the cited mechanism is present in the code as described. I tried to refute it on four fronts and all four failed. 1. SPLIT GUARD IS TPA-ONLY — PatientBillModel.js:597 reads exactly `if (this.paymentType === "TPA") {`. The else branch at 607-610 hardcodes `tpaShare = 0; ptShare = lineTotal;` for every line. CORPORATE is a real, first-class enum value (PatientBillModel.js:292 `enum: ["CASH", "TPA", "CORPORATE"]`), so it deterministically takes the else branch. The schema comment at line 47 even admits the gap: "TPA split (filled only when paymentType === TPA)". 2. THE OVERWRITE IS REAL AND UNCONDITIONAL — PatientBillModel.js:785 `this.recalcTotals();` sits in `pre("save")` (declared line 750) with no guard, so it runs on EVERY save. Line 648 `this.tpaPayableAmount = toDec(tpaPay);` (= 0 for CORPORATE) and line 663 `this.patientPayableAmount = toDec(_ptRounded);` (= full net) execute whenever `billItems.length > 0` (line 541). So billingController.js:1552's `bill.tpaPayableAmount = Number(req.body.requestedAmount) || ...` is genuinely wiped back to 0 by the pre-save hook on the very same `await bill.save()` at line 1566. 3. ALL THREE TPA ENDPOINTS DO ADMIT CORPORATE — verified verbatim: line 1541 (`bill.paymentType !== "TPA" && bill.paymentType !== "CORPORATE"` → preauth), 1621 (approve), 2294 (`!["TPA","CORPORATE"].includes(bill.paymentType)` → settle). Also 1488, 1992, 2072, 2147 and dashboardsController.js:754 all treat CORPORATE as a payer case. So this is not…

findings.title: tpaSettle caps each remittance against the approved amount individually but never against the cumulative settled total — a claim can be settled repeatedly, double-crediting the bill
findings.line: 2283-2323 (ALLOWED / approved / overpay / duplicate guard), 2461-2465 (full settle keeps APPROVED)
findings.mechanism: `ALLOWED = ["APPROVED","PARTIAL_APPROVED"]` and a full settlement deliberately leaves tpaClaimStatus = APPROVED ("the schema enum has no SETTLED value", line 2462) — so the bill stays in an allowed state forever. The overpay guard compares only `settledAmount` against `approved = toN(bill.tpaApprovedAmount)`; it never sums the existing TPA_CLAIM payment rows. The only replay protection is the per-UTR duplicate check at line 2317 (`p.transactionId === ...`), which a different UTR string trivially bypasses, and the route (routes/Billing/billingRoutes.js:90) has no idempotencyGuard.
findings.failureScenario: Claim approved ₹1,00,000. POST /billing/:id/tpa-settle {settledAmount:100000, transactionId:"UTR-1"} → payment row +₹1,00,000, status stays APPROVED. The same (or any) tpa.claim user re-posts {settledAmount:100000, transactionId:"UTR-2"} (retry, re-keyed UTR, or malice) → status APPROVED is in ALLOWED, overpay = max(0, 100000-100000) = 0, UTR-2 is not a duplicate → a SECOND ₹1,00,000 TPA_CLAIM row is pushed. bill.advancePaid (PatientBillModel.js:684-685) now reads ₹2,00,000 against a ₹1,00,000 approval; day-book/TPA collection tiles and form16aGenerator's TDS base are inflated, and recordRefund's tpaNet cap (billingService.js:2076-2081) now permits refunding ₹2,00,000 of insurer money that was never received.
findings.fixSketch: In the retry block compute `alreadySettled = sum(payments where paymentMode==="TPA_CLAIM")` and gate on `alreadySettled + settledAmount <= approved + 10`; add idempotencyGuard("tpaSettle") on the route.
findings.verifyEvidence: All claimed mechanisms verified against actual code. 1. billingController.js:2283-2284 — `const ALLOWED = ["APPROVED", "PARTIAL_APPROVED"];` guard as described. 2. billingController.js:2461-2465 — full-settle branch is a no-op on status: comment "Fully settled — keep APPROVED (the schema enum has no SETTLED value; APPROVED + a TPA_CLAIM payment row is the signal)". Only `bill.remarks` is appended. Partial branch (line 2457) sets `PARTIAL_APPROVED`, also in ALLOWED. So the bill NEVER leaves the settle-allowed set after a settlement. 3. billingController.js:2301-2313 — `const approved = toN(bill.tpaApprovedAmount) || toN(bill.tpaPayableAmount);` / `const overpay = Math.max(0, settledAmount - approved);` / `if (overpay > 10) throw`. The cap compares ONLY the current request's settledAmount against the full approved figure. No reduce/sum over existing `bill.payments` TPA_CLAIM rows exists anywhere in the function. 4. Grep of `tpaApprovedAmount` across the controller: written at 1652/1669 (approve), zeroed only at 1874 and 2191 (deny/reject paths). Never decremented by tpaSettle. Line 2379 comment confirms intent: "the original tpaApprovedAmount stays, the TPA_CLAIM payment row records what they actually wired". So `approved` is the full amount on every replay and overpay = max(0, 100000-100000) = 0. 5. billingController.js:2317-2323 — sole replay guard is `(bill.payments||[]).find(p => p.paymentMode === "TPA_CLAIM" && p.transactionId === String(req.body.transactionId).trim())`.…

findings.title: tpaDeny's state guard is ineffective against the case its own comment claims to fix — a fully settled claim (which stays APPROVED) can still be denied, zeroing tpaApprovedAmount while the money stays on the bill
findings.mechanism: The comment at 2176-2178 states "Pre-R7av tpaDeny could be invoked on already-SETTLED claims (wiping the approved amount)" and the guard is `ALLOWED = ["PENDING","SUBMITTED","APPROVED","PARTIAL_APPROVED","NOT_APPLICABLE"]`. But there is no SETTLED state: a full settlement intentionally leaves tpaClaimStatus = APPROVED and a partial one leaves PARTIAL_APPROVED — both are in ALLOWED. The handler then unconditionally does `b.tpaApprovedAmount = 0` (line 2191) and overwrites `b.remarks` wholesale (line 2192, destroying the settlement remarks appended at 2464), while the TPA_CLAIM payment rows are left untouched.
findings.failureScenario: Claim approved ₹80,000, TPA wires ₹80,000, settle posts the TPA_CLAIM row (status remains APPROVED). A month later the desk mis-clicks "Deny" on that bill → 200 OK: tpaClaimStatus = REJECTED, tpaApprovedAmount = 0, remarks replaced with "TPA Denied: …". The TPA register/denial-rate KPI now shows a rejected claim that carries ₹80,000 of received insurer money, and there is no state left from which to re-approve (tpaApprove's ALLOWED_FROM excludes REJECTED, line 1612) — the claim must be re-submitted from scratch, resetting the maker-checker trail.
findings.fixSketch: Block deny when any positive TPA_CLAIM payment row exists (`bill.payments.some(p => p.paymentMode==="TPA_CLAIM" && toNum(p.amount) > 0)`) — a settled claim must be reversed via refund, not denied; and append to remarks instead of overwriting.
findings.verifyEvidence: Verified against actual code; every load-bearing element of the mechanism holds. GUARD — billingController.js:2183 is verbatim `const ALLOWED = ["PENDING","SUBMITTED","APPROVED","PARTIAL_APPROVED","NOT_APPLICABLE"];`, and its own comment at 2176-2178 claims to fix the case where "tpaDeny could be invoked on already-SETTLED claims (wiping the approved amount)". SETTLE TERMINAL STATES — the strongest refutation attempt only partially lands. The model DOES have a terminal `SETTLED_WRITEOFF` enum value (PatientBillModel.js:420) which is NOT in ALLOWED, so the write-off branch (set at 2398) is correctly blocked. This makes the finding's phrasing "there is no SETTLED state" imprecise. But it covers only one of three branches:   • shortfall === 0 (full settle) -> stays APPROVED (2461-2464, comment: "Fully settled — keep APPROVED (the schema enum has no SETTLED value; APPROVED + a TPA_CLAIM payment row is the signal)") -> in ALLOWED -> deny passes.   • shortfall > 0 + shortfallTo=PATIENT -> PARTIAL_APPROVED (2457) -> in ALLOWED -> deny passes.   • shortfall > 0 + WRITEOFF -> SETTLED_WRITEOFF (2398) -> blocked (correct). The full-settlement case is exactly what the guard's comment claims to fix, and it sails through. The guard is ineffective against its stated purpose. MUTATION — 2190-2192: unconditional `b.tpaClaimStatus = "REJECTED"`, `b.tpaApprovedAmount = 0`, and `if (req.body.reason) b.remarks = \`TPA Denied: ${req.body.reason}\`` — a wholesale overwrite that destroys the " | TP…

findings.title: tpaSettle shortfallTo=PATIENT silently becomes a WRITE-OFF when no line carries a TPA share — the hospital discounts the shortfall it intended to collect
findings.mechanism: The PATIENT branch redistributes the shortfall by re-tagging line items, but only when `totalTpaShare = sum(item.tpaPayableAmount) > 0`. Otherwise the else-branch at 2451 does `bill.extraDiscount = priorExtra + shortfall` with the reason "(no per-line split — collected from patient at desk)". extraDiscount is a *concession*: recalcTotals (PatientBillModel.js:643, 646, 663-664) subtracts it from the patient share and adds it to totalDiscount. So the caller asks to CHARGE the patient and the system CREDITS them the same amount. This branch is the normal path for every CORPORATE bill (see the recalcTotals finding — item.tpaPayableAmount is always 0 there) and for any bill-level-TPA bill whose lines were never split.
findings.failureScenario: CORPORATE/bill-level-TPA bill of ₹50,000, approved ₹50,000, employer/TPA remits only ₹46,000. TPA desk POSTs /tpa-settle {settledAmount:46000, shortfallTo:"PATIENT", transactionId:"UTR-9"} expecting the ₹4,000 to become a patient due. Instead extraDiscount = ₹4,000 → patientPayableAmount drops by ₹4,000 → balanceAmount 0 → the patient is discharged owing nothing, the ₹4,000 is booked as a discount (not a write-off), and it lands in the "Discount" KPI rather than writeOffAmount. The remarks even claim "₹4000.00 → patient liability" (line 2459).
findings.fixSketch: In the no-split branch, either reject with 400 ("bill has no per-line TPA split — cannot route shortfall to patient") or raise the patient share via a real charge line, and never reuse extraDiscount for a shortfall the desk intends to collect.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED, IMPACT REFUTED. CONFIRMED — the sign inversion is real and reachable: 1. billingController.js:2451-2456 is verbatim as cited: in the shortfallTo=PATIENT branch, when totalTpaShare (sum of item.tpaPayableAmount, computed 2414-2415) is 0, the else does `bill.extraDiscount = priorExtra + shortfall` with reason "(no per-line split — collected from patient at desk)". 2. extraDiscount is unambiguously a CONCESSION, not a charge. In models/PatientBillModel/PatientBillModel.js (note: real path is PatientBillModel/PatientBillModel.js, not Billing/PatientBillModel.js as filed): :643 `const extra = Math.min(Math.max(0, toNum(this.extraDiscount)||0), ptPay)`; :646 `this.totalDiscount = toDec(disc + extra)`; :659-663 `_ptExact = ptPay - extra` → `this.patientPayableAmount = toDec(_ptRounded)`. So the caller asks to CHARGE and the hook CREDITS the same amount. 3. Reachability of the else-branch on the normal CORPORATE path is confirmed: tpaSettle allows CORPORATE (:2294 `["TPA","CORPORATE"].includes(bill.paymentType)`); tpaApprove allows CORPORATE (:1621) and sets `tpaApprovedAmount = Number(req.body.approvedAmount) || b.tpaPayableAmount || 0` (:1652), so `approved` at :2301 is nonzero and shortfall>0 fires; and recalcTotals gates the split strictly on `if (this.paymentType === "TPA")` (:597) with the else at :607-609 forcing `tpaShare = 0; ptShare = lineTotal`. Hence every CORPORATE bill has item.tpaPayableAmount == 0 → totalTpaShare == 0 → else-branch. The auditor's…

findings.title: tpaApprove accepts any approvedAmount with no upper bound, no negative/NaN check and no relation to the bill total — an approval of an arbitrary amount lets a matching TPA_CLAIM remittance be posted
findings.mechanism: `Number(req.body.approvedAmount)` is written straight to tpaApprovedAmount with no validation against bill.tpaPayableAmount / netAmount, no `Number.isFinite` check and no `>= 0` check (contrast tpaSettle, which validates settledAmount at 2255-2258). It is the ONLY figure tpaSettle's overpay guard compares against (line 2301-2313), so whatever is approved here becomes the ceiling for money posted onto the bill. A negative value (e.g. -5000) is truthy and persists; `approvedAmount: 0` silently falls back to the full payable, so an insurer's zero-approval reads as a full approval.
findings.failureScenario: On a ₹12,000 TPA bill, a tpa.claim user (or a second TPA-coordinator account colluding to pass the maker-checker check at 1636) POSTs /tpa-approve {approvedAmount: 500000} → 200, tpaApprovedAmount = ₹5,00,000 on a ₹12,000 bill with no error. POST /tpa-settle {settledAmount:500000, transactionId:"X"} → overpay = 0 → a ₹5,00,000 TPA_CLAIM payment row lands on a ₹12,000 bill. advancePaid, the day-book by-mode tile and the TDS/26AS base (form16aGenerator.js:79-93) all report ₹5,00,000 of phantom insurer receipts, and recordRefund's tpaNet cap (billingService.js:2076-2081) then authorises a ₹5,00,000 "refund to insurer".
findings.fixSketch: Validate: `const appr = Number(req.body.approvedAmount); if (req.body.approvedAmount != null && (!Number.isFinite(appr) || appr < 0 || appr > toN(bill.tpaPayableAmount) + 1)) return 400;` and treat an explicit 0 as zero-approval (route to /tpa-deny) rather than falling back to the full payable.
findings.verifyEvidence: I tried to refute this on four fronts (schema-level min, route RBAC, a downstream clamp, and the refund cash-out path) and the core mechanism survived all four. CITED LINE VERBATIM — billingController.js:1652 inside the retryVE block:   b.tpaApprovedAmount = Number(req.body.approvedAmount) || b.tpaPayableAmount || 0; No Number.isFinite check, no >= 0 check, no comparison against b.tpaPayableAmount or b.netAmount. The surrounding lines 1651-1663 validate/derive everything else (status enum 1612, paymentType 1621, maker-checker 1636, actor from req.user 1661) — the money figure is the one unvalidated input. Confirmed. NO SCHEMA BACKSTOP — PatientBillModel.js:424 `tpaApprovedAmount: { type: Dec, default: () => toDec(0) }`. No `min`, unlike tpaPercent at :53 which does carry `{ min: 0, max: 100 }`. So Mongoose will not reject 500000 or -5000. THE CONTRAST IS REAL — tpaSettle at :2255-2258 does exactly the validation tpaApprove lacks (`if (!Number.isFinite(settledAmount) || settledAmount < 0) return 400`). This proves the guard is the codebase's own intended standard, not my invention. APPROVED IS THE SOLE CEILING — billingController.js:2301-2313: `const approved = toN(bill.tpaApprovedAmount) || toN(bill.tpaPayableAmount); const overpay = Math.max(0, settledAmount - approved); if (overpay > 10) throw 400`. With tpaApprovedAmount=500000, settledAmount=500000 → overpay=0 → passes → the TPA_CLAIM row is pushed at :2333-2343 with amount 500000 on a ₹12,000 bill. Nothing between :2313…

findings.title: Generated IRDAI claim form's Part-B bill break-up rows never add up to the printed "TOTAL (Gross)" footer whenever any discount exists
findings.mechanism: claimFormService accumulates each bucket's amount from `toNum(it.netAmount)` (line 107, tax-exclusive AND discount-exclusive per the model's item semantics) while separately accumulating `gross += toNum(it.grossAmount) || amt` (line 111, pre-discount). insurerFormService renders the bucket amounts as the "Amount" column but stamps the footer as `["TOTAL (Gross)", "", inr(t.gross || t.net)]`. The column therefore sums to (gross − discount) while the row labelled TOTAL prints gross. The same mismatch propagates to the overlay path via claimFieldValues' `grossAmount: inr(t.gross || t.net)` (line 315), which the field-map hint list wires to the insurer form's "total bill"/"total amount" boxes (insurerFormTemplateController.js:69).
findings.failureScenario: Admission billed ₹1,00,000 gross with a ₹10,000 line discount and ₹2,000 GST. GET /api/billing/:billId/insurer-form.pdf prints Part-B heads summing to ₹90,000 (Room ₹40,000 + Pharmacy ₹30,000 + Lab ₹20,000) under a footer reading "TOTAL (Gross) Rs. 1,00,000.00", with "Net Payable Rs. 92,000.00" below. The insurer's claim processor sees a break-up that does not reconcile to its own stated total and raises a query/short-pays — exactly the workflow the module exists to avoid.
findings.fixSketch: Print the actual column sum in the footer (`rows.reduce(...)`, i.e. net-of-discount) and label it "TOTAL (Net of Discount, pre-tax)", or add gross/discount/tax as explicit footer rows so gross − discount + tax = net is visible and self-consistent.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED, IMPACT OVERSTATED — downgrade medium -> low. Confirmed at the cited lines: - PatientBillModel.js:558-569: `const gAmt = unit*qty; const dAmt = (gAmt*discountPercent)/100; const nAmt = gAmt - dAmt; ... item.grossAmount = toDec(gAmt); item.netAmount = toDec(nAmt);` -> item.netAmount is indeed discount-exclusive AND tax-exclusive, as the finding asserts. - claimFormService.js:107-111: `const amt = toNum(it.netAmount); buckets[bucket].amount += amt; ... gross += toNum(it.grossAmount) || amt;` -> bucket amounts use the net-of-discount base while `gross` uses the pre-discount base. Two different bases, exactly as claimed. - insurerFormService.js:234-239: rows are `(claim.billBreakup||[]).map(x => [x.name, ..., inr(x.amount)])` and the footer is `{ footer: ["TOTAL (Gross)", "", inr(t.gross || t.net)] }`; table() line 146 `if (footer) drawRow(footer, true)` renders it as a bold row inside the same Amount column. So the column sums to (gross - discount) while the bold row beneath it prints gross. The arithmetic defect is real. In the finding's own scenario the form therefore reads Gross 1,00,000 -> Discount 10,000 -> Tax 2,000 -> Net Payable 92,000, and the column sum 90,000 is exactly gross - discount. gross - discount + t…

findings.dimension: Pharmacy (dispense, GRN, stock, Schedule-X, vendor return)
findings.title: applyAdvanceToSale: per-sale idempotency skip credits the bill without debiting the advance — free money on any 2nd apply
findings.mechanism: The FIFO bleed loop skips an advance when `(adv.appliedTo || []).some(r => String(r.billId) === String(sale._id))` is true, and compensates by doing `need -= slice; continue;` — i.e. it treats the slice as already spent. That check was added (D2-09) to make a retry *inside one request* safe, but it is scoped to `billId` only, so it is equally true on every **subsequent, independent request** against the same sale. The sale-side mutation is computed BEFORE the loop (`newPaid = amountPaid + toApply`, `newBal = bal - toApply`, line 2890-2891) and applied unconditionally after it (2979-2992) — it never consults `need`, `debitedAdvanceIds`, or `advanceSlices`. So when every eligible advance is skipped, `sale.amountPaid` grows and `sale.balanceDue` goes to 0 while `PatientAdvance.appliedAmount` is untouched and the same rupees stay spendable in the pool.
findings.failureScenario: Sale PHM-26-0007, grandTotal ₹1000, balanceDue ₹1000. Patient has one PatientAdvance of ₹1000 (ACTIVE, untagged). (1) POST /api/pharmacy/sales/<id>/apply-advance {amount: 400} → advance debited 400, appliedTo row pushed with billId=<saleId>, balanceDue=₹600. (2) POST the same endpoint again {amount: 600} → `advances` re-reads the same advance (remaining ₹600, status PARTIALLY_APPLIED so still matched by advWhere), `cap=min(600,600)=600`, `toApply=600`; in the loop `alreadyApplied===true` → `need -= 600` → loop exits with zero debits; then `sale.amountPaid = 400+600 = 1000`, `sale.balanceDue = 0`, collectionLog gets a ₹600 mode:"Advance" row with `sourceAdvanceId: null`. Result: ₹600 of pharmacy revenue is booked as collected, the advance still shows ₹600 unspent and can be applied to an IPD bill or refunded to the patient — the hospital is out ₹600 and the Day-Book advance-utilisation total (BillingAudit ADVANCE_APPLIED is emitted per `result.slices`, which is empty) shows nothing. Same path fires for any Supplemented sale whose balance was re-opened by addItems after a first apply. The Idempotency-Key guard on the route does not help — these are two legitimately distinct requests with different keys.
findings.fixSketch: Scope the guard to the current request, not the sale: after the loop assert `need === 0` (or compute `actuallyApplied = toApply - need`) and use `actuallyApplied` for `newPaid`/`newBal`/collectionLog; throw if `actuallyApplied <= 0`. For genuine retry-idempotency, key the appliedTo row on the request's `receiptNumber` (already hoisted in `appliedReceiptNumberHoisted`) rather than `billId`, e.g. `r.receiptNumber === receiptNumber`, and drop the `need -= slice` compensation.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited line matches the real code and the full chain reproduces. (1) Guards pass on a 2nd request: pharmacyController.js:2812-2820 only rejects non-Completed/Partial-Return/Supplemented status and balanceDue<=0. After a partial 1st apply (bal ₹600) both pass. (2) The advance is re-matched — the finding's weakest link, and it holds. advWhere (2853-2861) filters status:{$in:["ACTIVE","PARTIALLY_APPLIED"]}; PatientAdvanceModel.js:256-258 is the pre-save hook writing exactly `this.status = "PARTIALLY_APPLIED"` when 0<applied<total (enum at :116 confirms the string). So the ₹1000 advance at applied=400 is re-read with remainingByAdv=600, totalAvailable=600 → NO_ADVANCE (2873) does not fire; cap=min(600,600)=600, toApply=600 (2880-2881). (3) The skip fires across requests: 2936-2942 tests `String(r.billId) === String(sale._id)` against the appliedTo row pushed by request 1 at 2962-2970 (`billId: sale._id`). The predicate is scoped to the SALE, not the request, so it is equally true on an independent 2nd request. `need -= slice; continue;` consumes ₹600 with zero debits; debitedAdvanceIds and advanceSlices stay empty. (4) Sale mutation is unconditional and never consults the loop: newPaid/newBal computed from toApply at 2890-2891 BEFORE the loop, written at 2979-2980 after it. amountPaid 400→1000, balanceDue→0, saved at 3007. collectionLog row (2982-2992) books ₹600 mode:"Advance" with sourceAdvanceId: lastAdvId = null (lastAdvId only assigned at 2974 inside the debit branch).…

findings.dimension: Pharmacy (dispense, GRN, stock, Schedule-X, vendor return)
findings.title: Schedule-X DAY_LOCKED guard queries VERIFY rows by batchId, but verifyBalance writes VERIFY rows without one — post-verify narcotic dispenses are never blocked
findings.mechanism: `recordDispense` looks for the day lock with `ScheduleXEntry.findOne({ drugId, batchId: batchId || null, date: today, rowType: "VERIFY" })`. `verifyBalance` creates its VERIFY rows from the `dailyBalance` per-drug summary and never sets `batchId` (ScheduleXEntryModel.js:39 leaves it optional/undefined). The counter path always supplies a real batchId (pharmacyController.js:1215 `batchId: u.batch._id` → 1442-1443), so the filter becomes `{batchId: ObjectId(...)}` and can never match the batch-less VERIFY row. `{batchId: null}` would match a missing field, but that branch only runs for a batch-less dispense, which the sale flow never produces. The lock is therefore dead code for every real dispense.
findings.failureScenario: Pharmacist verifies the day at 20:00 → one VERIFY row per Schedule-X drug is written with `closingBalance: 8`, `balanceVerifiedBy/At` stamped, and the day is legally signed. At 21:30 a night dispense of Morphine 2 units from batch B posts to POST /api/pharmacy/sales. The DAY_LOCKED check misses the VERIFY row, the CAS decrements ScheduleXBalance to 6, and a new DISPENSE row is appended into the already-signed day. `dailyBalance(today)` now reports dispensed=+2 and closing=6 while the signed VERIFY row on the same day still says closing=8 — the printed NDPS register self-contradicts. `verifyBalance` cannot be re-run (line 319-323 throws ALREADY_VERIFIED), so the new row can never be verified. On an NDPS inspection the register shows an unverified, post-signature narcotic movement.
findings.fixSketch: Make the guard batch-agnostic (the lock is per-day-per-drug, matching how verifyBalance writes): `ScheduleXEntry.findOne({ drugId, date: today, rowType: "VERIFY" })`. Optionally add a partial unique index on `{date, drugId, rowType:"VERIFY"}` so the lock is enforced at the storage layer too.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link in the claimed mechanism is present in the actual code. 1. GUARD — scheduleXRegister.js:100-102: `const verified = await ScheduleXEntry.findOne({ drugId, batchId: batchId || null, date: today, rowType: "VERIFY" }).lean();` → DAY_LOCKED 409 at :103-106. 2. VERIFY ROW CREATION — scheduleXRegister.js:332-344: `ScheduleXEntry.create({ date: day, drugId: s.drugId, drugName: s.drugName, openingBalance, received, dispensed, closingBalance, rowType: "VERIFY", balanceVerifiedBy/ById/At, remarks })`. There is NO batchId key. It is built by iterating `summary` from `dailyBalance()` (:325), whose per-drug group object (:267-272) is `{drugId, drugName, opening, received, dispensed, closing, rows, verified...}` — it has no batch dimension at all, so batch info isn't even available to write. Confirms the auditor's claim. 3. MODEL — ScheduleXEntryModel.js:39: `batchId: { type: Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: "PharmacyDrugBatch" }` — optional, no default. VERIFY docs therefore have the field ABSENT. A Mongo filter `{batchId: ObjectId("...")}` cannot match a doc missing the field. `{batchId: null}` WOULD match (null matches missing), but that branch only runs when batchId is falsy. 4. CALLERS ALWAYS PASS A REAL batchId, so the null branch is never taken on live paths:    - pharmacyController.js:1200/1215 — fifoConsume result `u.batch._id` pushed as `batchId: u.batch._id` into scheduleXItems; forwarded at :1441-1443 `batchId: sx.batchId`.    - autoBillingService.js:3479-3481 (IPD indent…

findings.dimension: Pharmacy (dispense, GRN, stock, Schedule-X, vendor return)
findings.title: applyAdvanceToSale transaction 'capability probe' always reports replica-set — endpoint 500s on every call against standalone Mongo
findings.mechanism: The D2-09 rewrite detects transaction support by `session.startTransaction(); await session.abortTransaction(); useTx = true;` inside a try. Both calls are purely client-side in the MongoDB Node driver: `startTransaction()` only mutates local session state, and `abortTransaction()` on a session in STARTING_TRANSACTION (no operations issued) short-circuits and returns without sending an `abortTransaction` command. Neither ever contacts the server, so neither can throw the 'Transaction numbers are only allowed on a replica set member or mongos' error the comment expects. `useTx` is therefore unconditionally `true`, and the `retryVersionError` standalone fallback at 3054 — plus all of the standalone-only compensation logic at 3009-3037 — is unreachable. Every other service in this codebase still uses the option-based check that correctly returns false on standalone (patientAdvanceService.js:175-176, admissionService.js:143-144, mlcService.js:171-172), so pharmacy is the lone outlier.
findings.failureScenario: Deployment runs a single-node mongod (the per-hospital Docker deploy / dev box — no `replSet` config exists anywhere in the repo). Pharmacist clicks 'Apply Advance' on an IPD credit sale. The probe sets useTx=true; `session.withTransaction()` runs `doApply(session)`; the first session-bound write (`Counter.findOneAndUpdate({_id:'pharmacyCreditCollection'}, ..., {session})` at 2904-2908) throws MongoServerError code 20 — not a TransientTransactionError, so withTransaction does not retry — and it propagates to the outer catch, returning 500 `{code: "APPLY_ADVANCE_FAILED"}`. Advance can never be applied to a pharmacy bill on that deployment, which in turn deadlocks the IPD discharge pharmacy-clearance gate (getOutstandingForAdmission) for any patient whose only funds are in the advance pool.
findings.fixSketch: Probe the topology instead of the client-side session state — e.g. `const topo = mongoose.connection.client?.topology?.description?.type; useTx = topo === 'ReplicaSetWithPrimary' || topo === 'Sharded';` — or simply reuse the existing option-based helper the rest of the codebase uses (patientAdvanceService.js:175-176). Alternatively make the probe real by issuing a trivial session-bound read/write inside the try before aborting.
findings.verifyEvidence: CONFIRMED against installed mongodb@6.20.0 (Backend/node_modules/mongodb/package.json:3) — the probe is provably inert. 1. PROBE IS CLIENT-SIDE ONLY (pharmacyController.js:2784-2796):    - sessions.js:228-255 `startTransaction()` checks only `snapshotEnabled` and `inTransaction()`, then mutates local state (incrementTransactionNumber, new Transaction, transition(STARTING_TRANSACTION)). NO topology check, NO server round-trip. Modern drivers deliberately removed the standalone check here.    - sessions.js:362-370 `abortTransaction()` short-circuits exactly as the finding claims: `if (this.transaction.state === TxnState.STARTING_TRANSACTION) { this.transaction.transition(TxnState.TRANSACTION_ABORTED); return; }` — returns BEFORE `const command = { abortTransaction: 1 }` at line 378. No command is sent.    - Checked the one escape hatch that would refute the finding: transactions.js:20-25 stateMachine lists TRANSACTION_ABORTED as a LEGAL successor of STARTING_TRANSACTION, so transition() does not throw MongoRuntimeError. Nothing inside the try can throw => `useTx` is unconditionally true whenever the session opens. 2. STANDALONE IS THE SHIPPED TOPOLOGY: docker-compose.yml:17-18 runs `image: mongo:7` with no `command:` / no `--replSet` (bare standalone). Backend/.env.production.example:34 only documents replicaSet as an opt-in the per-hospital deploy does not take. 3. PHARMACY IS THE LONE OUTLIER (confirmed): patientAdvanceService.js:173-177 (actual path services/Billing/, not s…

findings.dimension: Pharmacy (dispense, GRN, stock, Schedule-X, vendor return)
findings.title: #28 Schedule-X reversals move the balance but append no register row — ScheduleXBalance and the statutory NDPS register permanently diverge
findings.mechanism: `recordReversal` only `$inc`s `ScheduleXBalance.balance` and explicitly does not append a `ScheduleXEntry` row (header comment 220-223: 'the caller owns the paper trail'). But the two stores are read by different code with different derivations: `recordDispense` stamps `openingBalance`/`closingBalance` from the live `ScheduleXBalance` doc (140-141), while `dailyBalance` recomputes `closing = opening + received - dispensed` purely from `ScheduleXEntry` rows (299), with `opening` backfilled from the previous row's `closingBalance` (292-299). A reversal changes the first and not the second, so from the first return/cancel/vendor-return onward the printed register's arithmetic no longer matches the balance the next dispense will print.
findings.failureScenario: Morphine balance 10 (register rows agree). Sale S1 dispenses 4 → register row prints opening 10 / dispensed 4 / closing 6; balance doc = 6. Pharmacist processes a full return of S1 → returnItems reverses `scheduleXRegisteredQty=4` (3425-3430), balance doc = 10, PHARMACY_RETURNED is a ClinicalAudit row, but no ScheduleXEntry is written. `dailyBalance(today)` still reports dispensed=4, closing=6 — the NDPS register shows 4 units dispensed to a patient that were physically returned to the cabinet, and no 'received back' line. The next dispense of 3 units then prints openingBalance=10 / closingBalance=7 (read from the balance doc), while the previous row on the same page closed at 6 — an unexplained +4 jump. The same divergence occurs in the opposite direction for a Schedule-X vendor return (4282-4287), which silently removes narcotics from the cabinet with no register row at all.
findings.fixSketch: Have `recordReversal` append a `ScheduleXEntry` row inside the same call — `rowType: "RECEIVE"` with `received: |delta|` for credit-backs, and a `DISPENSE`-class row (or a new `ADJUST`/`VENDOR_RETURN` rowType on the enum) with the vendor as the recipient for decrements — stamping opening/closing from the returned `balanceDoc`, with the same create-failure compensation `recordDispense` uses (162-177).
findings.verifyEvidence: CONFIRMED. Read scheduleXRegister.js in full, all 3 call sites, the model enum, and the register read path. 1) recordReversal (scheduleXRegister.js:234-246) performs ONLY `ScheduleXBalance.findOneAndUpdate({drugId, [balance:{$gte:-delta}] }, {$inc:{balance: delta}, $set:{lastUpdatedBy/ById/At}}, {new:true})` and returns `updated?.toObject() ?? null`. No ScheduleXEntry.create. The header comment 220-223 explicitly states the omission is by design ("the caller owns the paper trail"). 2) No caller compensates. Grep of `ScheduleXEntry` across controllers/Pharmacy/pharmacyController.js returns ONLY comment mentions (lines 557, 1449) — zero ScheduleXEntry.create calls in the controller. All three reversal call sites only console.error on a null return:    - 3425-3437 (returnItems): recordReversal({signedQty: reverseQty}) then `if (res===null) console.error(...); continue;`    - 4035-4044 (cancelSale): recordReversal({signedQty: reg}) then console.error on null    - 4282-4291 (recordVendorReturn): recordReversal({signedQty: -qtyN}) then console.error on null    The "paper trail" the comment defers to is ClinicalAudit / PharmacyVendorReturn — a DIFFERENT store from the statutory ScheduleXEntry register. 3) The two stores are independently derived, exactly as the finding claims:    - recordDispense stamps row metadata from the live balance doc: line 140-141 `const closing = Number(balanceDoc.balance||0); const opening = closing + n;`    - dailyBalance recomputes from ScheduleXEntry r…

findings.dimension: Pharmacy (dispense, GRN, stock, Schedule-X, vendor return)
findings.title: Schedule-X register failure at dispense only writes a remark — narcotics leave the shelf with no NDPS register row and the sale still completes
findings.mechanism: `fifoConsume` decrements DrugBatch.remaining and `Sale.create` commits before the register is touched. If `scheduleXRegister.recordDispense` throws for any reason other than the pre-gated witness checks — DAY_LOCKED, INSUFFICIENT_REGISTER_BALANCE (balance doc drifted below physical stock), a Drug-master `schedule` read blip, a transient Mongo error — the catch at 1467 logs, appends `Schedule-X register pending: ...` to `sale.remarks`, and lets the 201 succeed. The R7hr-239 pre-gate at 1010-1034 only covers `witnessName`/`witnessId`/`WITNESS_SELF`; it does not pre-validate register balance or the day lock, both of which are exactly the conditions that reject downstream. `scheduleXRegisteredQty` is then left at 0 for that line, so a later return also correctly refuses to credit the register — the physical stock and the register stay divergent with no reconciliation endpoint.
findings.failureScenario: A GRN of Pethidine was recorded before the D3-06 hardening (or the balance doc was hand-repaired short), leaving ScheduleXBalance.balance = 2 while DrugBatch.remaining = 20. Pharmacist dispenses 5 with a valid witness. Pre-gate passes (witness present, not self). fifoConsume decrements remaining to 15, Sale.create commits, then `recordDispense`'s CAS `{drugId, balance: {$gte: 5}}` returns null → INSUFFICIENT_REGISTER_BALANCE → swallowed. The counter prints the bill, 5 ampoules of a Schedule-X narcotic leave the cabinet, and the statutory register has zero rows for the movement. The only trace is a remark string on the sale doc and a console.error line — nothing surfaces in the 201 response, so the pharmacist has no signal to stop.
findings.fixSketch: Either (a) pre-flight the register — reserve the Schedule-X balance via the CAS BEFORE fifoConsume/Sale.create and roll it back on any later failure, so a register rejection blocks the dispense the way GRN's D3-06 fix blocks receipt; or (b) at minimum, surface the failure in the response (`warning`/`code: SCHEDULE_X_REGISTER_PENDING`) and add an Admin reconcile endpoint, so the divergence is visible and repairable rather than log-only.
findings.verifyEvidence: Mechanism confirmed in full at the cited lines. pharmacyController.js:1198 fifoConsume decrements DrugBatch.remaining and Sale.create (~1421) commits BEFORE the register is touched. The register loop at 1430-1495 wraps scheduleXRegister.recordDispense in a per-item try; the catch at 1467-1474 only console.errors, appends "Schedule-X register pending: ..." to sale.remarks, and falls through — the 201 at 1583 (`res.status(201).json({ success: true, data: sale })`) carries no warning/code. registeredByDrug never receives the key, so item.scheduleXRegisteredQty stays 0 and the R8-#28 reversal caps at 3415/4027 correctly refuse to credit the register on a later return — the divergence is permanent. The pre-gate at 1010-1034 checks only witnessName/witnessId/WITNESS_SELF; it does NOT pre-validate register balance. scheduleXRegister.js:115-135 is the CAS `{drugId, balance:{$gte:n}}` → null → INSUFFICIENT_REGISTER_BALANCE (409) thrown straight into the swallowing catch. No reconcile endpoint exists — confirmed by the codebase's own D3-06 comment at pharmacyController.js:559-561 ("no admin endpoint bumps balance directly").
