Reachability is in fact BROADER than the finding's scenario: recordReceipt (scheduleXRegister.js:191-202) is the sole upsert of the balance doc and fires from GRN only when `drug.schedule === "X"` at GRN time (pharmacyController.js:566). A drug reclassified to Schedule X in the Drug master AFTER stock was received therefore has NO ScheduleXBalance doc → every disp…

findings.title: Lab critical-value auto-alert never fires: no caller ever supplies criticalLow/criticalHigh and no reference-range master data exists
findings.mechanism: `enterResults` only pushes to `criticalHits` when `_classifyResult` returns `critical:true`, which requires a *numeric* `r.criticalLow`/`r.criticalHigh` on the posted result row (`fin = (x) => typeof x === 'number' && Number.isFinite(x)`, L15). Two supply paths exist and both are dead: (a) the client payload — `Frontend/src/Components/Investigation/InvestigationOrders.jsx:499-511` builds each result row as only `{parameterName, value, unit, normalRange, isAbnormal:false}`; there is no refLow/refHigh/criticalLow/criticalHigh input in the dialog at all; (b) the master fallback at L369-379 calls `InvestigationMaster.resolveReferenceRange(_master, ...)`, which returns null unless `InvestigationMaster.parameters[].referenceRanges[]` is populated — grepping the whole repo, `referenceRanges` occurs ONLY in `models/Investigation/InvestigationMasterModel.js` and this service: no seeder, no migration, no admin UI, no route body that a user could author it through. With no bounds, `_classifyResult` falls to L26-29 and returns `flag:""`/`critical:false` (the UI hardcodes `isAbnormal:false`). So `criticalHits` is always empty, the `if (criticalHits.length)` block at L423 never executes, and `criticalValueAlerter.emit` is never called for any lab result. The server-side H/L flagging advertised in the model comment (InvestigationOrderModel.js:11-21) is equally inert — `flag` is always "".
findings.failureScenario: Lab tech charts Potassium = 7.4 mmol/L on an INV order through the Investigation Orders result dialog. Payload = {parameterName:'Potassium', value:'7.4', unit:'mmol/L', normalRange:'', isAbnormal:false}. `_classifyResult` sees no numeric criticalHigh and no master range → returns flag:"", critical:false. No CriticalValueAlert row is created, no OPEN alert lands on the nursing queue, no 10-min PANIC SLA clock starts, no escalation. The result saves as an ordinary normal value and the ward is never notified. NABH IPSG.2 / NABL-ISO 15189 7.4.1(h) critical-value loop is non-functional in production despite the code appearing to implement it.
findings.fixSketch: Ship reference/critical ranges as real data: seed `InvestigationMaster.parameters[].referenceRanges[]` for the common analytes (K, Na, Glucose, Hb, Platelets, INR, Creatinine, Troponin) and add an admin authoring UI; add refLow/refHigh/criticalLow/criticalHigh inputs to the result-entry dialog; coerce incoming bounds with Number() before `_classifyResult` (a form posts strings, which `fin()` silently rejects); and add a startup/CI assertion that at least the panic-analyte masters carry criticalLow/criticalHigh.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE MECHANISM CONFIRMED. (1) investigationOrderService.js:12-30 — _classifyResult returns critical:true only at L21-22, gated on fin() at L15 (`typeof x === "number" && Number.isFinite(x)`); absent numeric criticalLow/criticalHigh it falls through to L26-29 returning flag:"" / critical:false. (2) Client path dead: Frontend/src/Components/Investigation/InvestigationOrders.jsx:500-509 and 594-600 construct result rows as exactly {parameterName, value, unit, normalRange, isAbnormal}; the dialog inputs (L1758-1801) expose only those five fields — no refLow/refHigh/criticalLow/criticalHigh. It is the ONLY caller of enter-results (L521); no other frontend surface posts bounds. (3) Master fallback unpopulated: InvestigationMasterModel.js:177-213 resolveReferenceRange returns null at L182 unless parameters[].referenceRanges[] is non-empty; repo-wide grep for `referenceRanges` hits ONLY InvestigationMasterModel.js, investigationOrderService.js, and 3 markdown docs (TASK-LOG, AUDIT-R8-20DIM, NABH-NABL-COMPLIANCE-REVIEW). Backend/scripts grep for InvestigationMaster returns only wipePatientData.js, resetPatientData.js, fix-duplicate-indexes-v2.js — no seeder. Result: criticalHits (L386) is always empty, the `if (criticalHits.length)` block at L423 never executes, and criticalValueAlerter.emit (L427) never fires from lab result entry. Out of the box the lab auto-alert is inert. TWO SUB-CLAIMS REFUTED. (a) "no route body that a user could author it through" is FALSE: investigationMaster…

findings.title: Entire Investigation Orders lab UI calls the API with raw fetch() and no Authorization header — every call 401s behind the global JWT wall
findings.mechanism: Every backend call on this page uses `fetch(...)` with at most `headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }` — no `Authorization: Bearer`. `Backend/routes/index.js:126` mounts `authenticate` globally and `/investigation-orders` is mounted below it at L233. `Backend/middleware/auth.js:36-56` reads the token ONLY from the `Authorization: Bearer` header (or `?token=` on whitelisted SSE prefixes, which this is not) and returns `401 {code:'NO_TOKEN'}` otherwise. There is no global fetch monkey-patch anywhere in Frontend/src (only axios has the Bearer interceptor), and `config/api.js` exports a bare URL string with no wrapper. The file's own comment at L182-185 states it outright: "The page's own fetch() calls carry no auth header, so this uses axios (global Bearer interceptor)" — the TAT tile was moved to axios for exactly this reason, but the 11 other call sites were left on fetch.
findings.failureScenario: Lab Technician opens Lab → Investigation Orders. `loadOrders()` (L248) GETs /investigation-orders with no Bearer → 401 NO_TOKEN → the worklist renders empty. Clicking Verify (L447) POSTs {verifiedBy:'Pathologist'} with no Bearer → 401 → toast 'Authentication required. Please login.' Sample collection, result entry, external-result attach, cancel and add-test are all unusable from this page. The 401 also carries a HARD_LOGOUT code, so the axios interceptor may punt the user to /login mid-workflow.
findings.fixSketch: Replace every raw `fetch` on this page with the interceptor-authed `axios` instance already imported at L3 (or route through a service module), then add an ESLint rule banning bare `fetch(${API}...)` in Frontend/src so this cannot regress.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four refutation avenues failed; the defect is real as described. 1) NO AUTH HEADER — `rg -n "Authorization" Frontend/src/Components/Investigation/InvestigationOrders.jsx` returns ZERO matches. All 11 cited call sites use raw `fetch()` with at most `headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }`. Verified verbatim: - L248 `const res = await fetch(`${API}/investigation-orders?${params}`);` (no options object at all) - L261 `fetch(`${API}/investigation-orders/summary`)` - L269 `fetch(`${API}/investigations?limit=300&isActive=true`)` - L379-382 POST create — `headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }` only - L407 `fetch(`${API}/investigation-orders/${id}`)` - L422-428 collect-sample POST — Content-Type only - L447-451 verify POST — `headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ verifiedBy: "Pathologist" })` - L469-472 cancel, L520-524 enter-results, L566-570 enter-external-result, L1483-1487 print — all Content-Type only. 2) NO GLOBAL FETCH PATCH — rg over all of Frontend/src for `window.fetch|globalThis.fetch|originalFetch|interceptFetch` yields only 4 false positives (local identifiers: AdminHome.jsx:77 `const fetch = async () =>`, NursingEquipmentPage.jsx:134 `refetch`, RoomChargesPage.jsx:143 `refetch`, useHospitalSettings.js:111 `_invalidateAndRefetch`). Frontend/src/config/api.js:16 exports `export const API_BASE_URL = RAW_API_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:5050/api";` — a bare string, no wrapper/client. 3) NO COOKIE FALLBACK IN AUTH —…

findings.title: Westgard/QC release gate on investigation orders is unreachable — `analyser` is never populated, and the LabTrend gate matches analysers by unvalidated free text
findings.mechanism: The gate at L490-494 only inspects items where `i.analyser` is truthy. `item.analyser` is written in exactly one place — L350, `if (analyser) item.analyser = String(analyser).trim()`, sourced from the `enterResults` request body. The only caller, `Frontend/src/Components/Investigation/InvestigationOrders.jsx:526-530`, posts `{itemId, results, interpretation}` and never sends `analyser` (there is no analyser field in the result dialog). So `analysers` is always `[]`, `if (analysers.length)` at L495 is always false, and `verifyResults` releases every result without ever reading LabQCLog. The parallel LabTrend gate (`controllers/Clinical/labRecordsController.js:247-259`) does fire, but matches `LabTrend.equipmentId` (a free-text String, labRecordsModels.js:74) against `LabQCLog.equipmentName` (free text, LabQCLogModel.js:33) via a case-insensitive anchored regex with zero referential integrity — no equipment master, no enum, no FK. Separately, `LabQCLog.result` is a client-declared PASS/FAIL (`qcCreate`, labRecordsController.js:422-425): nothing evaluates `actualValue` against `expectedRangeLow/High`, and `westgardRule` is free text evaluated nowhere in the repo (grep: `westgard` appears only in the model file and docs). There is no Westgard rule engine.
findings.failureScenario: Bio-Rad control on the AU480 chemistry analyser fails 1-3s; the tech honestly logs POST /api/lab-records/qc {equipmentName:'AU480', result:'FAIL'}. Ten minutes later the same tech enters and a doctor verifies 40 patient biochem results on that run via the Investigation Orders page. `verifyResults` computes `analysers = []` (no item carries an analyser), skips the LabQCLog lookup entirely, and flips all 40 items to VERIFIED — the reports dispatch. Even on the LabTrend path, a sheet typed as 'AU-480' or 'AU480 ' instead of 'AU480' finds no QC row and releases silently.
findings.fixSketch: Make the analyser a first-class required field on internal result entry (dropdown bound to an Equipment master, not free text), reject verify when a COMPLETED internal item has no analyser, resolve QC rows by equipmentId ObjectId rather than a name regex, and compute PASS/FAIL server-side in `qcCreate` from actualValue vs expectedRange with real Westgard rules (1-3s, 2-2s, R-4s, 4-1s, 10x) instead of trusting the client's flag.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link in the claimed chain verified against actual code. 1) SOLE PRODUCER — investigationOrderService.js:340 destructures `analyser` from each itemResults[] entry; L350 `if (analyser) item.analyser = String(analyser).trim();` is the ONLY write to item.analyser in the repo. Repo-wide grep for `item.analyser|analyser:|analyserCalibratedOn` returns exactly 5 hits: service L340/350/351 and model InvestigationOrderModel.js:95-96 (schema defaults `analyser: {type:String, default:""}`). No route, importer, HL7/analyser interface, or job writes it. 2) SOLE CALLER OMITS IT — Frontend/src/Components/Investigation/InvestigationOrders.jsx:520-532 POSTs `{itemResults: resultForms.map(f => ({itemId, results, interpretation})), enteredBy}`. `openResultEntry` (L491-511) builds each form with only {itemId,name,results,interpretation} — no analyser key, no analyser input in the dialog. Backend/scripts/_e2e_opd.js:108 also omits it, so the 136/136 E2E suite never exercises the gate. 3) GATE IS DEAD — service L490-494 filters `i.analyser && i.resultStatus === "COMPLETED"`; with analyser always "" (falsy) `analysers` is always []. `if (analysers.length)` at L495 is false, so LabQCLog is never required (L496) or queried (L499), and L508-514 flips every COMPLETED item to VERIFIED unconditionally. Confirmed: verifyResults never reads QC state on the InvestigationOrder path. 4) LabTrend GATE — labRecordsController.js:247-258 confirmed as described: `LabTrend.equipmentId` (free-text String, labR…

findings.title: Lab result-entry / verification / amendment / rejection identity is taken from the request body, not req.user — the NABL authorising signatory is a client-controlled string
findings.mechanism: `investigationOrderService` never reads `req.user`. `enterResults({enteredBy})`, `verifyResults({verifiedBy})`, `amendResult({amendedBy})` and `rejectSample({rejectedBy})` all take the actor name straight from the HTTP body (controller passes `req.body` verbatim: investigationOrderController.js:84, 95, 105, 114, 144) and stamp it onto `item.resultEnteredBy` / `item.verifiedBy` / `amendments[].amendedBy` / `item.rejectedBy` and `order.actionLog[].performedBy`. No user id is stored on the item at all. The sibling module does this correctly — `labRecordsController.js:262-263` and `:331` stamp `verifiedBy: req.user?.id` + `verifiedByName: await resolveUserName(req)` — so the investigation-order path is an unfixed inconsistency, not a design choice. Worse, the only client hardcodes constants: `InvestigationOrders.jsx:450` sends `{verifiedBy: 'Pathologist'}` and `:531`/`:573` send `{enteredBy: 'Lab Technician'}` / `{enteredBy:'Staff'}`, and `:136` prints that literal on the report as `Verified by: Pathologist`.
findings.failureScenario: Any account holding `lab.verify` (Admin/Doctor/Radiologist, permissions.js:215) POSTs /investigation-orders/:id/verify with body {verifiedBy: 'Dr. R. Sharma, MD Pathology'}. The report is released and permanently attributed to Dr. Sharma, who never saw it, with no user-id anywhere to contradict it. Conversely, in normal use every single released report in the database is signed by the literal string 'Pathologist' — so when a wrong result reaches a patient, there is no record of who authorised release. Same for amendments: `amendResult` writes `amendments[].amendedBy = amendedBy || 'Pathologist'` (L297), defeating the append-only trail's purpose (ISO 15189 7.4.1.7 requires identification of the person authorising release/amendment).
findings.fixSketch: Derive the actor server-side in the controller: pass `{...req.body, actor: {id: req.user.id, name: await resolveUserName(req), role: req.user.role}}` and ignore any body-supplied *By field; add `verifiedById`/`resultEnteredById`/`amendedById` ObjectId refs to the item + amendment schemas and stamp them; drop the hardcoded strings from the frontend.
findings.verifyEvidence: All cited mechanisms verified in actual code; only a cosmetic path error in the finding (controller is at Backend/controllers/Investigation/investigationOrderController.js, not Backend/investigationOrderController.js; line numbers shift ~10). SERVICE (Backend/services/Investigation/investigationOrderService.js) — every cited line confirmed: - L313 `async enterResults(orderId, { itemResults = [], enteredBy })` -> L392 `item.resultEnteredBy = enteredBy || "Lab Technician"`; L413 actionLog `performedBy: enteredBy || "Lab Technician"`. - L482 `async verifyResults(orderId, { verifiedBy, itemIds = null })` -> L512 `item.verifiedBy = verifiedBy` (NO fallback — omitting verifiedBy stamps undefined on a RELEASED report while L532 actionLog logs literal "Pathologist"); L513 verifiedAt. - L279 `async amendResult(orderId, { itemId, amendments = [], amendedBy })` -> L297 `amendments.push({... amendedBy: amendedBy || "Pathologist" ...})`; L302 re-attestation `item.verifiedBy = amendedBy || item.verifiedBy`; L304 actionLog `performedBy: amendedBy || "Pathologist"`. - L237 `async rejectSample(orderId, { itemIds, rejectionReason, rejectedBy })` -> L255 `item.rejectedBy = rejectedBy || "Lab Staff"`; L262 actionLog; L270 `order.__forceAdminUserId = rejectedBy || "Lab Staff"` (client string even drives the force-transition audit). The service never receives `req` at all — grep confirms zero `req.user` reads in the file. CONTROLLER (controllers/Investigation/investigationOrderController.js) — pa…

findings.title: LabQCLog mass-assignment lets any lab.records.write user set retainUntil, weaponising the TTL index to auto-delete QC evidence
findings.mechanism: `qcCreate` does `const body = req.body || {}` then `LabQCLog.create(body)` (L418, L430), overriding only `result`, `performedBy`, `performedById`, `performedByRole` and `performedAt`. Every other schema path — including `retainUntil` — is taken verbatim from the client. `LabQCLogModel.js:81-84` defaults `retainUntil` to now+3y precisely to satisfy the NABH AAC.3 / ISO 15189 two-year QC retention floor, and L95 puts a TTL index on it (`{retainUntil: 1}, {expireAfterSeconds: 0}`), so Mongo's TTL monitor physically deletes any document whose retainUntil is in the past, within ~60s, with no audit row and no soft-delete. There is no allow-list/pick and no schema-level protection on the field.
findings.failureScenario: A lab technician (role has `lab.records.write`, permissions.js:229) POSTs /api/lab-records/qc {equipmentName:'AU480', result:'FAIL', analyte:'Glucose', retainUntil:'2020-01-01'}. It returns 201 and shows as a compliant QC log; within a minute Mongo's TTL sweep permanently deletes the row. The FAIL vanishes from `qcList`, from the NABL auditor's 2-year QC history, and from the `LabTrend` release gate's `findOne(...).sort({performedAt:-1})` — so the very next verify on that analyser is no longer blocked. A tech can systematically make every failing control self-destruct while the PASS rows persist.
findings.fixSketch: Whitelist the writable fields in `qcCreate` (pick equipmentId/equipmentName/controlSampleLot/controlLevel/analyte/expected*/unit/actualValue/westgardRule/correctiveAction/shift/notes) and always compute `retainUntil` server-side; same treatment for `performedAt` (clamp back-dating to a small window). Consider dropping the TTL index in favour of an archival job so no client-settable path can trigger a hard delete of compliance evidence.
findings.verifyEvidence: All four legs verified in actual code. (1) labRecordsController.js:418-430: `const body = req.body || {}` then `LabQCLog.create(body)`; only result/performedBy/performedById/performedByRole/performedAt are overridden — no pick/allow-list, so `retainUntil` is taken verbatim from the client. (2) LabQCLogModel.js:81-84: `retainUntil` defaults to now+3y, is NOT `immutable`, and has no pre-save clamp. (3) LabQCLogModel.js:95: `LabQCLogSchema.index({ retainUntil: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 0 })` is a real TTL index → a past date causes physical deletion within ~60s with no audit row and no soft-delete. There is no DELETE route on the QC collection, so the TTL is the ONLY deletion primitive and mass-assignment hands it to the client. (4) labRecordsRoutes.js:27 `router.post("/qc", requireAction("lab.records.write"), ctrl.qcCreate)` with permissions.js:229 `"lab.records.write": ["Admin","Lab Technician","Radiologist"]` — no sanitizer middleware on the route and no global body-key stripping. Refutation attempts failed: searched for a global mongo-sanitize/body sanitizer, `immutable: true`, and pre-save clamps — none apply. Notably BillingAudit.js:252-264 contains exactly the defensive pre-save retainUntil clamp this model lacks ("Only adjust retainUntil for new docs that didn't get an explicit..."), so the codebase already recognizes the hazard; LabQCLog is the inconsistent outlier. Release gates are real and read as claimed: labRecordsController.js:251-253 and investigationOrderServi…

findings.title: Rejecting a sample after results are drafted reopens the item but leaves the order stuck in terminal COMPLETED — it vanishes from the pending worklist and can no longer be cancelled or extended
findings.mechanism: `enterResults` L401-406 flips `order.orderStatus = 'COMPLETED'` once every item is COMPLETED/VERIFIED. `rejectSample` (the R8 #23 path) then reopens items with `item.sampleStatus='REJECTED'; item.resultStatus='PENDING'` (L253-257) and force-bypasses the LabResult matrix (L269-270), but never touches `order.orderStatus`. `statusTransitionGuard.js:142` declares `InvestigationOrder.COMPLETED: []` — terminal. Nothing can move the header back: on recollection `collectSamples` L216 only advances `PENDING → SAMPLE_COLLECTED` (the R8 #12 fix), and re-entering results hits `allItemsDone` false → the `else if` at L407 only fires for PENDING/SAMPLE_COLLECTED, so COMPLETED sticks. `rejectSample` also leaves `item.results[]` (the values from the compromised specimen) in place and, on recollection, `collectSamples` L202 keeps the old `accessionNumber` (`if (!item.accessionNumber)`) and never clears `rejectionReason/rejectedBy/rejectedAt`.
findings.failureScenario: Single-test order INV-20260717-0012 (Serum Potassium). Tech enters the result → order becomes COMPLETED. Pathologist spots gross haemolysis before verifying and POSTs /reject-sample {rejectionReason:'Hemolysed'}. The item drops to sampleStatus=REJECTED/resultStatus=PENDING but the header still reads COMPLETED: the order does not appear under the ?status=SAMPLE_COLLECTED or ?status=PENDING worklist filters (InvestigationOrders.jsx:195-205 deep-links), `getDashboardSummary` counts it as completed, `cancelOrder` refuses with 'Cannot cancel completed order' (L565-566) and `addTest` refuses with 'Cannot add test to completed or cancelled order' (L627-628). The order is orphaned — the recollection is only ever found by someone who knows the order number. If it is silently reprinted before recollection, `item.results[]` still holds the haemolysed potassium value.
findings.fixSketch: In `rejectSample`, after reopening items, recompute the header (if any item is back to PENDING, force the header down to SAMPLE_COLLECTED/PENDING using the same audited `__forceTransition` already set at L269), clear `item.results = []`, and clear/roll the accession + rejection stamps on recollection in `collectSamples` (NABL 7.2.5 wants a fresh accession for a fresh specimen). Add an E2E case: enter results → reject → assert the order is back on the pending worklist and re-cancellable.
findings.verifyEvidence: Core defect verified in the real code. investigationOrderService.js:237-273 (rejectSample) mutates only item fields (L253-257: sampleStatus='REJECTED', rejectionReason/rejectedBy/rejectedAt, resultStatus='PENDING') and sets order.__forceTransition=true (L269-270), but never assigns order.orderStatus. enterResults L404-406 sets order.orderStatus='COMPLETED' when allItemsDone. statusTransitionGuard is at Backend/utils/statusTransitionGuard.js (NOT middleware/ as cited) and L138-144 declares InvestigationOrder { PENDING:[...], SAMPLE_COLLECTED:[...], IN_PROGRESS:['COMPLETED','CANCELLED'], COMPLETED:[], CANCELLED:[] } — COMPLETED terminal; enforced via the model pre-save at InvestigationOrderModel.js:370-372. A grep of every `orderStatus =` write in the Investigation module confirms NO path ever moves the header below COMPLETED: collectSamples L216-218 only fires on PENDING (R8 #12 fix), enterResults L407-408 and verifyResults L526-528 only reach IN_PROGRESS from non-COMPLETED states. Consequences verified: cancelOrder L565-566 ("Cannot cancel completed order") and addTest L627-628 ("Cannot add test to completed or cancelled order") refuse permanently; getOrders L141 filters solely on header orderStatus (`if (orderStatus) q.orderStatus = orderStatus`) and no rejected-sample worklist endpoint exists (grep of controllers/Investigation finds only the reject handler), so the reopened order is invisible under ?status=PENDING/SAMPLE_COLLECTED deep-links seeded at InvestigationOrders.j…

findings.title: All-EXTERNAL (outside-lab) investigation order can never be verified — verify 409s with ILLEGAL_TRANSITION PENDING → COMPLETED
findings.mechanism: enterExternalResult() mutates only item.resultStatus (PENDING → COMPLETED) and NEVER touches order.orderStatus — unlike enterResults(), which advances the header to IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED at investigationOrderService.js:401-409. For an order whose items are all performedAt==='EXTERNAL', collectSamples() also `continue`s over every item (line 195), so the header is never moved off 'PENDING'. Later verifyResults() computes allVerified===true and sets order.orderStatus='COMPLETED' (line 523-525). The pre('save') state-machine guard (InvestigationOrderModel.js:367-398) then calls _ioAssert('InvestigationOrder','PENDING','COMPLETED'), and the registry only allows PENDING → [SAMPLE_COLLECTED, IN_PROGRESS, CANCELLED] (statusTransitionGuard.js:139). assertTransition throws code 'ILLEGAL_TRANSITION' / status 409, save() aborts, and the in-memory VERIFIED stamps are discarded. The controller surfaces it as 409 (investigationOrderController.js:155). Same trap fires on an INTERNAL order if a tech enters results without first calling /collect-sample: enterResults sets COMPLETED from a PENDING header (line 404-406). Note the E2E suite never exercises the EXTERNAL path (no 'EXTERNAL' reference anywhere under Backend/scripts), so 136/136 green does not cover this.
findings.failureScenario: Doctor orders 'MRI Brain' where InvestigationMaster.performedAt==='EXTERNAL'. POST /api/investigation-orders → order INV-20260717-0007, orderStatus=PENDING, item.sampleStatus='N/A'. Staff POST /:id/enter-external-result {itemId, externalLabName:'City Imaging', interpretation:'No acute infarct'} → 200, item.resultStatus=COMPLETED, header still PENDING. Radiologist clicks Verify (Frontend InvestigationOrders.jsx:447 → POST /:id/verify) → HTTP 409 {code:'ILLEGAL_TRANSITION', message:'Illegal InvestigationOrder transition: PENDING → COMPLETED'}. The outside MRI report can never be released; the order is stuck in the PENDING worklist forever and getDashboardSummary() keeps counting it as pending.
findings.fixSketch: In enterExternalResult(), mirror enterResults(): after stamping the item, advance the header (PENDING/SAMPLE_COLLECTED → IN_PROGRESS, or → COMPLETED when every item is done). Additionally add 'COMPLETED' to the PENDING row of LEGAL_TRANSITIONS.InvestigationOrder, or have verifyResults() step the header PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED rather than jumping.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link of the cited chain is present in the actual code, and no bypass exists. 1. Header starts PENDING: InvestigationOrderModel.js:197 `default: "PENDING"`; investigationOrderService.js:90 sets `sampleStatus: "N/A"` for EXTERNAL items at create. 2. collectSamples() skips EXTERNAL: investigationOrderService.js:195 `if (item.performedAt === "EXTERNAL") continue;` — for an all-EXTERNAL order the header advance at :216-218 is never reached in practice (no sample to collect; every item is N/A). 3. enterExternalResult() (:451-479) mutates ONLY the item — :466 `item.resultStatus = "COMPLETED"` — and never touches order.orderStatus before `await order.save()` at :477. This is exactly the asymmetry claimed: enterResults() at :401-409 DOES advance the header (allItemsDone -> COMPLETED, else PENDING/SAMPLE_COLLECTED -> IN_PROGRESS), enterExternalResult() has no equivalent block. 4. verifyResults() :522-525: `const allVerified = order.items.every(i => i.resultStatus === "VERIFIED"); if (allVerified) { order.orderStatus = "COMPLETED"; ... }` — jumps straight from PENDING. 5. Guard fires: InvestigationOrderModel.js:370-375 `if (!this.isNew && this.isModified("orderStatus") && this.__priorOrderStatus) _ioAssert("InvestigationOrder", this.__priorOrderStatus, this.orderStatus, {force: !!this.__forceTransition, ...})`. __priorOrderStatus is snapshotted at :351 by post("init") (doc loaded via findById, so it is set). PENDING->COMPLETED is a genuine value change so isModified() is true. 6.…

findings.title: Lab Technician can create an already-'verified' imaging/histopath report and forge the authorizing radiologist's signature (create path skips the strip that update has)
findings.mechanism: reportCreate does `const body = req.body || {}` … `body.status = body.status || 'reported'` … `LabReport.create(body)` with no field whitelist. LabReportSchema (models/Clinical/labRecordsModels.js:125-131) exposes status enum ['draft','reported','verified'] plus verifiedBy / verifiedByName / verifiedAt as ordinary writable paths, so all four are mass-assignable. reportUpdate explicitly deletes body.status==='verified', body.verifiedBy and body.verifiedAt (lines 319-321, comment 'R7hr-233 — audit: lab self-verify escalation'), and trendUpdate does the same (227-230) — but neither create path does. The routes gate create at requireAction('lab.records.write') = [Admin, Lab Technician, Radiologist] (labRecordsRoutes.js:39, permissions.js:229) while verification is requireAction('lab.records.verify') = [Admin, Doctor, Radiologist] (permissions.js:230). A Lab Technician therefore reaches 'verified' without holding the verify action. It also bypasses trendVerify's QC-release gate (labRecordsController.js:247-259), which only runs on the PATCH /verify route. Once created verified, reportUpdate/trendUpdate refuse all content edits with 409 REPORT_VERIFIED_LOCKED (313-315 / 221-223), so the forged record is immutable and there is no un-verify route.
findings.failureScenario: A Lab Technician (role has lab.records.write, not lab.records.verify) POSTs /api/lab-records/reports with {UHID:'BIMS0042', reportType:'imaging-ct', testName:'CT Head Plain', impression:'No intracranial bleed', status:'verified', verifiedBy:'<Dr. Radiologist ObjectId>', verifiedByName:'Dr. A. Sharma', verifiedAt:'2026-07-17T10:00:00Z'}. Server returns 201. The NABL print and the patient file now show a CT report released and signed by Dr. Sharma, who never saw it; the same body with status:'verified' on POST /api/lab-records/trends forges a released CBC sheet and skips the analyser QC-failure block. Any subsequent PUT to correct it returns 409 REPORT_VERIFIED_LOCKED.
findings.fixSketch: In reportCreate and trendCreate, whitelist the accepted body (or explicitly `delete body.verifiedBy; delete body.verifiedByName; delete body.verifiedAt; if (body.status === 'verified') body.status = 'reported';`) exactly as the update handlers already do — verification must only be reachable via PATCH /:id/verify under lab.records.verify.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every element of the claimed chain is present in the actual code; I attempted refutation on four fronts and all failed. 1) CREATE PATHS HAVE NO STRIP (verified verbatim). labRecordsController.js:290-303 reportCreate: `const body = req.body || {}` … only `body.reportedBy`/`reportedByName`/`reportDate` are server-stamped, then `body.status = body.status || "reported"` and `LabReport.create(body)`. No whitelist, and verifiedBy/verifiedByName/verifiedAt are never touched. labRecordsController.js:197-211 trendCreate: same shape — `body.status = body.status || "draft"` then `LabTrend.create(body)`. Note the `||` idiom is exactly what lets a client-supplied `status:"verified"` survive: it only fills a MISSING status, it does not constrain a supplied one. 2) UPDATE PATHS DO STRIP — asymmetry is real. labRecordsController.js:319-321 (reportUpdate): `if (body.status === "verified") delete body.status; delete body.verifiedBy; delete body.verifiedAt;` under comment "R7hr-233 (audit: lab self-verify escalation) — verification is the doctor-only transition and must go through reportVerify". labRecordsController.js:227-230 (trendUpdate): same plus `delete body.verifiedByName`. So the codebase already treats this exact forgery as a known threat on PUT — the POST simply was never given the same guard. 3) FIELDS ARE MASS-ASSIGNABLE (models/Clinical/labRecordsModels.js). LabReportSchema:125 `status: { enum: ["draft","reported","verified"] }`; :129-131 `verifiedBy` (ObjectId ref User), `verifie…

findings.title: createOrder silently drops investigations that are inactive or resolve to no master — order returns 201 with the test missing and no warning
findings.mechanism: The item loop does `const inv = await InvestigationMaster.findById(item.investigationId); if (!inv || !inv.isActive) continue;`. A skipped item is neither collected into an error nor reported back. The only guard is line 95 (`if (!orderItems.length) throw`), which fires only when EVERY item is dropped. Partial drops are indistinguishable from success — the controller returns 201 with the populated order (controller line 32), and the actionLog remark 'Created with N test(s)' reflects the surviving count, not the requested one. The same silent path is reused by requestRetest (controller 217-234), which clones parent items into createOrder.
findings.failureScenario: Doctor orders CBC + Serum Troponin-I for a chest-pain patient. Troponin-I was deactivated in the master (isActive=false) that morning during a price-list revision. POST /api/investigation-orders {items:[{investigationId: CBC},{investigationId: Troponin}]} → 201 with an order containing only CBC. The doctor's UI shows a successful order; nobody is told Troponin was dropped, it is never collected, never billed, and never resulted. The cardiac marker is silently missing from the workup.
findings.fixSketch: Accumulate skipped items and either reject the whole order (400 with the offending investigationIds) or return them in a `skipped[]`/warning field on the 201 envelope so the ordering clinician sees the drop.
findings.verifyEvidence: Mechanism verified verbatim. investigationOrderService.js:61-63: `for (const item of items) { const inv = await InvestigationMaster.findById(item.investigationId); if (!inv || !inv.isActive) continue; }` — skipped items are never accumulated or surfaced. Line 95 `if (!orderItems.length) throw new Error("No valid investigations found")` fires ONLY on a total drop, so partial drops are indistinguishable from success. Line 121 writes actionLog remark `Created with ${orderItems.length} test(s)` — the SURVIVING count, so the audit trail also conceals the drop. Controller investigationOrderController.js:32 returns `sendOk(res, data, undefined, 201)` with no skipped/warning field. Corroborating evidence the drop is an oversight, not intentional leniency: the sibling addTest at investigationOrderService.js:631-632 performs the same lookup and HARD-THROWS (`const inv = await InvestigationMaster.findById(investigationId); if (!inv) throw new Error("Investigation not found");`). Same operation, opposite handling. Reachability confirmed: routes/index.js:233 mounts `/investigation-orders`; scripts/pentest-rbac.js:64 shows POST /investigation-orders passes for Admin/Doctor/Receptionist. InvestigationMasterModel.js:121 defines `isActive: { type: Boolean, default: true }`, and investigationMasterService.js:95-99 `deactivate()` is a SOFT delete (`$set: { isActive: false }`), so the _id remains valid and referencable after deactivation. One nuance that refines (but does not refute) the audito…

findings.title: Report dispatch/print is not gated on VERIFIED — a Lab Tech can release a report whose results are still unverified or empty
findings.mechanism: markReportPrinted uses findByIdAndUpdate to stamp reportPrintedAt/reportPrintedBy and push a REPORT_PRINTED actionLog row, with no read of order.orderStatus or items[].resultStatus. Because it is findOneAndUpdate it also bypasses both pre('save') hooks (the state guard at InvestigationOrderModel.js:367 and the verified-lock at :281), so no model-layer check compensates. The route is requireAction('lab.dispatch') = [Admin, Lab Technician, Radiologist] (permissions.js:216) — i.e. the same technician who typed the result can dispatch it, with no pathologist release in between. This is the exact release-control that the QC gate in verifyResults (lines 486-505) and the LabResult COMPLETED → VERIFIED matrix (statusTransitionGuard.js:159-164) exist to enforce, and dispatch sits entirely outside them.
findings.failureScenario: Lab Tech enters a Potassium of 7.8 via POST /:id/enter-results (item.resultStatus='COMPLETED', not VERIFIED, analyser 'AU480' whose latest LabQCLog result is 'FAIL'). Instead of routing to the pathologist, they POST /api/investigation-orders/:id/print → 200. reportPrintedAt is stamped and the report is handed to the ward as an issued document, even though /verify would have returned 409 QC_FAILED and blocked exactly this release.
findings.fixSketch: Load the order in markReportPrinted and reject (409) unless every item (or every itemId being printed) has resultStatus==='VERIFIED'; convert to a findById/save so the model hooks also apply.
findings.verifyEvidence: The cited code matches exactly and the defect is real — in fact reachable more easily than the auditor claimed. 1. MISSING GATE CONFIRMED — investigationOrderService.js:541-559. markReportPrinted is verbatim as described: a bare findByIdAndUpdate stamping reportPrintedAt/reportPrintedBy + $push of a REPORT_PRINTED actionLog row. There is NO read of order.orderStatus and NO inspection of items[].resultStatus. The only failure path is `if (!order) throw new Error("Order not found")` (:557). Contrast with every sibling mutator, which all load-then-check: verifyResults (:483-505) runs the QC gate, enterResults (:353-362) rejects REJECTED samples and VERIFIED-locked items, cancelOrder (:565-568) guards orderStatus. Dispatch is the sole state-changing endpoint with no precondition. 2. RBAC SEGREGATION GAP CONFIRMED — permissions.js:214/215/216:    "lab.result-entry": ["Admin","Lab Technician","Radiologist"]    "lab.verify":       ["Admin","Doctor","Radiologist"]   ← Lab Technician deliberately EXCLUDED    "lab.dispatch":     ["Admin","Lab Technician","Radiologist"]    The deliberate exclusion of Lab Technician from lab.verify proves verification is an intended separate tier; granting that same technician lab.dispatch with no VERIFIED precondition routes around the tier entirely. Route confirmed at investigationOrderRoutes.js:39. Controller markPrinted (investigationOrderController.js:159-167) is a thin passthrough adding no check. 3. NO COMPENSATING CONTROL ANYWHERE — a repo-wide…

findings.title: enterExternalResult blanks previously-recorded externalReportRef/interpretation on partial update and accepts results on a CANCELLED order
findings.mechanism: Two gaps versus its INTERNAL sibling enterResults(). (1) Missing-field handling is asymmetric: line 463 preserves (`externalLabName || item.externalLabName`) but lines 464-465 unconditionally coerce (`item.externalReportRef = externalReportRef || ''` and `item.interpretation = interpretation || ''`), so any call that omits those keys wipes the stored values rather than leaving them. (2) There is no `if (order.orderStatus === 'CANCELLED') throw` guard — enterResults has one at line 316-317 and rejectSample at line 245, but enterExternalResult has none. A cancelled order's header is not modified by this method, so the state-machine guard never fires either, and the item's LabResult PENDING → COMPLETED move is legal in the matrix. The result lands on an order whose BillingTriggers were already voided by cancelOrder (lines 590-615).
findings.failureScenario: (1) Staff attach the outside USG: POST /:id/enter-external-result {itemId, externalLabName:'City Imaging', externalReportRef:'CI-2026-8891', interpretation:'Grade-II fatty liver'}. Next day someone corrects only the lab name: POST /:id/enter-external-result {itemId, externalLabName:'City Imaging Pvt Ltd'} → 200, and externalReportRef + interpretation are both silently blanked to '' — the outside report reference and the radiologist's impression are lost with no amendment trail. (2) A doctor cancels an order (POST /:id/cancel, triggers voided, line removed from the bill); staff then POST /:id/enter-external-result → 200, so a result is charted against a cancelled, unbilled order.
findings.fixSketch: Use `if (externalReportRef !== undefined)` / `if (interpretation !== undefined)` guards, and add the same CANCELLED (and VERIFIED-locked) checks enterResults already performs at lines 316-317 and 359-362.
findings.verifyEvidence: Both cited mechanisms verified in the actual code at Backend/services/Investigation/investigationOrderService.js:451-479. GAP 1 (partial-update blanking) — CONFIRMED at lines 463-465, exactly as filed:   item.externalLabName   = externalLabName || item.externalLabName;  // preserves   item.externalReportRef = externalReportRef || "";                  // coerces   item.interpretation    = interpretation || "";                     // coerces The controller (Backend/controllers/Investigation/investigationOrderController.js:134) calls `svc.enterExternalResult(req.params.id, req.body)` — no whitelist, no merge — so an omitted key arrives as undefined and the || "" wipes the stored value. GAP 2 (no CANCELLED guard) — CONFIRMED by absence at lines 455-468. The three sibling mutators in the same file all guard: collectSamples (line 189-190), rejectSample (line 245), enterResults (line 316-317). enterExternalResult has none. I verified no other layer catches it:   - Header state-machine assert (models/Investigation/InvestigationOrderModel.js:370) only fires on `this.isModified("orderStatus")`; this method never touches the header, so it never fires.   - Item-level matrix (utils/statusTransitionGuard.js:159-164) has `LabResult: { PENDING: ["IN_PROGRESS","COMPLETED"], ... }` — PENDING→COMPLETED is legal, so the item's move passes.   - Post-verification lock (InvestigationOrderModel.js:281-303) only engages when the prior snapshot is VERIFIED. The write therefore reaches order.save() on…

findings.title: Unverified lab results and draft imaging reports are pulled into the discharge-summary investigations paragraph
findings.mechanism: The aggregator (documented at lines 16-19 as the single source of truth for the discharge-summary investigations paragraph AND the Doctor/Nurse Investigations tab) accepts InvestigationOrder items with `['COMPLETED','VERIFIED'].includes(it.resultStatus)` at line 94 — COMPLETED means a technician typed the value but no pathologist has released it. For LabReport it is worse: the loop at lines 104-108 applies no status filter at all, so rows with status 'draft' (labRecordsModels.js:125) and rows with an empty impression (the `|| 'reported'` fallback at line 106) are emitted. LabTrend readings at 112-129 are likewise unfiltered on the sheet's status. The whole design of the release chain — QC gate at investigationOrderService.js:486-505, COMPLETED → VERIFIED matrix, lab.records.verify RBAC — is bypassed at read time.
findings.failureScenario: A tech drafts a CT report (LabReport status='draft', impression:'? mass lesion — awaiting radiologist review') and enters a Creatinine of 4.2 that is never verified. The patient is discharged; the discharge summary's auto-generated investigations paragraph reads 'Day 3 (15 Jul 2026): CT Abdomen (CT scan) — ? mass lesion — awaiting radiologist review; Creatinine 4.2 mg/dL.' The radiologist later corrects the draft to 'benign hepatic cyst', but the legally-signed discharge summary already went out with the unreleased provisional finding.
findings.fixSketch: Filter to it.resultStatus==='VERIFIED' for InvestigationOrder items and r.status==='verified' for LabReport/LabTrend when the caller is the discharge-summary composer; if the live clinician tab needs drafts, pass an explicit `includeUnverified` flag and label them as provisional.
findings.verifyEvidence: All three cited mechanisms exist verbatim in the code, and the caller chain to the discharge summary is real. 1) admissionInvestigationsService.js:94 — `if (!["COMPLETED", "VERIFIED"].includes(it.resultStatus)) continue;`. The enum at InvestigationOrderModel.js:104-108 is PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/VERIFIED, and investigationOrderService.js:508-514 flips COMPLETED -> VERIFIED only inside verifyResults(). So COMPLETED strictly precedes pathologist release; accepting it emits pre-release values by construction. 2) admissionInvestigationsService.js:104-108 — the LabReport loop applies NO status filter. labRecordsModels.js:125 defines `status: enum ["draft","reported","verified"], default: "draft"`. Line 106's `(r.impression || r.findings || r.organism || "reported")` emits draft narrative text verbatim into the paragraph. 3) admissionInvestigationsService.js:112-129 — LabTrend readings are filtered only by inWindow(rd.date); the sheet-level release status (labRecordsModels.js:77, draft/reported/verified, default draft) is never consulted. The per-reading `status` at labRecordsModels.js:28 is a clinical flag (normal/borderline/critical), not a release gate — so it does not compensate. 4) The bypass claim holds: the QC-release gate at investigationOrderService.js:486-505 (blocks verify when the analyser's latest QC FAILED) only guards the write/verify path. Sibling read code enforces the intended discipline strictly — InvestigationOrderModel.js:286 (`if (prior.resultStatus !==…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: Doctor-order MAR seed writes `drugName` into a schema that has no such field → nameless medication row silently bypasses the drug-allergy gate at administration
findings.mechanism: The R7bn-4 auto-seed builds `medRow = { drugName: order.orderDetails?.medicineName, ... }`, but MARMedicationSchema (models/Clinical/MARModel.js:46) declares `medicineName: { required: true }` and has NO `drugName` path. The row is written with `MAR.updateOne({...},{ $push: { medications: medRow } })` (line 774) — updateOne does NOT run validators by default, so the `required: true` on medicineName never fires. Mongoose strict-mode casting strips the unknown `drugName` (also `twoNurseRequired`, `doctorOrderId`). I verified this empirically: `MAR.schema.path('medications').cast([medRow])` yields `{"dose":"10U","route":"","frequency":"BD","scheduledTimes":["08:00"],"startDate":...,"isHighAlert":true,...}` — medicineName absent, drugName gone; `new MAR({...medications:[medRow]}).validate()` errors on `medications.0.medicineName` and `medications.0.route` (route:"" is not in the enum), confirming the write only survives because updateOne skips validation. The downstream blast radius is the allergy gate: marController.js:224 calls `assertDrugSafeOrOverride(med, patientAllergies, ...)`, and utils/allergyCheck.js:114-125 `_drugProbe` joins `drug.drugName, drug.medicineName, drug.name, drug.genericName, drug.brandName` — ALL empty on this row → probe === "" → line 141 `if (!probe) return { collision: false }` → the check returns SAFE unconditionally. The header of allergyCheck.js names recordAdministration as call-site #4 and marController.js:210-212 explicitly notes this is the ONLY allergy checkpoint for ward-stock drugs (no pharmacy dispense in this path).
findings.failureScenario: Patient UH00001234 has `allergyList: [{ allergen: "Penicillin" }]`. A doctor POSTs a Medication order with `orderDetails.medicineName = "Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 1.2g IV"` (genericName contains Penicillin) and `admissionId` set. doctorOrderRoutes.js:774 pushes a medication row whose only identity fields are dose/route/frequency — medicineName is undefined. The nurse opens the Treatment Chart and NursePatientPanel.jsx:986 renders `{m.drug||m.medicineName||"—"}` → the row shows "—" with no drug name. She PATCHes /api/mar/:id/medication/:medId/administer with status GIVEN + fiveRightsChecked:true. `_drugProbe` returns "", the gate returns `{collision:false}` at allergyCheck.js:141, no 409 ALLERGY_COLLISION is raised, and the penicillin-class drug is charted GIVEN against a documented penicillin allergy. Secondary damage: autoBillingService.js:1290 computes `drugName = medication.drugName || medication.medicineName || medication.name || ""` → "" → findServiceByName("") misses and line 1319-1320 falls back to the generic `NRS-INJ` injection fee, so the drug is under-billed as a nursing admin charge.
findings.fixSketch: In doctorOrderRoutes.js:761 rename `drugName:` → `medicineName:` (and `doctorOrderId` → a real schema path, or add it to MARMedicationSchema). Default `route` to "Other" instead of "" so it satisfies the enum. Add `{ runValidators: true }` to the `MAR.updateOne` at line 774 so a required-field regression fails loudly instead of writing a nameless row. Harden allergyCheck.js:141 — an empty probe should throw/log a `DRUG_UNIDENTIFIED` error rather than silently returning `{collision:false}` (fail-closed on an unidentifiable drug). Backfill existing MAR rows with `medicineName: null`.
findings.verifyEvidence: I tried to refute this on four separate fronts and every one held up; all cited line numbers are exact. 1) FIELD MISMATCH — REAL. doctorOrderRoutes.js:761 writes `drugName: order.orderDetails?.medicineName || ... || ""`. MARModel.js:46 declares `medicineName: { type: String, required: true, trim: true }` and MARMedicationSchema has NO `drugName`, `twoNurseRequired`, or `doctorOrderId` path. Notably line 761 is the ONLY place in doctorOrderRoutes.js that writes a `drugName` key — every sibling path (e.g. line 282 `medicineName: order.orderDetails?.medicineName`) uses the correct name, so this is an anomaly, not a convention. 2) EMPIRICAL CLAIM — REPRODUCED VERBATIM. Ran the auditor's test against the real model: cast yields `{"dose":"10U","route":"","frequency":"BD","scheduledTimes":["08:00"],"startDate":...,"endDate":null,"prescribedBy":null,"isHighAlert":true,"isLASA":false,"isActive":true,"administrations":[],"_id":...}` — medicineName absent, drugName silently stripped by strict-mode cast (`drugName path exists: false`, `medicineName path exists: true`). `new MAR({...}).validate()` → `VALIDATE ERRORS: medications.0.medicineName, medications.0.route`. The `MAR.updateOne` at line 774 passes no `runValidators`, so the nameless row persists. Route enum is a validator not a cast, so `route:""` also survives the update. 3) RESCUE HOOKS — NONE. MARModel.js:126-165 has only a post-init snapshot and a pre-save append-only guard; nothing backfills medicineName. 4) THE STRONGEST REF…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: Shift-handover vitals snapshot does exact-case key lookups that never match the vital sheet's stored keys → every NABH MOM.2 handover records all-null vitals
findings.mechanism: `pullLatestVitals` reads the last vital row and does exact-key lookups: `get("pulse")`, `get("bp")`, `get("rr")`, `get("temp")`, `get("spo2")`. But the VitalSheet `values` Map is keyed by the vital's DISPLAY NAME, not a lowercase code. Frontend/src/Components/vital/VitalSheet.jsx:50-57 defines the catalog as `{ name: "Pulse" }, { name: "BP Systolic" }, { name: "BP Diastolic" }, { name: "GCS" }, { name: "Temperature" }, { name: "SpO2" }` and the submit handler at VitalSheet.jsx:569 writes `acc[v.name] = { value, unit }` — i.e. the literal keys "Pulse", "BP Systolic", "Temperature", "SpO2". None of "pulse"/"temp"/"spo2" match (wrong case, and "temp" vs "Temperature"/"spo2" vs "SpO2" differ in substance too); "bp" and "rr" have no counterpart at all since BP is split into two systolic/diastolic keys. Every `get()` therefore hits the `?? null` fallback. The correct pattern exists elsewhere in the codebase and proves the convention: nabhRegisterEmitter.js:982 normalises with `String(k).toLowerCase().replace(/[\s_-]/g,"")` before matching — shiftHandoverService is the only consumer doing a naive exact lookup.
findings.failureScenario: Night nurse charts 22:00 vitals via the Vital Sheet page: values = { "Pulse": {value:118}, "BP Systolic": {value:88}, "SpO2": {value:89} } — a deteriorating patient. At shift change she POSTs /api/shift-handover for the same uhid+date. `pullLatestVitals` finds the sheet and correctly resolves `vitalSheetRef`, but every getter misses, so the created ShiftHandover stores `vitalsSnapshot: { pulse: null, bp: null, rr: null, temp: null, spo2: null, takenAt: "22:00" }`. The incoming nurse's handover view — and `getLatestHandover`/`getHandoversByAdmission`, which surface `vitalsSnapshot` as the at-a-glance summary — shows blank vitals with a plausible-looking timestamp, hiding the tachycardia/hypotension/desaturation at the exact moment of transfer of care. The NABH MOM.2 handover record is permanently written with null vitals.
findings.fixSketch: Normalise both sides before lookup, mirroring nabhRegisterEmitter.js:982: build a key-normalised index of `last.values` (`k.toLowerCase().replace(/[\s_-]/g,'')`) and resolve each vital against an alias list — pulse:['pulse','heartrate'], temp:['temperature','temp'], spo2:['spo2','oxygensaturation'], rr:['respiratoryrate','rr'] — and compose `bp` from 'bpsystolic'/'bpdiastolic' as "88/60". Add a regression test asserting a non-null snapshot from a real VitalSheet.jsx payload.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED, IMPACT OVERSTATED. Confirmed at code level: - Backend/services/Nurse/shiftHandoverService.js:29-32 — `const get = (key) => { if (v instanceof Map) return v.get(key)?.value ?? null; return v[key]?.value ?? null; }` — exact-key lookup, no normalisation, in BOTH branches. - :37-41 — snapshot built from get("pulse"), get("bp"), get("rr"), get("temp"), get("spo2"). - Frontend/src/Components/vital/VitalSheet.jsx:49-58 — catalog is `{name:"Pulse"},{name:"BP Systolic"},{name:"BP Diastolic"},{name:"GCS"},{name:"IV Fluid"},{name:"Temperature"},{name:"Pain Score"},{name:"SpO2"}`; :569 writes `acc[v.name] = { value: Number(val), unit: v.unit }` — literal display-name keys. None of "pulse"/"bp"/"rr"/"temp"/"spo2" match → every get() hits `?? null`. - Backend/services/Vitals/vitalSheetService.js:67-94 — save path passes `values` through verbatim; no key normalisation anywhere on write. - Backend/models/Vitals/vitalSheetModel.js:19-32 (values = Map) and :91 (comment: activeVitals name = "Blood Pressure", "Pulse") confirm display names are the schema convention. - Independent second writer corroborates: Backend/scripts/seedDemoCompleteFile.js:166-176 stores `"BP Systolic"`, `"BP Diastolic"`, `"Pulse"`, `"Temperature"`, `"SpO2"`, `"Respiratory Rate"` — so no writer anywhere produces lowercase codes. - Convention claim verified: nabhRegisterEmitter.js:982 does `String(k).toLowerCase().replace(/[\s_-]/g,"")` before matching; seed comment :164-165 notes the print matcher us…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: Vital sheet has no append-only lock: a single POST wholesale-replaces a whole day's vitals and DELETE hard-destroys the day's record — any Nurse, no audit trail
findings.mechanism: VitalSheetSchema (models/Vitals/vitalSheetModel.js:42-104) has no lock/status/signed field and no post-init/pre-save append-only hook — in stark contrast to the sibling clinical records that DO enforce one (MARModel.js:126-171 append-only administrations; NursingCarePlanModel.js:148-165 completed-lock). `saveVitalSheet` upserts with `$set: { ..., tableData: resolvedTableData }` (line 94) — a full array REPLACEMENT, not an append. `updateVitalSheet` does the same via `sheet.tableData = resolvedTableData` (line 191). `deleteVitalSheet` (line 210) `findOneAndDelete`s the entire day's sheet. All four routes are gated only on `vitals.write` (vitalSheetRoutes.js:16-40) — held by Admin/Doctor/Nurse per the route header — with no ownership check, no discharge/finalise gate, and no ClinicalAudit emit anywhere in the controller or service (contrast marController.js:378-402 and nursingAssessmentsRoutes.js:132-144, which both emit). Critically, `validate(["uhid","date","tableData","activeVitals"])` does not stop erasure: middleware/validateRequest.js:5-12 only rejects `undefined`/`null`/empty-string, so `tableData: []` passes the guard and lands in the `$set`.
findings.failureScenario: A patient deteriorates and a nurse's 14:00 entry records SpO2 62%. To conceal it, any user holding `vitals.write` (any Nurse in the hospital — no ward or assignment scoping) sends `POST /api/vitalsheet { uhid: "UH00001234", date: "2026-07-17", tableData: [], activeVitals: [{name:"Pulse"}] }`. validateRequest passes (`[]` is neither undefined, null, nor an empty string), the upsert `$set`s `tableData: []`, and every vital observation for that day — including the 62% desat and its recordedBy/nurseName attribution — is gone. No append-only hook blocks it, no ClinicalAudit row records it, and no prior version is retained. `DELETE /api/vitalsheet/delete { uhid, date }` achieves the same in one call. A benign variant is just as damaging: two nurses with the sheet open concurrently each POST their own reconstructed `tableData`, and the second write silently discards the first nurse's rows (last-write-wins on a full-array $set) — the same lost-update race that MARModel.js:321-333 was explicitly hardened against.
findings.fixSketch: Add an append-only pre-save hook to VitalSheetSchema mirroring MARModel.js:126-171 (snapshot prior tableData `_id`s on post-init; reject removal or mutation of `time`/`values`/`recordedBy` on existing rows). Convert saveVitalSheet's `$set: { tableData }` to a `$push: { tableData: { $each: newRows } }` for new time-slots only, and reject a `tableData` payload that drops existing slots. Remove the DELETE route or re-gate it behind an admin-only `vitals.delete` action with a mandatory reason + ClinicalAudit emit and a soft-delete (`voidedAt`/`voidedBy`) instead of findOneAndDelete. Emit `emitClinicalAudit` on every vital-sheet mutation.
findings.verifyEvidence: I attempted to refute this on five independent fronts and each one held up against the actual code. 1. NO LOCK HOOK — CONFIRMED. Backend/models/Vitals/vitalSheetModel.js is 108 lines end-to-end and contains ZERO Mongoose middleware: no post("init"), no pre("save"), no lock/status/signed/voidedAt field. The schema (lines 42-99) is plain data + three indexes (102-104) + module.exports (106). Nothing prevents row removal or mutation. 2. DESTRUCTIVE FULL-ARRAY REPLACE — CONFIRMED at both cited sites. vitalSheetService.js:79-98 is `VitalSheet.findOneAndUpdate({uhid, date: formattedDate}, { $set: { ...,  activeVitals: activeVitals || [], tableData: resolvedTableData } }, { new: true, upsert: true, ... })` — line 94 is verbatim `tableData: resolvedTableData`, a whole-array `$set`, not a `$push`. updateVitalSheet line 191 is verbatim `sheet.tableData = resolvedTableData;` followed by `await sheet.save()` (195) — and since the schema has no pre-save hook, that save is unguarded. deleteVitalSheet line 210 is verbatim `VitalSheet.findOneAndDelete({ uhid, date: formattedDate })` — a hard delete of the whole day's sheet, no soft-delete field exists to use. 3. VALIDATOR DOES NOT STOP ERASURE — CONFIRMED. middleware/validateRequest.js:5-12 filters only `value === undefined || value === null || (typeof value === "string" && value.trim() === "")`. `tableData: []` is an object, not undefined/null/string → passes the `validate(["uhid","date","tableData","activeVitals"])` guard at vitalSheetRou…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: Nursing care-plan COP.1 completed-lock is bypassable three ways: hard DELETE, findOneAndUpdate on problems, and signedBy/status forgery via mass-assignment on create
findings.mechanism: NursingCarePlanModel.js:151-165 implements the R7az-D2-HIGH-3 lock as a `pre("save")` hook — it fires ONLY on `.save()` paths. The controller's `update` (line 55-73) correctly routes through findById + `.save()` and additionally strips `signedBy`/`signedAt` (lines 67-68). Three sibling handlers do not: (1) `delete` (line 106) calls `NursingCarePlan.findByIdAndDelete` — a query-path op that never triggers the pre-save hook and has no status check at all, so a signed/completed plan is destroyed outright; (2) `updateProblemStatus` (line 78-88) calls `findOneAndUpdate` with `$set` on `nursingProblems.$.status`/`.evaluation`/`.resolvedAt` — also a query path, also with no `status === completed` guard, so a locked plan's clinical problem list stays mutable; (3) `create` (line 16) is a bare `NursingCarePlan.create(req.body)` with no field whitelist — `signedBy` (ObjectId ref User), `signedAt`, and `status` are all writable schema paths (model lines 123-132), and the `.save()` lock hook short-circuits on `if (this.isNew) return next()` (line 152), so nothing validates them on insert. Every one of these routes is gated only on `mar.write` (nursingCarePlanRoutes.js:19/22/24) — any Nurse — with no author/ownership check and no ClinicalAudit emit.
findings.failureScenario: A nurse signs off care plan P (status "completed", signedBy = her User id) documenting a Braden-scale pressure-ulcer prevention plan. (a) Another nurse sends `DELETE /api/nursing-care-plans/P` — findByIdAndDelete removes the signed NABH COP.1 record with no lock check, no soft-delete, no audit row; the evidence that the prevention plan existed is unrecoverable. (b) Or she sends `PATCH /api/nursing-care-plans/P/problem/<pid>/status { status: "RESOLVED", evaluation: "skin intact" }` — findOneAndUpdate bypasses the pre-save hook entirely and stamps `resolvedAt: new Date()` on a locked plan, retroactively rewriting the clinical narrative after a pressure ulcer developed. (c) Or on creation she POSTs `{ UHID, ipdNo, status: "completed", signedBy: "<senior nurse's User ObjectId>", signedAt: "2026-07-01T09:00:00Z" }` — `create(req.body)` accepts all three, the isNew guard skips the lock hook, and the plan is born pre-signed in another clinician's name and immediately locked against correction.
findings.fixSketch: Move the lock enforcement out of the pre-save hook into a `pre(/^findOneAndUpdate|findOneAndDelete|deleteOne|updateOne/)` query hook so query-path ops are covered too (or load-then-save in updateProblemStatus/delete). In `delete`, load the doc first, refuse 409 CARE_PLAN_LOCKED when status is completed/COMPLETED, and soft-delete (`voidedAt`/`voidedBy`/reason) rather than findByIdAndDelete; re-gate on an admin-only action. In `create`, apply an explicit field whitelist and force `status: "draft"`, `signedBy: null`, `signedAt: null` server-side (mirroring the deletes at lines 67-68) — signing must be its own endpoint that stamps `req.user.id`. Add emitClinicalAudit on complete/delete/problem-status.
findings.verifyEvidence: All three mechanisms verified line-exact against the real code. I tried to refute each and could not. LOCK HOOK IS SAVE-PATH ONLY — CONFIRMED. NursingCarePlanModel.js:151-165 is `NursingCarePlanSchema.pre("save", ...)`, and it depends on `this._priorStatus` set by the `post("init")` hook at :148-150. Both are document-middleware: they fire only on a hydrated doc + `.save()`. There is no `pre(/^findOneAndUpdate/)` or delete query hook anywhere in the file (only the four `.index()` calls at :139-142 sit between the schema and the hooks). So any query-path op bypasses the lock by construction. Also confirmed `if (this.isNew) return next();` at :152 — the hook short-circuits on insert. (1) DELETE — CONFIRMED. Controller :106 `const plan = await NursingCarePlan.findByIdAndDelete(req.params.id);` — query path, no status check whatsoever, hard delete. Contrast the sibling `update` at :55-73, which correctly does findById → status guard (409 CARE_PLAN_LOCKED, :58-64) → `.save()` (:71). (2) updateProblemStatus — CONFIRMED. Controller :78-88 `findOneAndUpdate({_id, "nursingProblems._id"}, {$set: {"nursingProblems.$.status", "nursingProblems.$.evaluation", ...(status === "RESOLVED" && {"nursingProblems.$.resolvedAt": new Date()})}}, {new: true})`. Query path, zero `status === "completed"` guard. A locked plan's clinical problem list stays fully mutable, resolvedAt stamped retroactively. (3) create mass-assignment — CONFIRMED, and stronger than filed. Controller :16 is a bare `NursingCa…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: MAR high-alert-medication dual-witness gate keys off a client-supplied `isHighAlert` boolean that addMedication mass-assigns — the nurse being gated controls the gate
findings.mechanism: The ISMP independent-double-check at line 251 branches solely on `med.isHighAlert`, read off the MAR medication subdoc. `addMedication` (lines 143-147) pushes the raw request body: `MAR.findByIdAndUpdate(id, { $push: { medications: req.body } }, { runValidators: true })` — no field whitelist. `isHighAlert` is a plain writable schema path (MARModel.js:61) that DEFAULTS TO FALSE, so it is set purely from client input and is never derived server-side. The R8 #25 hardening at lines 141-142 anticipated tampering on this exact endpoint but only blocks embedded GIVEN administrations — it does not sanitise the HAM flag. `createOrGet` has the same shape (line 88-94 spreads `...m`). The codebase already owns the canonical derivations and simply doesn't apply them here: DrugModel.js:88 carries an authoritative `isHighAlert`, and DoctorOrderModel.js:26 defines `isHAM(name)` (keyword scan over insulin/opioids/heparin/KCl etc.) which auto-stamps `hamFlag` on save (lines 645-656) — that is why the doctor-order path at doctorOrderRoutes.js:767 (`isHighAlert: !!order.hamFlag`) is safe. The manual MAR path has no equivalent, so the only enforcement of a NABH IPSG.3 / ISMP control is a boolean chosen by the party the control exists to constrain.
findings.failureScenario: A nurse wants to chart an insulin dose at 02:00 without waking a second nurse for the independent double-check. She POSTs `/api/mar/:id/medication { medicineName: "Insulin Human Actrapid 40U", dose: "40U", route: "SC", startDate: "2026-07-17", isHighAlert: false }` (or simply omits isHighAlert — the schema default is false). She then PATCHes `/:id/medication/:medId/administer { status: "GIVEN", fiveRightsChecked: true }`. At line 251 `med.isHighAlert` is false, so the entire HAM block (lines 251-296) — witnessUserId requirement, same-actor rejection, witness-must-be-a-Nurse check — is skipped. The dose is charted with `isHamDose: false` and no `administeredByUser2Id`, so the audit emit at line 398 and any downstream ISMP compliance report both classify a 40U insulin administration as a routine non-HAM dose. The dual-witness control leaves no trace of having been skipped.
findings.fixSketch: Never trust client `isHighAlert`. In addMedication/createOrGet, whitelist the pushed fields and derive the flag server-side: look up the Drug master by medicineName/genericName and take `Drug.isHighAlert`, OR-ed with the existing keyword scanner (export `isHAM` from DoctorOrderModel.js:26 into a shared util and apply it to medicineName+genericName). Persist the derived value only, ignoring any inbound boolean. Also re-derive defensively at line 251 so historical rows written with a false flag are still gated at administration time.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited location matches the code; I tried and failed to refute the mechanism. 1) Mass-assignment sink confirmed — D:\Spherehealth\Backend\controllers\Clinical\marController.js:143-147:    const mar = await MAR.findByIdAndUpdate(req.params.id, { $push: { medications: req.body } }, { new: true, runValidators: true });    The raw request body is pushed with no field whitelist. The R8 #25 guard immediately above (lines 141-142) only rejects `req.body.administrations[].status === GIVEN` — it does not touch `isHighAlert`. Same shape in createOrGet at lines 88-94 (`...m` spread). 2) The gated field is a plain client-writable path — MARModel.js:61: `isHighAlert: { type: Boolean, default: false }`. Mongoose casts the pushed subdoc against MARMedicationSchema, so unknown keys are stripped but `isHighAlert` is a *declared* path and is therefore accepted straight from client input. No derivation exists: MARModel's only hooks are post("init") at :126 (snapshot for the append-only trail) and pre("save") at :144 (blocks mutation/removal of existing administration rows) — neither derives isHighAlert, and findByIdAndUpdate wouldn't fire pre("save") anyway. Default false means simply omitting the field also yields an ungated med. 3) The gate branches solely on that field — marController.js:251: `if (med.isHighAlert && finalStatus === "GIVEN") {` — guarding lines 252-296 (HAM_WITNESS_REQUIRED, HAM_WITNESS_SAME_ACTOR, User lookup + HAM_WITNESS_NOT_NURSE) and the `hamWitness ? {...}` stamp…

findings.dimension: Nursing (assessments, MAR, vitals, care plans, handovers)
findings.title: Every MAR administration ClinicalAudit row records `drug: undefined` — the emit reads `drugName`, a field MARMedicationSchema does not define
findings.mechanism: The R7bn-1/D9 audit emit builds `after: { drug: refreshedMed?.drugName, dose: refreshedMed?.dose, ... }`. MARMedicationSchema declares `medicineName` (MARModel.js:46) and has no `drugName` path — I confirmed via `MAR.schema.path('medications').cast([...])` that `drugName` is stripped by strict-mode casting and never materialises on a medication subdoc. `refreshedMed.drugName` is therefore `undefined` on 100% of administrations, including correctly-seeded rows where `medicineName` IS populated. This is the same field-name confusion as the doctorOrderRoutes.js:761 seed defect but on the read side, which is why it has gone unnoticed: for doctor-order-seeded rows BOTH the write and the read use the wrong name, so the trail looks internally consistent while carrying no drug identity at all.
findings.failureScenario: A nurse charts paracetamol GIVEN on a correctly-created MAR (medicineName: "Paracetamol 650mg"). marController.js:379-401 emits a MAR_DOSE_ADMINISTERED ClinicalAudit row whose `after` payload is `{ drug: undefined, dose: "650mg", status: "GIVEN", nurseName: "..." }` — Mongoose/JSON drops the undefined key entirely. A NABH MOM.4 / IPSG.3 surveyor querying ClinicalAudit for 'which drug was administered at 14:00' gets dose and nurse but no drug name; the audit trail cannot answer the one question it exists to answer, and a retrospective medication-error investigation cannot reconstruct what was given from the audit log alone.
findings.fixSketch: Change line 393 to `drug: refreshedMed?.medicineName` (optionally `|| refreshedMed?.genericName`). Grep the repo for other `\.drugName` reads against MAR medication subdocs and align them on `medicineName`; consider a schema-level virtual `drugName -> medicineName` as a compatibility shim so the two spellings can't silently diverge again.
findings.verifyEvidence: Verified every link in the chain; the finding is accurate as filed (line is 392, not 393 — off-by-one in the citation only). 1. READ SIDE — D:\Spherehealth\Backend\controllers\Clinical\marController.js:392, inside the R7bn-1/D9 ClinicalAudit emit (lines 377-402):    after: {      drug: refreshedMed?.drugName,      dose: refreshedMed?.dose,      ...    }    `refreshedMed` is `pushResult.medications.id(req.params.medId)` (line 344) — a hydrated MARMedicationSchema subdoc. 2. SCHEMA — D:\Spherehealth\Backend\models\Clinical\MARModel.js:44-70, MARMedicationSchema declares `medicineName` (line 46, required:true) and `genericName` (line 47). There is NO `drugName` path. I grepped the model for `strict|virtual|alias|drugName`: ZERO matches — so no `strict: false` escape hatch, no virtual, and no `alias: 'drugName'` shim. Mongoose default strict casting therefore drops `drugName` on write and it never materialises on the subdoc, making `refreshedMed.drugName` `undefined` on 100% of administrations — including MARs whose `medicineName` is correctly populated (which it must be, being required:true). 3. IMPACT IS REAL, NOT SWALLOWED — D:\Spherehealth\Backend\services\Compliance\clinicalAuditService.js:173 persists `after: opts.after || null` verbatim onto the ClinicalAudit row. So the row is written and durable; it simply carries `{ dose, status, scheduledTime, actualTime, nurseName, isHamDose }` with the `drug` key dropped as undefined. Dose/nurse/status survive; drug identity does no…

findings.dimension: Clinical documentation (notes, IA, discharge summary, co-sign)
findings.title: PUT /api/discharge-summary/:id mass-assignment forges finalization, bypassing every NABH gate
findings.mechanism: create() explicitly deletes status/finalizedBy/finalizedByName/finalizedByReg/finalizedAt from req.body (R7hr-226 mass-assignment guard, lines 84-88). update() does NOT — it spreads the raw req.body straight into findOneAndUpdate(..., data, ...) at line 193-197. The pre-check at 186 only 409s when the doc is ALREADY finalized, so a DRAFT sails through. The model's _refuseIfFinalized hook (DischargeSummaryModel.js:235-247) short-circuits with next() when the current status is 'draft', and attachStatusGuard only registers a pre('save') hook (statusTransitionGuard.js:308) which findOneAndUpdate never fires. 'finalized' is a valid enum value so runValidators passes.
findings.failureScenario: Any Doctor/Admin holding `discharge-summary.write` sends PUT /api/discharge-summary/<draftId> with {"status":"finalized","finalizedByName":"Dr. A. Consultant","finalizedAt":"2026-07-17T10:00:00Z","dischargeType":"Death"}. The record flips to finalized and becomes permanently immutable (update() and delete() both refuse a finalized doc; the model guard now blocks every subsequent write). Every gate in finalize() is skipped: MCI reg-no check (218-233), MCCD cause-of-death (262-271), PROM/PREM (279-297), nursing handover (299-324), NABH AAC.3 mandatory content (336-372), and the primary-attending-consultant gate (553-563). No DISCHARGE_SUMMARY_FINALIZED ClinicalAudit row is emitted, emitMortality never fires so the COP.18 Mortality register misses the death, emitLAMA never fires, notifiable-disease raise never fires, and admission.dischargeWorkflow is never advanced — so the receptionist discharge queue never receives the patient while the legal record reads as signed. This is precisely the hole R7hr-226 closed on create(), left open on update().
findings.fixSketch: Hoist the R7hr-226 field-strip out of create() into a shared helper and call it at the top of update() as well (delete data.status/finalizedBy/finalizedByName/finalizedByReg/finalizedAt/cosigned*/contentGateOverride*/nursingHandoverOverride*/legalHold*). Better: replace the raw spread with an explicit allow-list of clinical content fields, mirroring doctorNotesService's `allowed[]` pattern.
findings.verifyEvidence: I attempted to refute this on four independent layers; all four confirm the defect. 1) CONTROLLER — the asymmetry is real. `create()` (dischargeSummaryController.js:84-88) explicitly strips the finalize fields under the R7hr-226 comment: `delete data.status; delete data.finalizedBy; delete data.finalizedByName; delete data.finalizedByReg; delete data.finalizedAt;`. `update()` (168-207) performs NO such strip — it runs `_normaliseDischargeArrays(data)`, computes LOS, then passes the raw `req.body` object straight in at 193-197: `DischargeSummary.findOneAndUpdate({ _id: req.params.id, status: { $ne: "finalized" } }, data, { new: true, runValidators: true })`. `data` has no `$` operators, so Mongoose casts it to an implicit `$set` — every attacker-supplied key lands. The pre-check at 186 (`if (existing.status === "finalized")`) and the `status: { $ne: "finalized" }` filter at 194 are both *already-finalized* guards; a DRAFT satisfies both and proceeds. 2) MODEL HOOK — does not save it. `_refuseIfFinalized` IS registered on findOneAndUpdate/findByIdAndUpdate/updateOne (DischargeSummaryModel.js:257-259), but line 240-241 reads: `const doc = await this.model.findOne(query).select("status").lean(); if (!doc || doc.status !== "finalized") return next();` — it short-circuits unconditionally when the CURRENT status is "draft". It only protects the finalized→X direction, never draft→finalized. Exactly as the finding states. 3) STATUS GUARD — never fires. `attachStatusGuard` (statusTran…

findings.dimension: Clinical documentation (notes, IA, discharge summary, co-sign)
findings.title: finalize() performs the irreversible status flip BEFORE the primary-consultant and JR-cosign gates, permanently locking the record on a 403
findings.line: 384-410 (CAS write) vs 553-563 (consultant gate) and 585-602 (JR mustCosign gate)
findings.mechanism: The atomic CAS findOneAndUpdate that sets status:'finalized' + finalizedAt runs at line 384. Only afterwards, at line 542, does the controller load the admission and enforce the NOT_PRIMARY_CONSULTANT check (553-563) and the REQUIRE_SENIOR_COSIGN check (585-597). Those checks `return res.status(403/409)` — but the finalize write has already committed and is not rolled back. Once finalized, the record is immutable (model guard at DischargeSummaryModel.js:235-252) and a re-finalize by the correct actor hits the CAS `status: {$ne:'finalized'}`, falls to line 411, and returns 409 ALREADY_FINALIZED.
findings.failureScenario: Dr. B (a Doctor with `discharge-summary.write` but not the attending on admission X) calls PATCH /api/discharge-summary/<id>/finalize. He receives 403 NOT_PRIMARY_CONSULTANT and reasonably assumes nothing happened. In reality the summary is now status:'finalized', finalizedByName:'Dr. B', finalizedAt:now — permanently. Dr. A (the actual attending) then calls finalize and gets 409 ALREADY_FINALIZED. The admission's dischargeWorkflow.stage was never advanced to DoctorApproved (that block is inside the same post-403 section), so the patient never enters the receptionist discharge queue and the bed is never released — a workflow deadlock requiring DB surgery. The signed legal record also names the wrong consultant. Same trap fires on the mustCosign JR path: a JR who omits requireSeniorCosign:false gets 409 but the summary is already finalized without any co-sign.
findings.fixSketch: Move the admission load + primary-consultant gate + JR mustCosign gate to run BEFORE the CAS write at line 384, alongside the other pre-flight gates. The post-write section should then only contain the non-blocking emits and the dischargeWorkflow advance.
findings.verifyEvidence: Read the actual code; the cited mechanism is exact and I could not refute it. ORDER OF OPERATIONS (D:\Spherehealth\Backend\controllers\Clinical\dischargeSummaryController.js): - finalize() starts at line 210. Pre-CAS gates are ONLY: MCI reg-no (218-233), cause-of-death (262-271), PROM/PREM (279-297), nursing handover (299-324), mandatory-content (336-372). NONE of these is an ownership/authorization check. - Line 384-410: the irreversible CAS `DischargeSummary.findOneAndUpdate({_id, status:{$ne:"finalized"}}, {status:"finalized", finalizedByName, finalizedAt:new Date(), ...})` COMMITS here. - Line 542-545: only NOW is the admission loaded. - Line 553-563: `if (req.user?.role === "Doctor")` … `return res.status(403) NOT_PRIMARY_CONSULTANT` — after the write, with no compensating rollback anywhere in the function. - Line 585-597: JR `mustCosign` gate `return res.status(409) REQUIRE_SENIOR_COSIGN` — also post-write. Note line 599-601 (`selfFinalizeAck`) only runs on the SUCCESS branch, so a JR who omits `requireSeniorCosign:false` gets a finalized summary with NO ack and NO cosign — the SoD control at 585 is fully defeated. - Line 635-659: the `dischargeWorkflow.stage:"DoctorApproved"` advance sits below both gates, so it is skipped on the 403/409. ROUTE CONFIRMS NO EARLIER GATE: routes/Clinical/dischargeSummaryRoutes.js:22 — `router.patch("/:id/finalize", validateObjectIdParam("id"), requireAction("discharge-summary.write"), ctrl.finalize)`. Permission-only; any Doctor holding…

findings.dimension: Clinical documentation (notes, IA, discharge summary, co-sign)
findings.title: PATCH /doctor-notes/:id/diagnosis rewrites any signed note's diagnosis with no ownership check, no reason, no amendments[] entry, via a raw uncast collection write
findings.mechanism: updateDiagnosis() has no author/Admin check at all — contrast amendDoctorNote() (1060-1067: `if (!isAuthor && actorRole !== 'Admin') 403 AMEND_NOT_OWNER`) and updateDoctorNote() (761-765). The route only gates on requireAction('doctor-orders.write') (doctorNotesRoutes.js:36), i.e. any Doctor/Admin.
