Every clinical autosave draft is written by useAutoSave.js:44 (`localStorage.setItem(draftKey, payload)`) under the prefix `sphere_draft_*`, which matches NO entry in phiPrefixes — note `sphereai_` is present but `sphere_draft_` is not, so the guard reads as covering these keys while matching none of them. Confirmed patient-scoped draft keys: `sphere_draft_discharge_${patInfo._id}` (DischargeSummaryPage.jsx:1013), `sphere_draft_ipd_initial_${draftRole}_${patient._id}` (IPDInitialAssessmentPage.jsx:1015), `sphere_draft_consent_${patInfo._id}` (ConsentFormPage.jsx:994), `sphere_draft_er_${uhid}` (EmergencyAssessmentPage.jsx:218), `sphere_draft_opd_${visitNumber}` (OPDAssessmentPage.jsx:395), `sphere_draft_docnotes_`, `sphere_draft_nurse_`, `sphere_draft_pain_`, `sphere_draft_fall_`, `sphere_draft_nutrition_`, `sphere_draft_pressure_`, `sphere_draft_daily_`. These payloads are entire clinical form states (chief complaint, diagnosis, discharge summary prose). Two adjacent keys are likewise unswept: `sphere_sig_${uid}` — the user's digital signature PNG used to sign clinical records (useDigitalSignature.js:46,71) — and `pincode:*` (ReceptionConsole.jsx:418). Drafts are only removed on a successful submit via clearDraft(); an abandoned or in-progress form leaves them indefinitely.
findings.failureScenario: On a shared ward terminal, Dr. A opens a discharge summary for patient X, types the diagnosis and summary, and is paged away without submitting. useAutoSave writes the full form to `localStorage["sphere_draft_discharge_<patientX_id>"]` after 2s. Dr. A clicks Logout — the sweep runs but matches none of the `sphere_draft_*` keys, so the draft persists. Ward Boy B (or any next user, or anyone with physical access) logs in on the same browser profile, opens DevTools → Application → Local Storage, and reads patient X's full discharge diagnosis and clinical narrative without ever holding a permission for that record — an authorization-free PHI disclosure that also defeats audit logging (no server request is made). `sphere_sig_<drA_uid>` is likewise readable, exposing Dr. A's signature image to the next user.
findings.fixSketch: Add `"sphere_draft_"`, `"sphere_sig_"`, and `"pincode:"` to phiPrefixes at AuthContext.jsx:264. More robustly, invert the policy: sweep everything except a small allowlist of non-PHI UI prefs (`pf-theme`, `pf-density`, `his-settings-version`), so future draft keys are covered by default rather than by remembering to update a list. Consider having useAutoSave register its keys in a central registry that logout drains, and prefer sessionStorage for drafts.
findings.verifyEvidence: Every load-bearing claim verified against actual code; attempted refutation failed. 1) SWEEP LIST — confirmed verbatim. AuthContext.jsx:264 is exactly `const phiPrefixes = ["nabh_", "his_patient_", "his_admission_", "rc_", "sphereai_"];`, applied at :269 via `phiPrefixes.some((p) => k.startsWith(p))` over Object.keys of both localStorage and sessionStorage, plus a separate `break-glass:` removal at :270. The comment at :254-258 states the security intent ("so the next user on a shared terminal can't open DevTools and harvest the previous user's patient context. Security audit 2026-05-17 finding E-01 / E-02") and :261-263 warns "Keep this list in sync with whatever pages call localStorage.setItem with a patient-scoped key." 2) PREFIX MISS — confirmed. "sphere_draft_" does not startWith any of the five entries. "sphereai_" is a genuine near-miss (`sphere`+`ai_`) that does NOT prefix-match `sphere_draft_`, so the guard reads as covering these keys while matching zero of them. 3) WRITE PATH — confirmed. useAutoSave.js:44 `localStorage.setItem(draftKey, payload)` where payload = JSON.stringify({...formState, _meta}) — the ENTIRE clinical form state, debounced 2000ms (:38-51). clearDraft (:75-80) removes the key only when called explicitly after successful submit, so an abandoned/in-progress form persists indefinitely. 4) NO OTHER SWEEP. Repo-wide grep for `phiPrefixes|localStorage.clear|sessionStorage.clear` across context/hooks/config/utils/components returns ONLY AuthContext.js…

findings.dimension: Frontend — state, API wiring, XSS, secrets, print/render
findings.title: Cross-patient state bleed in nursing assessment pages — the #47-style `key` remount guard was applied only to FallRisk
findings.line: 418 (missing key), 159-179 (merge-only restore); NutritionalAssessmentPage.jsx:457, 147-165; cf. FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:523 (the guard that IS present)
findings.mechanism: FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:523 renders `<FallRiskContent key={patient?._id || "no-patient"} patient={patient} />` — the `key` forces a full remount on patient switch, resetting all form state. That guard is absent on the sibling pages, which render the content component with no key: PainAssessmentPage.jsx:418 `<PainContent patient={patient} />`, NutritionalAssessmentPage.jsx:457 `<NutritionalContent patient={patient} />` (same shape at NursingCarePlanPage.jsx:654, PressureAreaCarePage.jsx:571, DailyNursingAssessmentPage.jsx:628, PatientEducationPage.jsx:495 — none carry a key). In those components the patient-change effect only ever RESTORES; it never RESETS. PainAssessmentPage.jsx:159-179: `if (!patient) return;` then `if (stored) { setHistory(...); setReassessLog(...) }` and `if (raw) { if (df) setForm(f => ({ ...f, ...df })); ... }`. Both writes are conditional on data existing, and the form write is a MERGE (`{...f, ...df}`), not a replace. `form` is initialised once at mount (`useState(defaultForm)`, :149) and never reset. Identical at NutritionalAssessmentPage.jsx:162 (`setForm(f => ({ ...f, ...df }))`, no reset). Consequence: when the user switches patients via ClinicalLayout's `onPatientSelect={setPatient}`, the component does not remount, so patient X's typed values remain in `form`/`history` and are silently carried into patient Y's assessment — completely if Y has no draft, partially (unset keys only) if Y's draft covers a subset. The asymmetry with FallRisk strongly suggests the deferred #47 fall-sentinel remediation hardened only the fall page and left its siblings on the vulnerable pattern.
findings.failureScenario: A nurse selects patient X in Pain Assessment, records severe pain (score 9, site 'lumbar spine', character 'burning', analgesic 'Morphine 5mg'). Without leaving the page she selects patient Y from the ClinicalLayout picker. PainContent does not remount (no key). Patient Y has never had a pain assessment, so `sessionStorage["nabh_pain_<Y>"]` and `localStorage["sphere_draft_pain_<Y>"]` are both absent — every setter at :162-177 is skipped and the form still displays X's data. The nurse glances at the pre-filled form, hits Save, and handleSave POSTs X's pain score 9 / 'Morphine 5mg' against patient Y's record. Y now carries a fabricated severe-pain assessment; worse, useAutoSave immediately persists X's clinical data under `sphere_draft_pain_<Y>`, so the contamination is durable and re-loads on the next visit. Same mechanism yields wrong Braden/nutrition/care-plan entries on the sibling pages.
findings.fixSketch: Apply FallRisk's guard uniformly — add `key={patient?._id || "no-patient"}` to the content component on PainAssessmentPage.jsx:418, NutritionalAssessmentPage.jsx:457, PressureAreaCarePage.jsx:571, DailyNursingAssessmentPage.jsx:628, NursingCarePlanPage.jsx:654, PatientEducationPage.jsx:495. Independently, make the restore effects reset-then-restore (`setForm(df ? { ...defaultForm, ...df } : defaultForm); setHistory(data?.history || []); setReassessLog(data?.reassessLog || [])`) so correctness does not depend solely on the key. Add a regression test that switches patients and asserts the form equals defaults for a patient with no stored draft.
findings.verifyEvidence: All cited locations verified against actual code; every link in the chain holds. 1. ClinicalLayout.jsx:12-35 renders `{children}` unconditionally inside a plain flex <div> — no key, no conditional unmount, no remount trigger. Patient switch flows through `onPatientSelect` -> `setPatient` in the page component, which re-renders but does NOT remount the child content component (same type, same position => React reconciles in place). 2. FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:523 `<FallRiskContent key={patient?._id || "no-patient"} patient={patient} />` — the guard IS present, and its own #47 comment (lines 517-522) states the mechanism verbatim: "ClinicalLayout renders children without a key, so without this FallRiskContent stays mounted across patient switches and stale fall-event state ... would bleed into the next patient, raising a phantom sentinel for someone who never fell." 3. PainAssessmentPage.jsx:418 `<PainContent patient={patient} />` — no key. Grep over Frontend/src/pages/nursing confirms FallRisk is the ONLY page with the key. Missing on NutritionalAssessmentPage.jsx:457, PainAssessmentPage.jsx:418, NursingCarePlanPage.jsx:655, DailyNursingAssessmentPage.jsx:637, PressureAreaCarePage.jsx:572, PatientEducationPage.jsx:496, plus two the finding did not list: NurseInitialAssessmentPage.jsx:1453 and NursingNotes.jsx:4447. 4. Restore-only, merge-not-replace confirmed. PainAssessmentPage.jsx:149 `const [form, setForm] = useState(defaultForm);` initialises once and is never reset. Th…

findings.dimension: Frontend — state, API wiring, XSS, secrets, print/render
findings.title: Digital signature: localStorage cache is never revalidated and backend sync failure is swallowed
findings.mechanism: Two independent issues in the same hook. (1) Read path :40-41 — `const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey); if (cached) { setSignature(cached); return; }` returns early and unconditionally trusts the cache, so the backend GET at :52-62 never runs once a cache entry exists. The entry has no TTL and no version stamp, and is not cleared on logout (`sphere_sig_*` is absent from AuthContext.jsx:264's phiPrefixes), so it effectively lives forever per browser profile. (2) Write path :71-84 — `localStorage.setItem(cacheKey, dataUrl); setSignature(dataUrl);` runs BEFORE the PATCH, and the PATCH failure is caught and downgraded to `console.warn("[Signature] Failed to sync to backend:", e.message)` with no rethrow and no UI signal. The local cache and UI therefore report success even when the server never received the signature — the inverse of the POST-first/commit-on-2xx discipline applied elsewhere (e.g. FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:257 'POST first, only clearDraft + setSaved on 2xx').
findings.failureScenario: Dr. A re-draws her signature on ward terminal 1. The PATCH to /api/auth/signature fails (token expiring, replica blip) — the error is swallowed at :81-83, the UI shows the new signature, and localStorage caches it. On terminal 1 she keeps signing notes with a signature the server has never seen, so server-rendered artifacts and any other device fall back to her OLD signature — the same clinical record is signed with two different images depending on render path, undermining the NABH signed-record trail. Separately, because :40-41 never revalidates, a terminal that cached her old signature keeps stamping the superseded image onto new notes indefinitely, even after a successful update elsewhere.
findings.fixSketch: Make the write path server-authoritative: await the PATCH, and only setItem/setSignature on 2xx; on failure surface a toast and leave the prior signature intact. On the read path, serve the cache optimistically but still fire the backend GET and reconcile (or stamp the cache with the user's signatureUpdatedAt and invalidate on mismatch). Add `sphere_sig_` to the logout sweep so a shared terminal drops it.
findings.verifyEvidence: All three cited mechanisms verified verbatim in Frontend/src/hooks/useDigitalSignature.js. (1) READ PATH :40-41 — `const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey); if (cached) { setSignature(cached); return; }`. The early return makes step 2 (`user.signature`, :44-49) and step 3 (backend GET, :52-62) unreachable once any cache entry exists. STRONGER than claimed: Backend/routes/Auth/authRoutes.js:419 `/me` returns the full user doc (select "-password"), i.e. including `signature`, so AuthContext holds the authoritative server value on every login — and :41 discards it. No TTL/version stamp. An admin CAN change a user's signature out-of-band (Backend/routes/User/userRoutes.js:49 -> userController.adminSetSignature:449, emits USER_SIGNATURE_UPDATED), and a cached terminal never observes it. (2) WRITE PATH :71-84 — `localStorage.setItem(cacheKey, dataUrl); setSignature(dataUrl);` at :71-72 run BEFORE the PATCH at :76-80; the catch at :81-83 is a bare `console.warn("[Signature] Failed to sync to backend:", e.message)` with no rethrow, no UI signal. All 14 callers treat it as success, e.g. FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:505 `onSave={async (dataUrl) => { await saveSignature(dataUrl); setShowSetup(false); }}` closes the modal on failure. The cited contrast with POST-first/commit-on-2xx at FallRiskAssessmentPage.jsx:257 is accurate. (3) LOGOUT SWEEP — AuthContext.jsx:264 `phiPrefixes = ["nabh_", "his_patient_", "his_admission_", "rc_", "sphereai_"]` — `sphere_sig_` genuinely absent, so th…
